   347	  # The trigger `update_reading_list_document` is used to keep the `articles.reading_list_document` column updated.
   348	  #
   349	  # Its body is inserted in a PostgreSQL trigger function and that joins the columns values
   350	  # needed to search documents in the context of a "reading list".
   351	  #
   352	  # Please refer to https://github.com/jenseng/hair_trigger#usage in case you want to change or update the trigger.
   353	  #
   354	  # Additional information on how triggers work can be found in
   355	  # => https://www.postgresql.org/docs/11/trigger-definition.html
   356	  # => https://www.cybertec-postgresql.com/en/postgresql-how-to-write-a-trigger/
   357	  #
   358	  # Adapted from https://dba.stackexchange.com/a/289361/226575
   359	  trigger
   360	    .name(:update_reading_list_document).before(:insert, :update).for_each(:row)
   361	    .declare("l_org_vector tsvector; l_user_vector tsvector") do
   362	    <<~SQL
   363	      NEW.reading_list_document :=
   364	        setweight(to_tsvector('simple'::regconfig, unaccent(coalesce(NEW.title, ''))), 'A') ||
   365	        setweight(to_tsvector('simple'::regconfig, unaccent(coalesce(NEW.cached_tag_list, ''))), 'B') ||
   366	        setweight(to_tsvector('simple'::regconfig, unaccent(coalesce(NEW.body_markdown, ''))), 'C') ||
   367	        setweight(to_tsvector('simple'::regconfig, unaccent(coalesce(NEW.cached_user_name, ''))), 'D') ||
   368	        setweight(to_tsvector('simple'::regconfig, unaccent(coalesce(NEW.cached_user_username, ''))), 'D') ||
   369	        setweight(to_tsvector('simple'::regconfig,
   370	          unaccent(
   371	            coalesce(
   372	              array_to_string(
   373	                -- cached_organization is serialized to the DB as a YAML string, we extract only the name attribute
   374	                regexp_match(NEW.cached_organization, 'name: (.*)$', 'n'),
   375	                ' '
   376	              ),
   377	              ''
   378	            )
   379	          )
   380	        ), 'D');
   381	    SQL
   382	  end

   597	    # Time ago sometimes returns this phrase instead of a date

   599

   602

   604	      .order(organic_page_views_past_month_count: :desc)
   605	      .where("score > ?", 8)

Article’s core contract is heavier than a plain model: it owns serialized cached user/org snapshots, polymorphic comment/notification/mention attachments, feed/search scopes, and many callbacks that enqueue follow-up work. I’m reading the callback implementations and included concerns next, because those are where teardown changes are most likely to leak.

  1429

  1432

  1434	    self.description = hash["description"] if update_description
  1435

  1443	  def set_main_image(hash)

  1447	    elsif hash.key?("cover_image")

  1459	  # When an article is saved, it ensures that the tags that were adjusted by moderators and admins
  1460	  # remain adjusted. We do not allow the author to add or remove tags that were previously added or
  1461	  # removed by moderators and admins.
  1462	  #
  1463	  # This method is called before validation, so that the tag_list can be validated.
  1464	  #
  1465	  # @return [String] an array of tag names.
  1466	  def validate_tag
  1467	    distinct_tag_adjustments = TagAdjustment.where(article_id: id, status: "committed")
  1468	      .select('DISTINCT ON ("tag_id") *')
  1469	      .order(:tag_id, updated_at: :desc, id: :desc)
  1470	
  1471	    remove_tag_adjustments_from_tag_list(distinct_tag_adjustments)
  1472	    add_tag_adjustments_to_tag_list(distinct_tag_adjustments)
  1473	
  1474	    # check there are not too many tags
  1475	    return errors.add(:tag_list, I18n.t("models.article.too_many_tags")) if tag_list.size > MAX_TAG_LIST_SIZE
  1476	
  1477	    validate_tag_name(tag_list)
  1478	  end

  1480	  def remove_tag_adjustments_from_tag_list(distinct_adjustments)
  1481	    tags_to_remove = distinct_adjustments.select { |adj| adj.adjustment_type == "removal" }.pluck(:tag_name)
  1482	    tag_list.remove(tags_to_remove, parse: true) if tags_to_remove.present?
  1483	  end
  1484	
  1485	  def add_tag_adjustments_to_tag_list(distinct_adjustments)

  1487	    return if tags_to_add.blank?
  1488	
  1489	    tag_list.add(tags_to_add, parse: true)
  1490	    self.tag_list = tag_list.map { |tag| Tag.find_preferred_alias_for(tag) }
  1491	  end

  1553	  def future_or_current_published_at
  1554	    # allow published_at in the future or within 15 minutes in the past
  1555	    return if !published || published_at.blank? || published_at > 15.minutes.ago

  1560	  def correct_published_at?
  1561	    return true unless changes["published_at"]
  1562	
  1563	    # for drafts (that were never published before) or scheduled articles
  1564	    # => allow future or current dates, or no published_at
  1565	    if !published_at_was || published_at_was > Time.current
  1566	      # for articles published_from_feed (exported from rss) we allow past published_at
  1567	      if (published_at && published_at < 15.minutes.ago) && !published_from_feed
  1568	        errors.add(:published_at, I18n.t("models.article.future_or_current_published_at"))
  1569	      end
  1570	    else
  1571	      # for articles that have been published already (published or unpublished drafts) => immutable published_at
  1572	      # allow changes within one minute in case of editing via frontmatter w/o specifying seconds
  1573	      has_nils = changes["published_at"].include?(nil) # changes from nil or to nil
  1574	      close_enough = !has_nils && (published_at_was - published_at).between?(-60, 60)
  1575	      errors.add(:published_at, I18n.t("models.article.immutable_published_at")) if has_nils || !close_enough
  1576	    end
  1577	  end

  1648	    self.nth_published_by_author = (index || published_article_ids.size) + 1
  1649	  end

  1651	  def title_to_slug
  1652	    truncated_title = title.size > 100 ? title[0..100].split[0...-1].join(" ") : title
  1653	    "#{Sterile.sluggerize(truncated_title)}-#{rand(100_000).to_s(26)}"
  1654	  end

  1657	    return unless destroying || saved_changes.keys.intersection(%w[title cached_tag_list published archived]).present?
  1658

  1660	    organization&.touch(:latest_article_updated_at)

  1662

  1665	    async_bust
  1666	    touch_actor_latest_article_updated_at(destroying: destroying)

  1715	    return unless title&.match?(BIDI_CONTROL_CHARACTERS)
  1716

  1718	    self.title = bidi_stripped if bidi_stripped.blank? # title only contains BIDI characters = blank title
  1719	  end

  1721	  # Sometimes published_at is set to a date *way way too far in the future*, likely a parsing mistake. Let's nullify.
  1722	  # Do this instead of invlidating the record, because we want to allow the user to fix the date and publish as needed.
  1723	  def remove_invalid_published_at
  1724	    return if published_at.blank?
  1725

  1729	  def recompile_organization_pages
  1730	    # Skip recompiling on updates if only updated_at or semantic_embedding changed
  1731	    if !destroyed? && previous_changes.any? && (previous_changes.keys - %w[updated_at semantic_embedding]).empty?
  1732	      return
  1733	    end
  1734	
  1735	    was_published = destroyed? ? published? : published_before_last_save
  1736	    is_published = published?
  1737	    return unless is_published || was_published

  1760

  1762	      user.id == Settings::General.mascot_user_id ||
  1763	      user.id == Settings::Community.staff_user_id
  1764	  end

  1777	    # Only run this when the title has changed or it's a new record to avoid duplicating embed tags
  1778	    type_of == "status" && title.present? && extract_urls_from_title.any? && (new_record? || title_changed?)
  1779	  end

  1782	    urls = extract_urls_from_title
  1783	    return if urls.empty?
  1784	
  1785	    # Create embed tags for each URL found in the title

The callback chain confirms both inline and async behavior: destroy calls cache busting before associations are deleted, while create/update save paths fan out to spam checks, social images, AI summaries/embeddings/context notes, image enrichment, code detection, org page recompilation, and feed/search-related score recalculation. I’m collecting the concrete worker/service guards now so the audit distinguishes “enqueued” from “actually acts.”

FILE app/models/concerns/reactable.rb
     1	module Reactable
     2	  extend ActiveSupport::Concern
     3	
     4	  included do
     5	    has_many :reactions, as: :reactable, inverse_of: :reactable, dependent: :destroy
     6	    has_many :distinct_reaction_categories, -> { order(:category).merge(Reaction.distinct_categories) },
     7	             as: :reactable,
     8	             inverse_of: :reactable,
     9	             dependent: nil,
    10	             class_name: "Reaction"
    11	  end
    12	
    13	  class_methods do
    14	    # Batch sync reaction counts for multiple records efficiently
    15	    # Uses transaction + row locking to prevent race conditions
    16	    # Eliminates N+1 queries by using a single grouped query + batch update
    17	    #
    18	    # @param records [Array<ActiveRecord::Base>] records to sync
    19	    # @return [Integer] number of records updated
    20	    def sync_reactions_count_for_batch(records)
    21	      return 0 if records.empty?
    22	
    23	      ids = records.map(&:id)
    24	
    25	      transaction do
    26	        # Lock the rows to prevent concurrent updates (SKIP LOCKED for non-blocking)
    27	        where(id: ids).lock("FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED").load
    28	
    29	        # Single grouped query to get correct counts for all records
    30	        correct_counts = Reaction
    31	          .where(reactable_type: name, reactable_id: ids)
    32	          .public_category
    33	          .group(:reactable_id)
    34	          .count
    35	
    36	        # Use Arel for safe SQL generation instead of string interpolation
    37	        update_cases = ids.map do |record_id|
    38	          count = correct_counts[record_id] || 0
    39	          Arel.sql("WHEN #{connection.quote(record_id)} THEN #{count.to_i}")
    40	        end
    41	
    42	        # Perform batch update using ActiveRecord with sanitized Arel
    43	        where(id: ids).update_all(
    44	          Arel.sql(
    45	            "public_reactions_count = CASE id #{update_cases.join(' ')} END",
    46	          ),
    47	        )
    48	      end
    49	
    50	      ids.size
    51	    end
    52	  end
    53	
    54	  def sync_reactions_count
    55	    # Use transaction + row locking to prevent race conditions
    56	    self.class.transaction do
    57	      self.class.where(id: id).lock("FOR UPDATE").load
    58	      correct_count = reactions.public_category.count
    59	      self.class.where(id: id).update_all(public_reactions_count: correct_count)
    60	    end
    61	  end
    62	
    63	  # Thread-safe sync that can be called from background jobs
    64	  def sync_reactions_count!
    65	    sync_reactions_count
    66	    reload
    67	  end
    68	
    69	  def public_reaction_categories
    70	    # Get reaction counts for this reactable
    71	    reaction_counts = reaction_counts_for_reactable
    72	
    73	    # Filter to only public reaction categories that have reactions
    74	    categories_with_counts = reaction_counts.select do |count_data|
    75	      category_slug = count_data[:category]
    76	      count_data[:count].positive? && ReactionCategory[category_slug]&.visible_to_public?
    77	    end
    78	
    79	    # Sort by count (descending), then by position for ties, and limit to 3
    80	    sorted_categories = categories_with_counts
    81	      .sort_by { |count_data| [-count_data[:count], ReactionCategory[count_data[:category]]&.position || 99] }
    82	      .first(3)
    83	
    84	    # Convert back to ReactionCategory objects
    85	    sorted_categories.map do |count_data|
    86	      ReactionCategory[count_data[:category]]
    87	    end
    88	  end
    89	
    90	  private
    91	
    92	  def reaction_counts_for_reactable
    93	    return [] unless respond_to?(:id)
    94	
    95	    Rails.cache.fetch("reaction_counts_for_reactable-#{self.class.name}-#{id}", expires_in: 10.hours) do
    96	      reactions.group(:category).count.map do |category, count|
    97	        { category: category, count: count }
    98	      end
    99	    end
   100	  end
   101	end

     1	module Taggable
     2	  extend ActiveSupport::Concern
     3	
     4	  # rubocop:disable Metrics/BlockLength(RuboCop)
     5	  included do
     6	    scope :cached_tagged_with, lambda { |tag|
     7	      case tag
     8	      when String, Symbol
     9	        if ENV["OPTIMIZED_TAGGABLE_QUERY"] == "true"
    10	          # Accelerate native `WHERE` filtering escaping sequential regex scans by leveraging GIN index bounds organically dynamically. 
    11	          where("tags_array @> ARRAY[?]::text[]", tag.to_s)
    12	        else
    13	          # In Postgres regexes, the [[:<:]] and [[:>:]] are equivalent to "start of
    14	          # word" and "end of word", respectively. They're similar to `\b` in Perl-
    15	          # compatible regexes (PCRE), but that matches at either end of a word.
    16	          # They're more comparable to how vim's `\<` and `\>` work.
    17	          where("cached_tag_list ~ ?", "[[:<:]]#{tag}[[:>:]]")
    18	        end
    19	      when Array
    20	        tag.reduce(self) { |acc, elem| acc.cached_tagged_with(elem) }
    21	      when Tag
    22	        cached_tagged_with(tag.name)
    23	      else
    24	        raise TypeError, "Cannot search tags for: #{tag.inspect}"
    25	      end
    26	    }
    27	
    28	    scope :cached_tagged_with_any, lambda { |tags|
    29	      case tags
    30	      when String, Symbol
    31	        cached_tagged_with(tags)
    32	      when Array
    33	        if ENV["OPTIMIZED_TAGGABLE_QUERY"] == "true"
    34	          # Accelerate `any` conditions natively utilizing absolute overlap intersections eliminating `OR` sequential bottlenecks dynamically!
    35	          where("tags_array && ARRAY[?]::text[]", tags.map(&:to_s))
    36	        else
    37	          tags
    38	            .map { |tag| cached_tagged_with(tag) }
    39	            .reduce { |acc, elem| acc.or(elem) }
    40	        end
    41	      when Tag
    42	        cached_tagged_with(tags.name)
    43	      else
    44	        raise TypeError, "Cannot search tags for: #{tags.inspect}"
    45	      end
    46	    }
    47	
    48	    # We usually try to avoid using Arel directly like this. However, none of the more
    49	    # straight-forward ways of negating the above scope worked:
    50	    # 1. A subquery doesn't work because we're not dealing with a simple NOT IN scenario.
    51	    # 2. where.not(cached_tagged_with_any(tags).where_values_hash) doesn't work because where_values_hash
    52	    #    only works for simple conditions and returns an empty hash in this case.
    53	    scope :not_cached_tagged_with_any, lambda { |tags|
    54	      where(cached_tagged_with_any(tags).arel.constraints.reduce(:or).not)
    55	    }
    56	
    57	    before_save :sync_tags_array
    58	
    59	    def sync_tags_array
    60	      return unless self.class.column_names.include?("tags_array")
    61	      
    62	      # We meticulously avoid N+1 evaluation queries by identifying if ActsAsTaggableOn
    63	      # tags were explicitly interrogated/mutated. 
    64	      # Forem intrinsically instantiates `@tag_list` when validating front matter tags.
    65	      tags_were_mutated = cached_tag_list_changed? ||
    66	                          instance_variable_defined?(:@tag_list) ||
    67	                          (cached_tag_list.present? && tags_array.blank?)
    68	      
    69	      return unless tags_were_mutated
    70	      
    71	      new_array = if instance_variable_defined?(:@tag_list)
    72	                    tag_list.to_a
    73	                  else
    74	                    cached_tag_list.to_s.split(",").map(&:strip).reject(&:blank?).compact
    75	                  end
    76	                  
    77	      self.tags_array = new_array unless tags_array == new_array
    78	    end
    79	  end
    80	  # rubocop:enable Metrics/BlockLength(RuboCop)
    81	
    82	  # we check tags names aren't too long and don't contain non alphabet characters
    83	  def validate_tag_name(tag_list)
    84	    tag_list.each do |tag|
    85	      new_tag = Tag.new(name: tag)
    86	      new_tag.validate_name
    87	      new_tag.errors.messages[:name].each { |message| errors.add(:tag, "\"#{tag}\" #{message}") }
    88	    end
    89	  end
    90	end

    21	                  :public_reaction_categories, :subforem_id
    22	
    23	        add_attribute(:published_at) { published_at.to_i }
    24	        add_attribute(:readable_publish_date) { readable_publish_date }

    24	    # This method helps answer the question: What are the articles

    26	    # provides a date by which to filter out "stale to the user"
    27	    # articles.
    28	    #
    29	    # @note Do we need to continue using this method?  It's part of
    30	    #       the hot story grab experiment that we ran with the
    31	    #       Article::Feeds::LargeForemExperimental, but may not be
    32	    #       relevant.
    33	    #
    34	    # @param user [User]
    35	    # @param days_since_published [Integer] if someone
    36	    #        hasn't viewed any articles, give them things from the
    37	    #        database seeds.
    38	    #
    39	    # @return [ActiveSupport::TimeWithZone]
    40	    #
    41	    # @note the days_since_published is something carried
    42	    #       over from the LargeForemExperimental and may not be
    43	    #       relevant given that we have the :daily_decay.
    44	    #       However, this further limitation based on a user's
    45	    #       second most recent page view helps further winnow down
    46	    #       the result set.
    47	    def self.oldest_published_at_to_consider_for(user:, days_since_published: DEFAULT_DAYS_SINCE_PUBLISHED)
    48	      time_of_second_latest_page_view = user&.page_views&.second_to_last&.created_at
    49	      return days_since_published.days.ago unless time_of_second_latest_page_view
    50	
    51	      # If they have a page view *well in the past*, let's go max 18 days look back.
    52	      time_of_second_latest_page_view = 18.days.ago if time_of_second_latest_page_view < 18.days.ago
    53	      time_of_second_latest_page_view - number_of_hours_to_offset_users_latest_article_views.hours
    54	    end

FILE app/models/article_activity.rb
     1	# Per-article rolled-up analytics cache. One row per Article.
     2	#
     3	# Storage shape (raw counters; response shape is derived at read time so that
     4	# worker append operations can be applied via atomic Postgres jsonb updates
     5	# without re-querying the underlying tables):
     6	#
     7	#   daily_page_views[date] = { "total"=>N, "sum_read_seconds"=>N,
     8	#                              "logged_in_count"=>N }
     9	#   daily_reactions[date]  = { "total"=>N, "like"=>N, "readinglist"=>N,
    10	#                              "unicorn"=>N, "exploding_head"=>N,
    11	#                              "raised_hands"=>N, "fire"=>N,
    12	#                              "reactor_ids"=>[user_id, ...] }
    13	#   daily_comments[date]   = N            (count of scored comments)
    14	#   daily_referrers[date]  = { domain => N }
    15	class ArticleActivity < ApplicationRecord
    16	  belongs_to :article
    17	
    18	  REACTION_CATEGORIES = %w[like readinglist unicorn exploding_head raised_hands fire].freeze
    19	
    20	  # ---------- READ API (response shape used by AnalyticsService) ----------
    21	
    22	  def page_views_by_day
    23	    daily_page_views.each_with_object({}) do |(iso, raw), out|
    24	      out[iso] = derive_page_view_day(raw)
    25	    end
    26	  end
    27	
    28	  def reactions_by_day
    29	    daily_reactions.each_with_object({}) do |(iso, raw), out|
    30	      out[iso] = derive_reaction_day(raw)
    31	    end
    32	  end
    33	
    34	  def comments_by_day
    35	    daily_comments.transform_values(&:to_i)
    36	  end
    37	
    38	  def referrers_by_day
    39	    daily_referrers
    40	  end
    41	
    42	  # All-time totals derived from rolling counters and reactor_ids set.
    43	  def page_view_totals
    44	    sum_read = daily_page_views.values.sum { |v| v["sum_read_seconds"].to_i }
    45	    logged = daily_page_views.values.sum { |v| v["logged_in_count"].to_i }
    46	    avg = logged.positive? ? (sum_read.to_f / logged).round : 0
    47	    {
    48	      total: total_page_views,
    49	      average_read_time_in_seconds: avg,
    50	      total_read_time_in_seconds: total_page_views * avg
    51	    }
    52	  end
    53	
    54	  def reaction_totals
    55	    base = { total: total_reactions }
    56	    REACTION_CATEGORIES.each { |c| base[c.to_sym] = 0 }
    57	    reactor_set = Set.new
    58	    daily_reactions.each_value do |raw|
    59	      REACTION_CATEGORIES.each { |c| base[c.to_sym] += raw[c].to_i }
    60	      Array(raw["reactor_ids"]).each { |uid| reactor_set << uid }
    61	    end
    62	    base[:unique_reactors] = reactor_set.size
    63	    base
    64	  end
    65	
    66	  def referrer_totals(top: 20)
    67	    counts = Hash.new(0)
    68	    daily_referrers.each_value do |day_hash|
    69	      day_hash.each { |domain, n| counts[domain] += n.to_i }
    70	    end
    71	    domains = counts.sort_by { |_, n| -n }.first(top).map { |d, n| { domain: d, count: n } }
    72	    { domains: domains }
    73	  end
    74	
    75	  # ---------- WRITE API (atomic incremental appends) ----------
    76	  #
    77	  # All write methods accept a payload hash supplied inline by the worker so
    78	  # that destroy/update operations work without depending on the source row.
    79	
    80	  # payload: { iso, total, sum_read_seconds, logged_in_count, domain }
    81	  #
    82	  # All sub-statements run inside a single DB transaction so that a mid-write
    83	  # error can't leave per-day counters, totals, and referrers out of sync
    84	  # (the worker uses retry: false, so partial application would never be
    85	  # repaired by a retry — only by a full recompute).
    86	  def apply_page_view_delta!(payload)
    87	    iso = payload["iso"]
    88	    delta = {
    89	      "total" => payload["total"].to_i,
    90	      "sum_read_seconds" => payload["sum_read_seconds"].to_i,
    91	      "logged_in_count" => payload["logged_in_count"].to_i
    92	    }
    93	    self.class.transaction do
    94	      merge_day_counters!(:daily_page_views, iso, delta)
    95	      bump_total!(:total_page_views, delta["total"])
    96	      if payload["domain"].present? && delta["total"].positive?
    97	        append_referrer!(iso, payload["domain"], delta["total"])
    98	      end
    99	    end
   100	    reload
   101	  end
   102	
   103	  # payload: { iso, category, user_id }, sign: +1 (create) or -1 (destroy)
   104	  def apply_reaction_delta!(payload, sign:)
   105	    iso = payload["iso"]
   106	    cat = payload["category"]
   107	    delta = { "total" => sign }
   108	    delta[cat] = sign if REACTION_CATEGORIES.include?(cat)
   109	    self.class.transaction do
   110	      merge_day_counters!(:daily_reactions, iso, delta)
   111	      if sign.positive? && payload["user_id"]
   112	        append_reactor_id!(iso, payload["user_id"].to_i)
   113	      elsif sign.negative? && payload["user_id"]
   114	        remove_reactor_id!(iso, payload["user_id"].to_i)
   115	      end
   116	      bump_total!(:total_reactions, sign)
   117	    end
   118	    reload
   119	  end
   120	
   121	  # payload: { iso }, sign: +1 (score crossed into positive) or -1 (score dropped / destroy)
   122	  # Callers gate on score positivity; the worker just applies the supplied delta.
   123	  def apply_comment_delta!(payload, sign:)
   124	    iso = payload["iso"]
   125	    return if iso.blank?
   126	
   127	    self.class.transaction do
   128	      bump_day_int!(:daily_comments, iso, sign)
   129	      bump_total!(:total_comments, sign)
   130	    end
   131	    reload
   132	  end
   133	
   134	  # ---------- BACKFILL ----------
   135	
   136	  # Rebuilds every JSONB column from raw rows. Used the first time we see an
   137	  # article (lazy upsert) and as a manual repair tool.
   138	  def recompute_all!
   139	    pv = build_page_views_from_raw
   140	    rx = build_reactions_from_raw
   141	    cm = build_comments_from_raw
   142	    rf = build_referrers_from_raw
   143	
   144	    update!(
   145	      daily_page_views: pv,
   146	      daily_reactions: rx,
   147	      daily_comments: cm,
   148	      daily_referrers: rf,
   149	      total_page_views: pv.values.sum { |v| v["total"].to_i },
   150	      total_reactions: rx.values.sum { |v| v["total"].to_i },
   151	      total_comments: cm.values.sum(&:to_i),
   152	      last_aggregated_at: Time.current,
   153	    )
   154	  end
   155	
   156	  def derive_page_view_day(raw)
   157	    total = raw["total"].to_i
   158	    logged = raw["logged_in_count"].to_i
   159	    sum_read = raw["sum_read_seconds"].to_i
   160	    avg = logged.positive? ? (sum_read.to_f / logged).round : 0
   161	    {
   162	      "total" => total,
   163	      "average_read_time_in_seconds" => avg,
   164	      "total_read_time_in_seconds" => total * avg
   165	    }
   166	  end
   167	
   168	  def derive_reaction_day(raw)
   169	    out = REACTION_CATEGORIES.each_with_object({ "total" => raw["total"].to_i }) { |c, h| h[c] = raw[c].to_i }
   170	    out["unique_reactors"] = Array(raw["reactor_ids"]).uniq.length
   171	    out
   172	  end
   173	
   174	  private
   175	
   176	  # Atomically merge a hash of counter deltas into daily_<column>[iso].
   177	  # Uses a single UPDATE that constructs the full delta object via
   178	  # jsonb_build_object and merges it in with `||`.
   179	  def merge_day_counters!(column, iso, delta_hash)
   180	    quoted_iso = quote(iso)
   181	    # Quote both the JSON key (for jsonb_build_object) and the lookup key
   182	    # via connection.quote rather than string-interpolating raw input — keeps
   183	    # this safe even if a payload key ever contains a quote character.
   184	    pairs = delta_hash.flat_map do |key, n|
   185	      quoted_key = quote(key.to_s)
   186	      [
   187	        quoted_key,
   188	        "to_jsonb(COALESCE((((#{column}) -> #{quoted_iso}) ->> #{quoted_key})::int, 0) + #{n.to_i})"
   189	      ]
   190	    end
   191	    sql = <<~SQL
   192	      UPDATE article_activities
   193	      SET #{column} = jsonb_set(
   194	        #{column},
   195	        ARRAY[#{quoted_iso}],
   196	        COALESCE((#{column}) -> #{quoted_iso}, '{}'::jsonb) ||
   197	          jsonb_build_object(#{pairs.join(', ')}),
   198	        true
   199	      ),
   200	      updated_at = NOW()
   201	      WHERE id = #{id.to_i}
   202	    SQL
   203	    self.class.connection.exec_update(sql)
   204	  end
   205	
   206	  # daily_<column>[iso] is a bare integer; bump it by `delta`.
   207	  def bump_day_int!(column, iso, delta)
   208	    quoted_iso = quote(iso)
   209	    sql = <<~SQL
   210	      UPDATE article_activities
   211	      SET #{column} = jsonb_set(
   212	        #{column},
   213	        ARRAY[#{quoted_iso}],
   214	        to_jsonb(COALESCE(((#{column}) ->> #{quoted_iso})::int, 0) + #{delta.to_i}),
   215	        true
   216	      ),
   217	      updated_at = NOW()
   218	      WHERE id = #{id.to_i}
   219	    SQL
   220	    self.class.connection.exec_update(sql)
   221	  end
   222	
   223	  def append_reactor_id!(iso, user_id)
   224	    quoted_iso = quote(iso)
   225	    # Read prior list from the row, append, write back atomically using `||`
   226	    # merge of a fresh day-object containing the new array.
   227	    sql = <<~SQL
   228	      UPDATE article_activities
   229	      SET daily_reactions = jsonb_set(
   230	        daily_reactions,
   231	        ARRAY[#{quoted_iso}],
   232	        COALESCE(daily_reactions -> #{quoted_iso}, '{}'::jsonb) ||
   233	          jsonb_build_object(
   234	            'reactor_ids',
   235	            COALESCE(daily_reactions -> #{quoted_iso} -> 'reactor_ids', '[]'::jsonb)
   236	              || to_jsonb(#{user_id.to_i})
   237	          ),
   238	        true
   239	      ),
   240	      updated_at = NOW()

    13	  end
    14	
    15	  # exclude_article_ids is an integer array, web inputs are comma-separated strings
    16	  # ActiveRecord normalizes these in a bad way, so we are intervening
    17	  def article_ids=(input)
    18	    adjusted_input = input.is_a?(String) ? input.split(",") : input
    19	    adjusted_input = adjusted_input&.filter_map { |value| value.presence&.to_i }
    20	    write_attribute :article_ids, (adjusted_input || [])
    21	  end
    22	end

FILE app/workers/articles/backfill_activities_worker.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  # Backfills ArticleActivity rows for a batch of article_ids in one job
     3	  # rather than enqueueing one full-recompute job per article. Used by
     4	  # AnalyticsService when it discovers in-scope articles that don't yet have
     5	  # a cache row, to keep dashboard requests from spiking the queue with
     6	  # potentially thousands of jobs at once.
     7	  class BackfillActivitiesWorker
     8	    include Sidekiq::Job
     9	
    10	    # Coalesce repeated requests for the same owner/scope while one is
    11	    # already pending; the recompute is idempotent so collapsing dupes is safe.
    12	    sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority,
    13	                    retry: 3,
    14	                    lock: :until_executing,
    15	                    on_conflict: :replace
    16	
    17	    def perform(article_ids)
    18	      Array(article_ids).each_slice(100) do |chunk|
    19	        existing = ArticleActivity.where(article_id: chunk).pluck(:article_id).to_set
    20	        missing = chunk.reject { |id| existing.include?(id) }
    21	        next if missing.empty?
    22	
    23	        Article.where(id: missing).find_each do |article|
    24	          activity = ArticleActivity.find_or_create_by!(article_id: article.id)
    25	          activity.recompute_all!
    26	        end
    27	      end
    28	    end
    29	  end
    30	end

FILE app/workers/articles/handle_spam_worker.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  class HandleSpamWorker
     3	    include Sidekiq::Job
     4	
     5	    sidekiq_options queue: :high_priority
     6	
     7	    def perform(article_id)
     8	      article = Article.find_by(id: article_id)
     9	      return unless article
    10	
    11	      # Always run spam handling and score update
    12	      Spam::Handler.handle_article!(article: article)
    13	      article.reload.update_score
    14	
    15	      # Only enhance if clickbait score is 0 (not already processed) and score is >= 0 (good quality after spam check)
    16	      return unless article.clickbait_score.zero? && article.score >= 0
    17	
    18	      enhance_article(article)
    19	    end
    20	
    21	    private
    22	
    23	    def enhance_article(article)
    24	      # Create AI client for dependency injection
    25	      ai_client = Ai::Base.new
    26	      enhancer = Ai::ArticleEnhancer.new(article, ai_client: ai_client)
    27	
    28	      # Calculate and update clickbait score
    29	      clickbait_score = enhancer.calculate_clickbait_score
    30	      article.update_column(:clickbait_score, clickbait_score)
    31	
    32	      # Generate and apply tags if article has no tags and meets criteria
    33	      if should_generate_tags?(article, clickbait_score)
    34	        suggested_tags = enhancer.generate_tags
    35	        apply_tags_to_article(article, suggested_tags) if suggested_tags.any?
    36	      end
    37	    rescue StandardError => e
    38	      Rails.logger.error("Article enhancement failed for article #{article.id}: #{e}")
    39	    end
    40	
    41	    def should_generate_tags?(article, clickbait_score)
    42	      article.cached_tag_list.blank? &&
    43	        article.score >= 0 &&
    44	        clickbait_score < 0.6
    45	    end
    46	
    47	    def apply_tags_to_article(article, tag_names)
    48	      # Validate that all suggested tags exist and are supported
    49	      valid_tags = Tag.supported.where(name: tag_names).pluck(:name)
    50	
    51	      if valid_tags.any?
    52	        # Apply tags to article
    53	        article.tag_list = valid_tags
    54	        article.cached_tag_list = valid_tags.join(", ")
    55	        article.save
    56	
    57	        Rails.logger.info("Applied tags #{valid_tags.join(', ')} to article #{article.id}")
    58	      else
    59	        Rails.logger.warn("No valid tags found from suggestions #{tag_names.join(', ')} for article #{article.id}")
    60	      end
    61	    end
    62	  end
    63	end

FILE app/workers/articles/label_cleanup_worker.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  class LabelCleanupWorker
     3	    include Sidekiq::Job
     4	
     5	    sidekiq_options queue: :high_priority
     6	
     7	    # Maximum number of articles to process per run
     8	    MAX_ARTICLES_PER_RUN = 150
     9	
    10	    def perform
    11	      # Find and select articles published in the last 48 hours but not in the last 10 minutes
    12	      # that have the automod_label of "no_moderation_label"
    13	      selected_articles = find_eligible_articles
    14	
    15	      if selected_articles.any?
    16	        Rails.logger.info("LabelCleanupWorker: Processing #{selected_articles.size} articles with no_moderation_label")
    17	
    18	        # Process each selected article
    19	        selected_articles.each do |article|
    20	          Articles::HandleSpamWorker.perform_async(article.id)
    21	        end
    22	
    23	        Rails.logger.info("LabelCleanupWorker: Enqueued #{selected_articles.size} HandleSpamWorker jobs")
    24	      else
    25	        Rails.logger.info("LabelCleanupWorker: No eligible articles found for processing")
    26	      end
    27	    end
    28	
    29	    private
    30	
    31	    def find_eligible_articles
    32	      # Articles published in the last 48 hours but not in the last 10 minutes
    33	      # that have automod_label of "no_moderation_label"
    34	      # Using >= for 48.hours.ago (inclusive) and < for 10.minutes.ago (exclusive)
    35	      forty_eight_hours_ago = 48.hours.ago
    36	      ten_minutes_ago = 10.minutes.ago
    37	      
    38	      # Add a small buffer to handle precision issues
    39	      forty_eight_hours_ago = forty_eight_hours_ago - 1.second
    40	      
    41	      Article.published
    42	             .where(automod_label: "no_moderation_label")
    43	             .where("score > -80")
    44	             .where("published_at >= ? AND published_at < ?", forty_eight_hours_ago, ten_minutes_ago)
    45	             .order(published_at: :asc)
    46	             .limit(MAX_ARTICLES_PER_RUN)
    47	    end
    48	  end
    49	end

FILE app/workers/articles/quality_reaction_worker.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  class QualityReactionWorker
     3	    include Sidekiq::Job
     4	
     5	    sidekiq_options queue: :medium_priority, retry: 10, lock: :until_and_while_executing
     6	
     7	    def perform
     8	      return unless mascot_user
     9	
    10	      # Process each discoverable subforem
    11	      Subforem.cached_discoverable_ids.each do |subforem_id|
    12	        process_subforem_articles(subforem_id)
    13	      end
    14	    end
    15	
    16	    private
    17	
    18	    def process_subforem_articles(subforem_id)
    19	      # Get articles from the past day with score >= 0 and no existing mascot reactions
    20	      eligible_articles = Article.published
    21	        .where(subforem_id: subforem_id)
    22	        .where("published_at > ?", 1.day.ago)
    23	        .where("score >= 0")
    24	        .where(type_of: "full_post")
    25	        .where.not(id: Reaction.where(user: mascot_user, category: %w[thumbsup thumbsdown]).select(:reactable_id))
    26	        .order(score: :desc)
    27	        .where(max_score: 0)
    28	        .limit(12)
    29	        .includes(:user, :comments)
    30	
    31	      return if eligible_articles.count < 5
    32	
    33	      # Assess quality and get best/worst articles using AI with subforem context
    34	      assessment = Ai::ArticleQualityAssessor.new(eligible_articles, subforem_id: subforem_id).assess
    35	
    36	      return if assessment[:best].nil?
    37	
    38	      # Issue thumbs up to the best article
    39	      issue_thumbs_up(assessment[:best])
    40	
    41	      # Apply max_score of 15 to the worst article if we have at least 12 articles
    42	      if eligible_articles.count >= 12 && assessment[:worst]
    43	        apply_max_score(assessment[:worst])
    44	        Rails.logger.info(
    45	          "QualityReactionWorker: Subforem #{subforem_id} - Issued thumbs up to article #{assessment[:best].id} " \
    46	          "and applied max_score to article #{assessment[:worst].id}",
    47	        )
    48	      else
    49	        Rails.logger.info(
    50	          "QualityReactionWorker: Subforem #{subforem_id} - Issued thumbs up to article #{assessment[:best].id} " \
    51	          "(only #{eligible_articles.count} eligible articles, skipping max_score)",
    52	        )
    53	      end
    54	    end
    55	
    56	    def mascot_user
    57	      @mascot_user ||= User.mascot_account
    58	    end
    59	
    60	    def issue_thumbs_up(article)
    61	      # Remove any existing thumbs down from mascot
    62	      Reaction.where(
    63	        user: mascot_user,
    64	        reactable: article,
    65	        category: "thumbsdown",
    66	      ).destroy_all
    67	
    68	      # Create thumbs up reaction
    69	      Reaction.create!(
    70	        user: mascot_user,
    71	        reactable: article,
    72	        category: "thumbsup",
    73	        status: "confirmed",
    74	      )
    75	    end
    76	
    77	    def apply_max_score(article)
    78	      # Apply max_score of 15 to the article
    79	      article.update(max_score: 15)
    80	    end
    81	  end
    82	end

FILE app/workers/articles/update_article_activity_worker.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  # Maintains the per-article ArticleActivity cache row by applying a single
     3	  # event delta (page view / reaction / comment) instead of re-querying the
     4	  # whole day. If the article has no activity row yet, one is created and a
     5	  # full backfill from raw rows runs — this is the "upsert" referenced in the
     6	  # design. Subsequent events then take the fast append path.
     7	  #
     8	  # Args:
     9	  #   article_id : Integer
    10	  #   event_type : "page_view" | "reaction" | "comment" | nil (nil = full recompute)
    11	  #   action     : "create" | "destroy"
    12	  #   payload    : Hash of attributes carried inline so destroys work even
    13	  #                after the source row is gone. Keys vary by event_type:
    14	  #     page_view: { iso, total, sum_read_seconds, logged_in_count, domain }
    15	  #     reaction:  { iso, category, user_id }
    16	  #     comment:   { iso, score }
    17	  class UpdateArticleActivityWorker
    18	    include Sidekiq::Job
    19	
    20	    # Deltas are non-idempotent — applying the same job twice would
    21	    # double-count (or, on destroy, drive totals negative). retry: false
    22	    # accepts that a transient failure loses one event; the next full
    23	    # recompute (lazy upsert path or manual repair) reconciles drift.
    24	    # We use sidekiq-unique-jobs to ensure at most one job is enqueued/scheduled.
    25	    sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority,
    26	                    retry: false,
    27	                    lock: :until_executing,
    28	                    on_conflict: :replace
    29	
    30	    class << self
    31	      def perform_async(article_id, _event_type = nil, _action = "create", _payload = {})
    32	        return if article_id.nil?
    33	
    34	        # Fetch last_aggregated_at, page_views_count, and created_at in a single fast query
    35	        article_data = Article.left_outer_joins(:article_activity)
    36	                              .where(id: article_id)
    37	                              .pluck(:created_at, :page_views_count, "article_activities.last_aggregated_at")
    38	                              .first
    39	        return if article_data.nil?
    40	
    41	        created_at, page_views_count, last_aggregated_at = article_data
    42	
    43	        delay = debounce_delay_for(page_views_count.to_i, created_at)
    44	
    45	        if last_aggregated_at.nil?
    46	          # If never aggregated, run immediately
    47	          super(article_id)
    48	        else
    49	          elapsed = Time.current - last_aggregated_at
    50	          if elapsed >= delay
    51	            # If it has been longer than the delay, run immediately
    52	            super(article_id)
    53	          else
    54	            # Schedule it for the remaining delay time
    55	            # Since lock: :until_executing, on_conflict: :replace is active,
    56	            # this will replace/coalesce any already scheduled job for this article.
    57	            perform_in(delay - elapsed, article_id)
    58	          end
    59	        end
    60	      end
    61	
    62	      def debounce_delay_for(page_views, created_at)
    63	        base_delay = if page_views >= 100_000
    64	                       5.minutes
    65	                     elsif page_views >= 10_000
    66	                       1.minute
    67	                     elsif page_views >= 1_000
    68	                       30.seconds
    69	                     else
    70	                       10.seconds
    71	                     end
    72	
    73	        return base_delay if created_at.nil?
    74	
    75	        # Exponential decay/backoff based on age (up to 30 days to avoid float overflow)
    76	        days_old = [[(Time.current - created_at) / 1.day, 0].max, 30].min
    77	        calculated_delay = base_delay * (1.5**days_old)
    78	
    79	        [calculated_delay, 30.minutes].min
    80	      end
    81	    end
    82	
    83	    def perform(article_id, _event_type = nil, _action = "create", _payload = {})
    84	      return if article_id.nil?
    85	
    86	      activity = ArticleActivity.find_by(article_id: article_id)
    87	
    88	      if activity.nil?
    89	        return unless Article.exists?(id: article_id)
    90	
    91	        activity = ArticleActivity.find_or_create_by!(article_id: article_id)
    92	        activity.recompute_all! if activity.previously_new_record?
    93	        return
    94	      end
    95	
    96	      # Always recompute on execution so legacy queued jobs with delta-style
    97	      # args converge with newer article_id-only jobs. This avoids drift when
    98	      # an older non-idempotent delta job runs after a recompute/coalesced job.
    99	      activity.recompute_all!
   100	    end
   101	  end
   102	end

FILE app/workers/articles/update_dependent_embeds_worker.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  class UpdateDependentEmbedsWorker
     3	    include Sidekiq::Job
     4	    sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority, lock: :until_executing, on_conflict: :replace
     5	
     6	    def perform(article_id)
     7	      article = Article.find_by(id: article_id)
     8	      
     9	      references = LiquidEmbedReference.where(referenced_type: ["Article", nil], referenced_id: article_id)
    10	      
    11	      if article&.organization_id.present?
    12	        references = references.or(LiquidEmbedReference.where(referenced_type: "Organization", referenced_id: article.organization_id, tag_name: "org_posts"))
    13	      end
    14	
    15	      references.find_each do |reference|
    16	        record = reference.record
    17	        next unless record
    18	
    19	        original_html = record.processed_html
    20	
    21	        if record.is_a?(Article)
    22	          record.evaluate_and_update_column_from_markdown
    23	          if original_html != record.processed_html
    24	            record.async_bust
    25	          end
    26	        elsif record.respond_to?(:evaluate_markdown, true) && record.respond_to?(:processed_html=)
    27	          record.send(:evaluate_markdown)
    28	          if original_html != record.processed_html
    29	            record.update_column(:processed_html, record.processed_html)
    30	            
    31	            if record.respond_to?(:async_bust)
    32	              record.async_bust
    33	            elsif record.respond_to?(:bust_cache)
    34	              record.send(:bust_cache)
    35	            end
    36	          end
    37	        end
    38	      end
    39	    end
    40	  end

FILE app/workers/articles/update_page_views_worker.rb
     1	# [PROJECT_ROOT]/app/workers/articles/update_page_views_worker.rb
     2	
     3	module Articles
     4	  class UpdatePageViewsWorker
     5	    include Sidekiq::Job
     6	
     7	    GOOGLE_REFERRER = "https://www.google.com/".freeze
     8	
     9	    # Authoritative bot filter. The client gate in baseTracking.js can be
    10	    # bypassed by any non-browser client that POSTs to /page_views directly,
    11	    # so self-identified crawlers must also be dropped here before a row is
    12	    # written. Kept in parity with the client-side list.
    13	    BOT_USER_AGENT_REGEX = /
    14	  \b(bot|gptbot|crawl|spider)\b|
    15	  google|baidu|bing|msn|duckduckbot|teoma|slurp|
    16	  yandex|chatgpt|anthropic|cohere-ai|facebookexternalhit
    17	/ix
    18	
    19	    sidekiq_options queue: :medium_priority,
    20	                    lock: :until_executing,
    21	                    on_conflict: :replace,
    22	                    retry: false
    23	
    24	    def perform(create_params)
    25	      create_params = create_params.with_indifferent_access
    26	
    27	      return if bot_user_agent?(create_params[:user_agent])
    28	
    29	      has_viewable_id = create_params[:viewable_id].present?
    30	      has_viewable_type = create_params[:viewable_type].present?
    31	      create_params = create_params.except(:viewable_id, :viewable_type) if has_viewable_id != has_viewable_type
    32	      
    33	      return unless create_params[:article_id].present? ||
    34	                    (create_params[:viewable_id].present? && create_params[:viewable_type].present?)
    35	
    36	      if create_params[:article_id].present?
    37	        article = Article.find_by(id: create_params[:article_id])
    38	        return unless article&.published?
    39	        return if create_params[:user_id] && article.user_id == create_params[:user_id]
    40	      end
    41	
    42	      begin
    43	        PageView.create!(create_params)
    44	      rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
    45	        # This is the most likely error. It happens when this job runs twice
    46	        # due to a race condition. It's safe to ignore it, because the page
    47	        # view has already been created. We can simply stop the worker.
    48	        return
    49	      rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
    50	        # This indicates a data problem. We should log it to understand why
    51	        # the params are bad, and then stop the worker.
    52	        Rails.logger.error("Articles::UpdatePageViewsWorker validation failed: #{e.message} for params: #{create_params}")
    53	        return
    54	      end
    55	
    56	      if article
    57	        updated_count = article.page_views.sum(:counts_for_number_of_views)
    58	        if updated_count > article.page_views_count
    59	          article.update_column(:page_views_count, updated_count)
    60	        end
    61	
    62	        return unless create_params[:referrer] == GOOGLE_REFERRER
    63	
    64	        Articles::UpdateOrganicPageViewsWorker.perform_at(
    65	          25.minutes.from_now,
    66	          article.id,
    67	        )
    68	      end
    69	    end
    70	
    71	    private
    72	
    73	    def bot_user_agent?(user_agent)
    74	      user_agent.present? && BOT_USER_AGENT_REGEX.match?(user_agent)
    75	    end
    76	  end
    77	end

     5	  def perform(article_id)
     6	    article = Article.find_by(id: article_id)
     7	    return unless article && article.published? && article.respond_to?(:semantic_embedding)
     8	
     9	    # Lightweight representation: Title, Tags, and first 1000 characters of the body
    10	    text_to_embed = <<~TEXT
    11	      Title: #{article.title}
    12	      Tags: #{article.cached_tag_list}
    13	      Summary: #{article.body_markdown.truncate(1000)}

    16	    client = Ai::Embedding.new(affected_content: article, wrapper: self)

    18

    21	      UpdateUserInterestEmbeddingWorker.perform_async(article.user_id, article.id)
    22	      Concepts::ClassifyRecordWorker.perform_async("Article", article.id)

     1	module Articles
     2	  class Attributes
     3	    ATTRIBUTES = %i[archived body_markdown canonical_url description compellingness_score labels
     4	                    edited_at main_image organization_id user_id published clickbait_score
     5	                    title video_thumbnail_url published_at co_author_ids_list video_source_url].freeze
     6	
     7	    attr_reader :attributes, :article_user, :organization
     8	
     9	    def initialize(attributes, article_user, organization: nil)
    10	      @attributes = attributes
    11	      @article_user = article_user
    12	      @organization = organization
    13	    end
    14	
    15	    def for_update(update_edited_at: false)
    16	      hash = attributes.slice(*ATTRIBUTES)
    17	      # don't reset the collection when no series was passed
    18	      hash[:collection] = collection if attributes.key?(:series)
    19	      hash[:tag_list] = tag_list if !attributes[:tag_list].nil? || !attributes[:tags].nil?
    20	      hash[:edited_at] = Time.current if update_edited_at
    21	      hash[:published_at] = hash[:published_at].to_datetime if hash[:published_at]
    22	      hash
    23	    end
    24	
    25	    private
    26	
    27	    def collection
    28	      return unless attributes[:series].present?
    29	
    30	      # Use organization from attributes if being changed, otherwise use the passed organization
    31	      org = if attributes[:organization_id].present?
    32	              Organization.find_by(id: attributes[:organization_id])
    33	            else
    34	              organization
    35	            end
    36	      Collection.find_series(attributes[:series], article_user, organization: org)
    37	    end
    38	
    39	    def tag_list
    40	      if attributes[:tag_list]
    41	        attributes[:tag_list]
    42	      elsif attributes[:tags]
    43	        attributes[:tags].join(", ")

    45	    end

     9	    def initialize(user, tag, prefill)

    22	      return [prefill_user_editor_v2, true] if prefill.present? && editor_version2
    23	      return [prefill_user, true] if prefill.present? && user

FILE app/services/articles/creator.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  class Creator
     3	    def self.call(...)
     4	      new(...).call
     5	    end
     6	
     7	    # @param user [User]
     8	    # @param article_params [Hash]
     9	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String] :title
    10	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String] :body_markdown
    11	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String] :main_image
    12	    # @option article_params [Boolean] :published
    13	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String] :description
    14	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String] :video_thumbnail_url
    15	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String] :canonical_url
    16	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String] :series series slug
    17	    # @option article_params [Integer, NilClass] :collection_id
    18	    # @option article_params [Boolean] :archived
    19	    # @option article_params [String<Array>] :tags
    20	    # @option article_params [NilClass, String, ActiveSupport::TimeWithZone] :published_at
    21	    def initialize(user, article_params)
    22	      @user = user
    23	      @article_params = normalize_params(article_params)
    24	    end
    25	
    26	    def call
    27	      rate_limit!
    28	
    29	      create_article.tap do
    30	        subscribe_author if article.persisted?
    31	        refresh_auto_audience_segments if article.published?
    32	        complete_onboarding_first_post if article.published?
    33	      end
    34	    end
    35	
    36	    private
    37	
    38	    attr_reader :article, :user, :article_params
    39	
    40	    def normalize_params(original_params)
    41	      original_params.except(:tags).tap do |params|
    42	        # convert tags from array to a string
    43	        if (tags = original_params[:tags]).present?
    44	          params[:tag_list] = tags.join(", ")
    45	        end
    46	      end
    47	    end
    48	
    49	    def rate_limit!
    50	      rate_limit_to_use = if user.decorate.considered_new?
    51	                            :published_article_antispam_creation
    52	                          else
    53	                            :published_article_creation
    54	                          end
    55	
    56	      user.rate_limiter.check_limit!(rate_limit_to_use)
    57	    end
    58	
    59	    def refresh_auto_audience_segments
    60	      user.refresh_auto_audience_segments
    61	    end
    62	
    63	    def complete_onboarding_first_post
    64	      return if user.registered_at&.before?(28.days.ago)
    65	
    66	      user.onboarding_checklist&.complete_item!("made_first_post")
    67	    end
    68	
    69	    def create_article
    70	      begin
    71	        @article = Article.new(article_params) do |article|
    72	          article.user_id = user.id
    73	          article.show_comments = true
    74	        end
    75	      rescue ArgumentError => e
    76	        if e.message.include?("is not a valid type_of")
    77	          @article = Article.new(article_params.except(:type_of)) do |article|
    78	            article.user_id = user.id
    79	          end
    80	          @article.errors.add(:type_of, :invalid)
    81	          return @article
    82	        else
    83	          raise e
    84	        end
    85	      end
    86	
    87	      # Set collection after creation to avoid it being cleared by evaluate_front_matter
    88	      # which clears collection_id when title is present in frontmatter
    89	      if @article.save
    90	        found_series = series
    91	        if found_series.present?
    92	          @article.update_column(:collection_id, found_series.id)
    93	          @article.association(:collection).reset
    94	        end
    95	      end
    96	      @article
    97	    end
    98	
    99	    def series
   100	      @series ||= if article_params[:series].blank?
   101	                    nil
   102	                  else
   103	                    organization = article_params[:organization_id].present? ? Organization.find_by(id: article_params[:organization_id]) : nil
   104	                    Collection.find_series(article_params[:series], user, organization: organization)
   105	                  end
   106	    end
   107	
   108	    # Subscribe author to notifications for all comments on their article.
   109	    def subscribe_author
   110	      NotificationSubscription.create(user: user,
   111	                                      notifiable: article,
   112	                                      config: "all_comments")
   113	    end
   114	  end
   115	end

FILE app/services/articles/destroyer.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  module Destroyer
     3	    module_function
     4	
     5	    def call(article)
     6	      author = article.user
     7	
     8	      # comments will automatically lose the connection to their article once `.destroy` is called,
     9	      # due to the `dependent: nullify` clause, so to remove their notifications,
    10	      # we need to cache the ids in advance
    11	      article_comments_ids = article.comments.ids
    12	
    13	      article.destroy!
    14	
    15	      Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.id, notifiable_type: "Article")
    16	
    17	      unless article_comments_ids.blank?
    18	        Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article_comments_ids, notifiable_type: "Comment")
    19	      end
    20	
    21	      EdgeCache::BustUser.call(author)
    22	    end
    23	  end
    24	end

FILE app/services/articles/detect_code_block_languages.rb
     1	require "json"
     2	
     3	module Articles
     4	  class DetectCodeBlockLanguages
     5	    VERSION = "1.0"
     6	    FENCED_CODE_BLOCK_REGEX = /
     7	      (?<leading>\A|\n)
     8	      (?<indent>[ ]{0,3})
     9	      (?<fence>`{3,}|~{3,})
    10	      (?<info>[^\n]*)
    11	      \n
    12	      (?<code>.*?)
    13	      \n
    14	      \k<indent>\k<fence>(?<closing_whitespace>[ \t]*)
    15	      (?=\n|\z)
    16	    /mx.freeze
    17	    MAX_BLOCK_CHARS = 2_000
    18	    SUPPORTED_LANGUAGE_TAGS = (Rouge::Lexer.all.filter_map(&:tag) + ["plaintext"]).uniq.sort.freeze
    19	
    20	    def self.call(article)
    21	      new(article).call
    22	    end
    23	
    24	    def self.contains_unlabeled_code_blocks?(markdown)
    25	      markdown.to_s.to_enum(:scan, FENCED_CODE_BLOCK_REGEX).any? do
    26	        Regexp.last_match[:info].to_s.strip.blank?
    27	      end
    28	    end
    29	
    30	    def initialize(article, ai_client: nil)
    31	      @article = article
    32	      @ai_client = ai_client
    33	    end
    34	
    35	    def call
    36	      return false if article.blank? || article.body_markdown.blank? || article.user.blank?
    37	
    38	      unlabeled_blocks = extract_unlabeled_blocks
    39	      return false if unlabeled_blocks.blank?
    40	      return false if ai_client_unconfigured?
    41	
    42	      detected_languages = parse_response(ai_client.call(build_prompt(unlabeled_blocks)), unlabeled_blocks.count)
    43	      updated_markdown = apply_languages(detected_languages)
    44	      return false if updated_markdown == article.body_markdown
    45	
    46	      result = ContentRenderer.new(updated_markdown, source: article, user: article.user).process_article
    47	      article.update_columns(body_markdown: updated_markdown, processed_html: result.processed_html,
    48	                             reading_time: result.reading_time)
    49	      true
    50	    rescue StandardError => e
    51	      Rails.logger.error("Code block language detection failed for article #{article.id}: #{e}")
    52	      false
    53	    end
    54	
    55	    private
    56	
    57	    attr_reader :ai_client, :article
    58	
    59	    def ai_client
    60	      @ai_client ||= Ai::Base.new(model: Ai::Base::DEFAULT_LITE_MODEL, wrapper: self,
    61	                                  affected_content: article, affected_user: article.user)
    62	    end
    63	
    64	    def ai_client_unconfigured?
    65	      @ai_client.blank? && Ai::Base::DEFAULT_KEY.blank?
    66	    end
    67	
    68	    def extract_unlabeled_blocks
    69	      article.body_markdown.to_enum(:scan, FENCED_CODE_BLOCK_REGEX).filter_map do
    70	        match = Regexp.last_match
    71	        next unless match[:info].to_s.strip.blank?
    72	
    73	        match[:code]
    74	      end
    75	    end
    76	
    77	    def build_prompt(unlabeled_blocks)
    78	      blocks = unlabeled_blocks.each_with_index.map do |code, index|
    79	        <<~BLOCK
    80	          Block #{index + 1}:
    81	          #{code.to_s.strip.first(MAX_BLOCK_CHARS)}
    82	          #{code.to_s.length > MAX_BLOCK_CHARS ? "\n(Truncated)" : nil}
    83	        BLOCK
    84	      end.join("\n")
    85	
    86	      <<~PROMPT
    87	        You choose syntax-highlighting languages for markdown fenced code blocks.
    88	
    89	        Choose exactly one language for each code block from this list of supported highlighting tags:
    90	        #{SUPPORTED_LANGUAGE_TAGS.join(", ")}
    91	
    92	        Rules:
    93	        - Return ONLY a JSON array of strings in the same order as the blocks.
    94	        - Each string must be one of the supported tags above.
    95	        - If a block is ambiguous or plain text, use "plaintext".
    96	
    97	        Example response:
    98	        ["ruby", "javascript", "plaintext"]
    99	
   100	        #{blocks}
   101	      PROMPT
   102	    end
   103	
   104	    def parse_response(response, expected_count)
   105	      parsed = JSON.parse(strip_code_fence(response.to_s))
   106	      languages = parsed.is_a?(Array) ? parsed : parsed["languages"]
   107	      languages = Array(languages).first(expected_count).map { |language| normalize_language(language) }
   108	      languages.fill("plaintext", languages.length...expected_count)
   109	    rescue JSON::ParserError, TypeError
   110	      Array.new(expected_count, "plaintext")
   111	    end
   112	
   113	    def strip_code_fence(response)
   114	      response.strip.gsub(/\A```(?:json)?\s*|\s*```\z/, "")
   115	    end
   116	
   117	    def normalize_language(language)
   118	      lexer = Rouge::Lexer.find(language.to_s.strip.downcase)
   119	      normalized_language = lexer&.tag
   120	
   121	      if normalized_language.present? && SUPPORTED_LANGUAGE_TAGS.include?(normalized_language)
   122	        normalized_language
   123	      else
   124	        "plaintext"
   125	      end
   126	    end
   127	
   128	    def apply_languages(detected_languages)
   129	      unlabeled_index = -1
   130	
   131	      article.body_markdown.gsub(FENCED_CODE_BLOCK_REGEX) do
   132	        match = Regexp.last_match
   133	        next match[0] unless match[:info].to_s.strip.blank?
   134	
   135	        unlabeled_index += 1
   136	        language = detected_languages.fetch(unlabeled_index, "plaintext")
   137	
   138	        "#{match[:leading]}#{match[:indent]}#{match[:fence]}#{language}\n#{match[:code]}\n" \
   139	          "#{match[:indent]}#{match[:fence]}#{match[:closing_whitespace]}"
   140	      end
   141	    end
   142	  end
   143	end

FILE app/services/articles/enrich_image_attributes.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  module EnrichImageAttributes
     3	    IMAGES_IN_LIQUID_TAGS_SELECTORS = [
     4	      ".liquid-comment img", # CommentTag
     5	      ".ltag-github-readme-tag img", # GithubReadmeTag
     6	      ".ltag__link__pic img", # LinkTag and MediumTag profile pic
     7	      ".ltag__link__servicename img", # MediumTag
     8	      ".ltag__link__taglist img", # LinkTag
     9	      ".ltag__reddit--container img", # RedditTag
    10	      ".ltag__stackexchange--container img", # StackexchangeTag
    11	      ".ltag__twitter-tweet img", # TweetTag
    12	      ".ltag__user img", # UserTag and OrganizationTag
    13	      ".ltag__user-subscription-tag img", # UserSubscriptionTag
    14	      ".ltag_github-liquid-tag img", # GitHubIssueTag
    15	      ".podcastliquidtag img", # PodcastTag
    16	    ].join(", ").freeze
    17	
    18	    def self.call(article)
    19	      fast_image_headers = { "User-Agent" => "#{Settings::Community.community_name} (#{URL.url})" }
    20	      store_image_if_appropriate(article)
    21	      parsed_html = Nokogiri::HTML.fragment(article.processed_html)
    22	      main_image_height = default_image_height
    23	
    24	      # we ignore images contained in liquid tags as they are not animated
    25	      images = parsed_html.css("img") - parsed_html.css(IMAGES_IN_LIQUID_TAGS_SELECTORS)
    26	      return unless images.any? || article.main_image
    27	
    28	      images.each do |img|
    29	        src = img.attr("src")
    30	        next unless src
    31	
    32	        image = if URI.parse(src).relative?
    33	                  retrieve_image_from_uploader_store(src)
    34	                else
    35	                  src
    36	                end
    37	
    38	        next if image.blank?
    39	
    40	        img_properties = FastImage.new(image, timeout: 10, http_header: fast_image_headers)
    41	        img["width"], img["height"] = img_properties.size
    42	        img["data-animated"] = true if img_properties.type == :gif
    43	      end
    44	
    45	      if article.main_image && article.main_image.include?("ytimg.com")
    46	        main_image_height = 500 # Hardcoded for 1000/500
    47	      elsif article.main_image && Settings::UserExperience.cover_image_fit(subforem_id: article.subforem_id) == "limit"
    48	        main_image_size = FastImage.size(article.main_image, timeout: 15, http_header: fast_image_headers)
    49	        main_image_height = (main_image_size[1].to_f / main_image_size[0]) * 1000 if main_image_size
    50	      end
    51	
    52	      article.update_columns(processed_html: parsed_html.to_html, main_image_height: main_image_height)
    53	    end
    54	
    55	    def self.retrieve_image_from_uploader_store(src)
    56	      filename = File.basename(src)
    57	      uploader = ArticleImageUploader.new
    58	      uploader.retrieve_from_store!(filename)
    59	
    60	      return unless uploader.file.exists?
    61	
    62	      uploader.file&.file
    63	    end
    64	    private_class_method :retrieve_image_from_uploader_store
    65	
    66	    def self.default_image_height
    67	      # If FastImage times out, we don't want to fall back to the "max limit" — 300 is instead used as a safer default
    68	      # This will ultimately represent the height the image takes over *while it loads*.
    69	      # FastImage will reliably succeed. This is a fallback.
    70	      Settings::UserExperience.cover_image_fit == "limit" ? 300 : Settings::UserExperience.cover_image_height
    71	    end
    72	    private_class_method :default_image_height
    73	
    74	    def self.store_image_if_appropriate(article)
    75	      return if ApplicationConfig["AWS_BUCKET_NAME"].blank?
    76	
    77	      markdown_text = article.body_markdown
    78	      markdown_pattern = /!\[.*?\]\((.*?)\)/
    79	      html_pattern = /<img.*?src=["'](.*?)["']/
    80	      markdown_urls = markdown_text.scan(markdown_pattern).flatten
    81	      html_urls = markdown_text.scan(html_pattern).flatten
    82	      all_urls = markdown_urls + html_urls
    83	      stored_image_url = "https://#{ApplicationConfig['AWS_BUCKET_NAME']}.s3.amazonaws.com"
    84	      filtered_urls = all_urls.reject { |url| url.include?(stored_image_url) }
    85	      images_converted = []
    86	      filtered_urls.uniq.each do |url|
    87	        store = MediaStore.where(original_url: url).first_or_create
    88	        images_converted << store.output_url if store&.created_at&.> 1.minute.ago
    89	      rescue StandardError => e
    90	        Rails.logger.error("Error storing images: #{e.message}")
    91	      end
    92	      # This just updates the processed HTML based on new state (now has media stores)
    93	      article.evaluate_and_update_column_from_markdown if images_converted.any?
    94	    rescue StandardError => e
    95	      Rails.logger.error("Error storing images: #{e.message}")
    96	    end
    97	    private_class_method :store_image_if_appropriate
    98	  end
    99	end

FILE app/services/articles/page_view_updater.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  # This module is responsible for updating a specific page view for a given article and user.
     3	  #
     4	  # @see Articles::UpdatePageViewsWorker for the sibling that's responsible for recording page
     5	  #      views.
     6	  module PageViewUpdater
     7	    EXTENDED_PAGEVIEW_NUMBER = 60
     8	    # @param article_id [Integer]
     9	    # @param user_id [Integer]
    10	    #
    11	    # @return [TrueClass] if we updated or created a PageView
    12	    # @return [FalseClass] if we did not update a PageView
    13	    #
    14	    # @note Regardless of return status, consider the business logic successful unless we raise an
    15	    #       exception.  The return value is present for easing testing.  The `find_or_create_by`
    16	    #       adds a complication in the testing logic
    17	    #       (e.g., `expect { Articles::PageViewUpdater.call }.not_to change(PageView, :count) `
    18	    def self.call(article_id:, user_id:)
    19	      # Don't record views to non-existent or unpublished articles.
    20	      return false unless Article.published.exists?(id: article_id)
    21	      # Don't record author's own views.
    22	      return false if Article.published.from_subforem.exists?(id: article_id, user_id: user_id)
    23	
    24	      page_view = PageView.order(created_at: :desc)
    25	        .find_or_create_by(article_id: article_id, user_id: user_id)
    26	      return true if page_view.new_record?
    27	
    28	      new_time_mark = page_view.time_tracked_in_seconds + 15
    29	      page_view.update_column(:time_tracked_in_seconds, new_time_mark)
    30	      if new_time_mark == EXTENDED_PAGEVIEW_NUMBER
    31	        FeedEvent.record_journey_for(page_view.user, article: page_view.article, category: :extended_pageview)
    32	        UpdateUserInterestEmbeddingWorker.perform_async(user_id, article_id, 0.05) if user_id
    33	      end
    34	
    35	      true
    36	    end
    37	  end
    38	end

FILE app/services/articles/suggest.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  class Suggest
     3	    MAX_DEFAULT = 4
     4	
     5	    def self.call(article, max: MAX_DEFAULT)
     6	      new(article, max: max).call
     7	    end
     8	
     9	    def initialize(article, max: MAX_DEFAULT)
    10	      @article = article
    11	      @max = max
    12	      @total_articles_count = Article.published.from_subforem.estimated_count
    13	    end
    14	
    15	    def call
    16	      if cached_tag_list_array.any?
    17	        # avoid loading more data if we don't need to
    18	        tagged_suggestions = suggestions_by_tag(max: max)
    19	        return tagged_suggestions if tagged_suggestions.size == max
    20	
    21	        # if there are not enough tagged articles, load other suggestions
    22	        # ignoring tagged articles that might be relevant twice, hence avoiding duplicates
    23	        num_remaining_needed = max - tagged_suggestions.size
    24	        other_articles = other_suggestions(
    25	          max: num_remaining_needed,
    26	          ids_to_ignore: tagged_suggestions.map(&:id),
    27	        )
    28	        tagged_suggestions.union(other_articles)
    29	      else
    30	        other_suggestions
    31	      end
    32	    end
    33	
    34	    private
    35	
    36	    attr_reader :article, :max, :total_articles_count
    37	
    38	    def other_suggestions(max: MAX_DEFAULT, ids_to_ignore: [])
    39	      ids_to_ignore << article.id
    40	      Article.published.where(subforem_id: article.subforem_id)
    41	        .where(archived: false)
    42	        .where("published_at > ?", 3.months.ago)
    43	        .where.not(id: ids_to_ignore)
    44	        .not_authored_by(article.user_id)
    45	        .order(hotness_score: :desc)
    46	        .offset(rand(0..offset))
    47	        .where("score > ?", Settings::UserExperience.index_minimum_score)
    48	        .select(:id, :title, :path, :cached_tag_list, :cached_user, :main_image, :published, :published_at, :crossposted_at)
    49	        .first(max)
    50	    end
    51	
    52	    def suggestions_by_tag(max: MAX_DEFAULT)
    53	      Article
    54	        .published.where(subforem_id: article.subforem_id)
    55	        .where(archived: false)
    56	        .where("published_at > ?", 3.months.ago)
    57	        .cached_tagged_with_any(cached_tag_list_array)
    58	        .not_authored_by(article.user_id)
    59	        .where(tag_suggestion_query)
    60	        .order(hotness_score: :desc)
    61	        .offset(rand(0..offset))
    62	        .where("score > ?", Settings::UserExperience.index_minimum_score)
    63	        .select(:id, :title, :path, :cached_tag_list, :cached_user, :main_image, :published, :published_at, :crossposted_at)
    64	        .first(max)
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    def offset
    68	      total_articles_count > 1000 ? 200 : (total_articles_count / 10)
    69	    end
    70	
    71	    def tag_suggestion_query
    72	      # Fore big communities like DEV we can look at organic page views for indicator.
    73	      # For smaller communities, we'll a basic score check.
    74	      total_articles_count > 1000 ? "organic_page_views_past_month_count > 5" : "score > 1"
    75	    end
    76	
    77	    def cached_tag_list_array
    78	      (article.cached_tag_list || "").split(", ")
    79	    end
    80	  end
    81	end

FILE app/services/articles/suggest_stickies.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  class SuggestStickies
     3	    # @todo Should we make this configurable in the admin interface?
     4	    SUGGESTION_TAGS = %w[career productivity discuss explainlikeimfive].freeze
     5	
     6	    DEFAULT_TAG_ARTICLES_LIMIT = 3
     7	    DEFAULT_MORE_ARTICLES_LIMIT = 7
     8	
     9	    # @api public
    10	    #
    11	    # A set of suggested articles related to the given :article.
    12	    #
    13	    # @param article [ArticleDecorator]
    14	    # @param sample_size [Integer] How many Articles in the return set?
    15	    #
    16	    # @return [Array<Article>] An array Article records
    17	    #
    18	    # @note The resulting array of articles will be 30% articles that
    19	    #       share tags with the given article, and 70% of articles
    20	    #       that have tags in the SUGGESTION_TAGS constant.  These
    21	    #       percentages are related to the DEFAULT_TAG_ARTICLES_LIMIT
    22	    #       and DEFAULT_MORE_ARTICLES_LIMIT.
    23	    def self.call(article, sample_size: 3)
    24	      new(article, sample_size: sample_size).call
    25	    end
    26	
    27	    def initialize(article, sample_size:)
    28	      @article = article
    29	      @reaction_count_num = Rails.env.production? ? 15 : -1
    30	      @comment_count_num = Rails.env.production? ? 7 : -2
    31	      @sample_size = sample_size
    32	    end
    33	
    34	    def call
    35	      (tag_articles + more_articles).sample(@sample_size)
    36	    end
    37	
    38	    private
    39	
    40	    attr_accessor :article, :reaction_count_num, :comment_count_num
    41	
    42	    def tag_articles
    43	      article_tags = article.cached_tag_list.to_s.split(", ") - ["discuss"]
    44	
    45	      scope = Article
    46	        .where("public_reactions_count > ? OR comments_count > ?", reaction_count_num, comment_count_num)
    47	        .limit(DEFAULT_TAG_ARTICLES_LIMIT)
    48	
    49	      apply_common_scope(scope: scope, tags: article_tags)
    50	    end
    51	
    52	    # @note This previously used a `.limit(10 - tag_articles.count)`,
    53	    #       which meant we were running an unnecessary count query.
    54	    #       We also had a guard clause that said if we have more than
    55	    #       6 tag_articles, we should return an empty array.  However,
    56	    #       with the initial limit of 3 for tag articles, that guard
    57	    #       was unnecessary.
    58	    def more_articles
    59	      scope = Article
    60	        .where("comments_count > ?", comment_count_num)
    61	        .limit(DEFAULT_MORE_ARTICLES_LIMIT)
    62	
    63	      apply_common_scope(scope: scope, tags: SUGGESTION_TAGS)
    64	    end
    65	
    66	    # A helper method to ensure a consistent lookup
    67	    def apply_common_scope(scope:, tags:)
    68	      scope.published.from_subforem
    69	        .where(archived: false)
    70	        .cached_tagged_with_any(tags)
    71	        .unscope(:select)
    72	        .select(:id, :path, :title, :cached_tag_list, :cached_user, :organization_id, :user_id, :subforem_id) # Columns needed for _sticky_nav (includes cached_user for avatar)
    73	        .where.not(id: article.id)
    74	        .not_authored_by(article.user_id)
    75	        .where("published_at > ?", 5.days.ago)
    76	        .order(Arel.sql("RANDOM()"))
    77	    end
    78	  end
    79	end
