FILE app/services/articles/updater.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  class Updater
     3	    Result = Struct.new(:success, :article, keyword_init: true)
     4	
     5	    def self.call(...)
     6	      new(...).call
     7	    end
     8	
     9	    def initialize(user, article, article_params)
    10	      @user = user
    11	      @article = article
    12	      @article_params = normalize_params(article_params)
    13	    end
    14	
    15	    def call
    16	      user.rate_limiter.check_limit!(:article_update)
    17	      success = article.update(article_params)
    18	
    19	      if success
    20	        user.rate_limiter.track_limit_by_action(:article_update)
    21	
    22	        if became_unpublished?
    23	          remove_all_notifications
    24	          bust_author_profile_cache
    25	        end
    26	
    27	        send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers if remains_published?
    28	        refresh_auto_audience_segments if became_published?
    29	        complete_onboarding_first_post if became_published?
    30	      end
    31	
    32	      Result.new(success: success, article: article.decorate)
    33	    end
    34	
    35	    private
    36	
    37	    attr_reader :user, :article, :article_params
    38	
    39	    def normalize_params(original_params)
    40	      article_params = original_params.dup
    41	
    42	      # updated edited time only if already published and not edited by an admin
    43	      update_edited_at = article.user == user && article.published
    44	
    45	      # remove published_at values received from a user if an articles was published before (has past published_at)
    46	      # published_at will remain as it was in this case
    47	      article_params.delete :published_at if article.published_at && !article.scheduled?
    48	
    49	      # NOTE: It's surprising that this is article.user and not @user
    50	      Articles::Attributes.new(article_params, article.user, organization: article.organization)
    51	        .for_update(update_edited_at: update_edited_at)
    52	    end
    53	
    54	    def refresh_auto_audience_segments
    55	      user.refresh_auto_audience_segments
    56	    end
    57	
    58	    def complete_onboarding_first_post
    59	      return if user.registered_at&.before?(28.days.ago)
    60	
    61	      user.onboarding_checklist&.complete_item!("made_first_post")
    62	    end
    63	
    64	    def became_published?
    65	      article.published? && !article.published_previously_was
    66	    end
    67	
    68	    def remains_published?
    69	      article.published && article.saved_change_to_published.blank?
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    def became_unpublished?
    73	      article.saved_changes["published"] == [true, false]
    74	    end
    75	
    76	    # If the article has already been published and is only being updated, then we need to create
    77	    # mentions and send notifications to mentioned users inline via the Mentions::CreateAll service.
    78	    def send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers
    79	      Mentions::CreateAll.call(article)
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    # Remove any associated notifications if Article is unpublished
    83	    def remove_all_notifications
    84	      Notification.remove_all_by_action_without_delay(notifiable_ids: article.id, notifiable_type: "Article",
    85	                                                      action: "Published")
    86	      ContextNotification.delete_by(context_id: article.id, context_type: "Article",
    87	                                    action: "Published")
    88	
    89	      if article.comments.exists?
    90	        Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.comments.ids,
    91	                                notifiable_type: "Comment")
    92	      end
    93	      return unless article.mentions.exists?
    94	
    95	      Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.mentions.ids,
    96	                              notifiable_type: "Mention")
    97	    end
    98	
    99	    def bust_author_profile_cache
   100	      EdgeCache::BustUser.call(article.user)
   101	    end
   102	  end
   103	end

    10	    class ArticleScoreCalculatorForUser

    24	      }.freeze
    25	
    26	      # @param user [User] the user for whom we're calculating the article score.
    27	      # @param config [Hash<Symbol,(Integer|Float)>] exposes the means
    28	      #   for overriding the default configuration values
    29	      #
    30	      # @see DEFAULT_CONFIGURATION
    31	      def initialize(user:, config: {})
    32	        @user = user
    33	        DEFAULT_CONFIGURATION.each_pair do |key, value|
    34	          instance_variable_set("@#{key}", config.fetch(key, value))
    35	        end
    36	      end
    37	
    38	      # @api private
    39	      def score_followed_user(article)
    40	        user_following_users_ids.include?(article.user_id) ? @followed_user_score : @not_followed_user_score
    41	      end
    42	
    43	      # @api private
    44	      def score_followed_tags(article)
    45	        return @nil_user_tag_score unless @user
    46	
    47	        article_tags = article.decorate.cached_tag_list_array
    48	        user_followed_tags.sum do |tag|
    49	          article_tags.include?(tag.name) ? tag.points * @followed_tag_weight : @not_followed_tag_score
    50	        end
    51	      end
    52	
    53	      # @api private
    54	      def score_followed_organization(article)
    55	        return @not_followed_org_score unless article.organization_id?
    56	
    57	        user_following_org_ids.include?(article.organization_id) ? @followed_org_score : @not_followed_org_score
    58	      end
    59	
    60	      # @api private
    61	      def score_experience_level(article)
    62	        user_experience_level = @user&.setting&.experience_level || @default_user_xp_level
    63	
    64	        # Calculate the distance between the user's experience level
    65	        # and that of the article's experience level.
    66	        - (((article.experience_level_rating - user_experience_level).abs / 2) * @xp_level_weight)
    67	      end
    68	
    69	      # @api private
    70	      def score_comments(article)
    71	        article.comments_count * @comment_weight
    72	      end
    73	
    74	      private
    75	
    76	      def user_followed_tags
    77	        @user_followed_tags ||= (@user&.decorate&.cached_followed_tags || [])
    78	      end
    79	
    80	      def user_following_org_ids
    81	        @user_following_org_ids ||= (@user&.cached_following_organizations_ids || [])
    82	      end
    83	
    84	      def user_following_users_ids
    85	        @user_following_users_ids ||= (@user&.cached_following_users_ids || [])
    86	      end
    87	    end
    88	  end
    89	end

FILE app/services/articles/feeds/basic.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  module Feeds
     3	    class Basic
     4	      def initialize(user: nil, number_of_articles: Article::DEFAULT_FEED_PAGINATION_WINDOW_SIZE, page: 1, tag: nil)
     5	        @user = user
     6	        @number_of_articles = number_of_articles
     7	        @page = [page, 1].max
     8	        @tag = tag
     9	        @article_score_applicator = Articles::Feeds::ArticleScoreCalculatorForUser.new(user: @user)
    10	      end
    11	
    12	      def default_home_feed(**_kwargs)
    13	        articles = Article.published
    14	          .order(hotness_score: :desc)
    15	          .with_at_least_home_feed_minimum_score
    16	          .limit(@number_of_articles)
    17	          .offset((@page - 1) * @number_of_articles)
    18	          .limited_column_select
    19	          .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :context_notes, top_comments: :user)
    20	          .from_subforem
    21	
    22	        return articles unless @user
    23	
    24	        articles = articles.where.not(user_id: UserBlock.cached_blocked_ids_for_blocker(@user.id))
    25	        if (hidden_tags = @user.cached_antifollowed_tag_names).any?
    26	          articles = articles.not_cached_tagged_with_any(hidden_tags)
    27	        end
    28	        articles.sort_by.with_index do |article, index|
    29	          tag_score = score_followed_tags(article)
    30	          user_score = score_followed_user(article)
    31	          org_score = score_followed_organization(article)
    32	
    33	          # NOTE: Not quite understanding the purpose of the `-
    34	          # index`.  My guess is that it helps reduce the impact of the
    35	          # hotness score on the sort order.
    36	          tag_score + org_score + user_score - index
    37	        end.reverse!
    38	      end
    39	
    40	      private
    41	
    42	      # Alias :feed to preserve past implementations, but favoring a
    43	      # convergence of interface implementations.
    44	      alias feed default_home_feed
    45	
    46	      # Creating :more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized to conform
    47	      # to the public interface of
    48	      # Articles::Feeds::LargeForemExperimental
    49	      alias more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized default_home_feed
    50	
    51	      delegate(:score_followed_tags,
    52	               :score_followed_user,
    53	               :score_followed_organization,
    54	               to: :@article_score_applicator)
    55	    end
    56	  end
    57	end

FILE app/services/articles/feeds/custom.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  module Feeds
     3	    TIME_AGO_MAX = Rails.env.production? ? 10.days.ago : 90.days.ago
     4	
     5	    class Custom
     6	      def initialize(user: nil, number_of_articles: Article::DEFAULT_FEED_PAGINATION_WINDOW_SIZE, page: 1, tag: nil, feed_config: nil)
     7	        @user = user
     8	        @number_of_articles = number_of_articles
     9	        @page = [page, 1].max
    10	        @tag = tag
    11	        @feed_config = feed_config
    12	      end
    13	
    14	      def default_home_feed(**_kwargs)
    15	        return [] if @feed_config.nil? || @user.nil?
    16	        
    17	        execute_feed_query
    18	      end
    19	
    20	      private
    21	
    22	      def execute_feed_query
    23	        # Optimize lookback calculation - cache the result
    24	        # Note: Partial indexes are optimized for 7-day lookback (covers 95%+ of queries)
    25	        # If you change this, consider updating the partial indexes in the migration
    26	        lookback_setting = Settings::UserExperience.feed_lookback_days.to_i
    27	        lookback = lookback_setting.positive? ? lookback_setting.days.ago : TIME_AGO_MAX
    28	        
    29	        # Pre-calculate user-specific data to avoid repeated database calls
    30	        user_data = preload_user_data
    31	        
    32	        # Build optimized base query with better index usage
    33	        articles = build_optimized_base_query(lookback, user_data)
    34	        
    35	        # Apply user-specific filters early in the query
    36	        articles = apply_user_filters(articles, user_data)
    37	        
    38	        # Apply subforem-specific filters
    39	        articles = apply_subforem_filters(articles)
    40	        
    41	        # Apply weighted shuffle if needed
    42	        articles = weighted_shuffle(articles, @feed_config.shuffle_weight) if @feed_config.shuffle_weight.positive?
    43	        
    44	        # Randomly shuffle top 5 articles if all articles are recent (within a week)
    45	        articles = shuffle_top_five_if_recent(articles)
    46	        
    47	        articles
    48	      end
    49	
    50	
    51	
    52	      def preload_user_data
    53	        {
    54	          blocked_user_ids: UserBlock.cached_blocked_ids_for_blocker(@user.id),
    55	          hidden_tags: @user.cached_antifollowed_tag_names,
    56	          user_activity: @user.user_activity
    57	        }
    58	      end
    59	
    60	      def build_optimized_base_query(lookback, user_data)
    61	        # Use a more efficient query structure with better index hints
    62	        base_query = Article.published
    63	          .with_at_least_home_feed_minimum_score
    64	          .where("articles.published_at > ?", lookback)
    65	          .select("articles.*, (#{score_sql_method}) as computed_score")
    66	          .order(Arel.sql("computed_score DESC"))
    67	          .limit(@number_of_articles)
    68	          .offset((@page - 1) * @number_of_articles)
    69	          .limited_column_select
    70	          .includes(:subforem) # Only include essential associations
    71	          .from_subforem
    72	
    73	        # Include necessary associations for performance
    74	        base_query = base_query.includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :context_notes, top_comments: :user)
    75	        
    76	        base_query
    77	      end
    78	
    79	      def score_sql_method
    80	        @feed_config.score_sql(@user)
    81	      end
    82	
    83	
    84	      def apply_user_filters(articles, user_data)
    85	        # Apply user-specific filters early to reduce dataset size
    86	        if user_data[:blocked_user_ids].any?
    87	          articles = articles.where.not(user_id: user_data[:blocked_user_ids])
    88	        end
    89	        
    90	        if user_data[:hidden_tags].any?
    91	          articles = if ENV["OPTIMIZED_FEED_TAGS_QUERY"] == "true"
    92	                       articles.where.not("articles.tags_array && ARRAY[?]::text[]", user_data[:hidden_tags].map(&:to_s))
    93	                     else
    94	                       articles.not_cached_tagged_with_any(user_data[:hidden_tags])
    95	                     end
    96	        end
    97	        
    98	        articles
    99	      end
   100	
   101	      def apply_subforem_filters(articles)
   102	        # Apply subforem-specific filters
   103	        if RequestStore.store[:subforem_id] == RequestStore.store[:root_subforem_id]
   104	          articles = articles.where(type_of: :full_post)
   105	        end
   106	        
   107	        articles
   108	      end
   109	
   110	      def weighted_shuffle(arr, shuffle_weight)
   111	        # Each element gets a new sort key: its original index plus a random offset.
   112	        # We choose the random offset uniformly from -2*shuffle_weight to 2*shuffle_weight.
   113	        # The average absolute offset then is (2*shuffle_weight)/2 = shuffle_weight.
   114	        arr.each_with_index.sort_by do |item, index|
   115	          index + (rand * (4 * shuffle_weight) - 2 * shuffle_weight)
   116	        end.map(&:first)
   117	      end
   118	
   119	      def shuffle_top_five_if_recent(articles)
   120	        return articles if articles.empty?
   121	        
   122	        # Check if all articles are published within the last week
   123	        one_week_ago = 1.week.ago
   124	        all_recent = articles.all? { |article| article.published_at > one_week_ago }
   125	        
   126	        return articles unless all_recent
   127	        
   128	        # Use dynamic shuffle count only if recent_page_views_shuffle_weight > 0
   129	        shuffle_count = if @feed_config.recent_page_views_shuffle_weight > 0.0
   130	                          calculate_dynamic_shuffle_count
   131	                        else
   132	                          5 # Default behavior
   133	                        end
   134	        
   135	        # Split articles into top N (based on shuffle count) and the rest
   136	        top_articles = articles.first(shuffle_count)
   137	        rest = articles[shuffle_count..-1] || []
   138	        
   139	        # Randomly shuffle the top articles
   140	        shuffled_top_articles = top_articles.shuffle
   141	        
   142	        # Return shuffled top articles + unshuffled rest
   143	        shuffled_top_articles + rest
   144	      end
   145	
   146	      def calculate_dynamic_shuffle_count
   147	        return 5 unless @user&.user_activity&.recently_viewed_articles&.any?
   148	        
   149	        # Get the most recent page view timestamp
   150	        most_recent_page_view = @user.user_activity.recently_viewed_articles.first
   151	        return 5 unless most_recent_page_view&.size >= 2
   152	        
   153	        begin
   154	          most_recent_timestamp = most_recent_page_view[1].to_datetime
   155	          hours_since_last_view = ((Time.current - most_recent_timestamp) / 1.hour).ceil
   156	          
   157	          # Calculate base shuffle count: 20 for within 1 hour, decreasing by 1 for each additional hour
   158	          # Minimum of 5 (original behavior)
   159	          base_shuffle_count = [21 - hours_since_last_view, 5].max
   160	          
   161	          # Apply the weight multiplier
   162	          weighted_shuffle_count = (base_shuffle_count * @feed_config.recent_page_views_shuffle_weight).round
   163	          
   164	          # Ensure minimum of 5 and maximum of 20
   165	          [[weighted_shuffle_count, 5].max, 20].min
   166	        rescue StandardError
   167	          # If there's any error parsing the timestamp, fall back to default
   168	          5
   169	        end
   170	      end
   171	
   172	      # Preserve the public interface
   173	      alias feed default_home_feed
   174	      alias more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized default_home_feed
   175	    end
   176	  end
   177	end

FILE app/services/articles/feeds/find_featured_story.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  module Feeds
     3	    module FindFeaturedStory
     4	      # @param stories [ActiveRecord::Relation, #detect] pick a
     5	      #        featured story from this enumerable
     6	      # @param must_have_main_image [Boolean] if true, the featured
     7	      #        story must have a main image
     8	      # @return [Article]
     9	      #
    10	      # @note the must_have_main_image parameter name matches PR #15240
    11	      #
    12	      # @note One might assume that this would query on the
    13	      #       `Articles.where(featured: true)` but in my sleuthing,
    14	      #       the origin of this method's inner logic never included a
    15	      #       consideration for `featured == true`.  Perhaps that
    16	      #       should change?  This has been reported in
    17	      #       https://github.com/forem/forem/issues/15613 and impacts
    18	      #       Articles::Feeds::VariantQuery
    19	      def self.call(stories, must_have_main_image: true)
    20	        featured_story =
    21	          if must_have_main_image
    22	            if stories.is_a?(ActiveRecord::Relation)
    23	              stories.where.not(main_image: nil).first
    24	            else
    25	              stories.detect { |story| story.main_image.present? }
    26	            end
    27	          else
    28	            stories.first
    29	          end
    30	
    31	        featured_story || Article.new
    32	      end
    33	    end
    34	  end
    35	end

FILE app/services/articles/feeds/large_forem_experimental.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  module Feeds
     3	    class LargeForemExperimental
     4	      def initialize(user: nil, number_of_articles: Article::DEFAULT_FEED_PAGINATION_WINDOW_SIZE, page: 1, tag: nil)
     5	        @user = user
     6	        @number_of_articles = number_of_articles
     7	        @page = page
     8	        @tag = tag
     9	        @article_score_applicator = Articles::Feeds::ArticleScoreCalculatorForUser.new(user: user)
    10	      end
    11	
    12	      def default_home_feed(user_signed_in: false)
    13	        _featured_story, stories = featured_story_and_default_home_feed(user_signed_in: user_signed_in, ranking: true)
    14	        stories
    15	      end
    16	
    17	      # @param user_signed_in [Boolean] are we treating this as an
    18	      #        anonymous user?
    19	      # @param ranking [Boolean] if true, apply a ranking algorithm
    20	      # @param must_have_main_image [Boolean] if true, the featured
    21	      #        story must have a main image
    22	      #
    23	      # @note the must_have_main_image parameter name matches PR #15240
    24	      def featured_story_and_default_home_feed(user_signed_in: false, ranking: true, must_have_main_image: true)
    25	        featured_story, hot_stories = globally_hot_articles(user_signed_in, must_have_main_image: must_have_main_image)
    26	        hot_stories = rank_and_sort_articles(hot_stories) if @user && ranking
    27	        [featured_story, hot_stories]
    28	      end
    29	
    30	      # Adding an alias to preserve public method signature.
    31	      # Eventually, we should be able to remove the alias.
    32	      alias default_home_feed_and_featured_story featured_story_and_default_home_feed
    33	
    34	      def more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized
    35	        _featured_story, stories = featured_story_and_default_home_feed(user_signed_in: true)
    36	        first_quarter(stories).shuffle + last_three_quarters(stories)
    37	      end
    38	
    39	      # Adding an alias to preserve public method signature.  However,
    40	      # in this code base there are no further references of
    41	      # :more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized_at_end
    42	      alias more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized_at_end more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized
    43	
    44	      # @api private
    45	      def rank_and_sort_articles(articles)
    46	        ranked_articles = articles.each_with_object({}) do |article, result|
    47	          article_points = score_single_article(article)
    48	          result[article] = article_points
    49	        end
    50	        ranked_articles = ranked_articles.sort_by { |_article, article_points| -article_points }.map(&:first)
    51	        ranked_articles.to(@number_of_articles - 1)
    52	      end
    53	
    54	      # @api private
    55	      def score_single_article(article, base_article_points: 0)
    56	        article_points = base_article_points
    57	        article_points += score_followed_user(article)
    58	        article_points += score_followed_organization(article)
    59	        article_points += score_followed_tags(article)
    60	        article_points += score_experience_level(article)
    61	        article_points += score_comments(article)
    62	        article_points
    63	      end
    64	
    65	      delegate(:score_followed_user,
    66	               :score_followed_tags,
    67	               :score_followed_organization,
    68	               :score_experience_level,
    69	               :score_comments,
    70	               to: :@article_score_applicator)
    71	
    72	      # @api private
    73	      # rubocop:disable Layout/LineLength
    74	      def globally_hot_articles(user_signed_in, must_have_main_image: true, article_score_threshold: -15, min_rand_limit: 15, max_rand_limit: 80)
    75	        # rubocop:enable Layout/LineLength
    76	        if user_signed_in
    77	          hot_stories = experimental_hot_story_grab
    78	          hot_stories = hot_stories.not_authored_by(UserBlock.cached_blocked_ids_for_blocker(@user.id))
    79	          featured_story = featured_story_from(stories: hot_stories, must_have_main_image: must_have_main_image)
    80	          new_stories = Article.published.from_subforem
    81	            .where("score > ?", article_score_threshold)
    82	            .limited_column_select.includes(top_comments: :user)
    83	            .order(published_at: :desc)
    84	            .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :subforem, :context_notes)
    85	            .limit(rand(min_rand_limit..max_rand_limit))
    86	          hot_stories = hot_stories.to_a + new_stories.to_a
    87	        else
    88	          hot_stories = Article.published.from_subforem.limited_column_select
    89	            .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :subforem, top_comments: :user)
    90	            .page(@page).per(@number_of_articles)
    91	            .with_at_least_home_feed_minimum_score
    92	            .order(hotness_score: :desc)
    93	          featured_story = featured_story_from(stories: hot_stories, must_have_main_image: must_have_main_image)
    94	        end
    95	        [featured_story, hot_stories.to_a]
    96	      end
    97	
    98	      private
    99	
   100	      def featured_story_from(stories:, must_have_main_image:)
   101	        return stories.first unless must_have_main_image
   102	
   103	        stories.where.not(main_image: nil).first
   104	      end
   105	
   106	      def experimental_hot_story_grab
   107	        start_time = Articles::Feeds.oldest_published_at_to_consider_for(user: @user)
   108	        Article.published.limited_column_select.includes(top_comments: :user)
   109	          .includes(:distinct_reaction_categories, :subforem, :context_notes)
   110	          .where("published_at > ?", start_time)
   111	          .page(@page).per(@number_of_articles)
   112	          .order(score: :desc)
   113	      end
   114	
   115	      def first_quarter(array)
   116	        array[0...(array.length / 4)]
   117	      end
   118	
   119	      def last_three_quarters(array)
   120	        array[(array.length / 4)..array.length]
   121	      end
   122	    end
   123	  end
   124	end

FILE app/services/articles/feeds/variant_query.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  module Feeds
     3	    # This object is responsible for building the relevancy feed based on the given user and the
     4	    # variant they are currently assigned.
     5	    #
     6	    # This class uses the Articles::Feeds::VariantQuery::Config to create a SQL statement.  The SQL
     7	    # statement calculates a `relevancy_score` for each article based on the configuration of the
     8	    # chosen relevancy levers.
     9	    #
    10	    # @see config/feed-variants/README.md
    11	    # @see app/models/articles/feeds/README.md
    12	    class VariantQuery
    13	      Config = Struct.new(
    14	        :variant,
    15	        :description,
    16	        :levers, # Array <Articles::Feeds::RelevancyLever::Configured>
    17	        :order_by, # Articles::Feeds::OrderByLever
    18	        :max_days_since_published,
    19	        # when true, each time you call the query you will get different randomized numbers; when
    20	        # false, the resulting randomized numbers will be the same within a window of time.
    21	        :reseed_randomizer_on_each_request,
    22	        keyword_init: true,
    23	      ) do
    24	        alias_method :reseed_randomizer_on_each_request?, :reseed_randomizer_on_each_request
    25	      end

    97	        # These are the variables we'll pass to the SQL statement.
    98	        repeated_query_variables = query_parameters.merge(
    99	          omit_article_ids: omit_article_ids,

   101	          user_id: @user&.id,
   102	        )

   104	        # This needs to be an Array for Article.sanitize_sql.
   105	        unsanitized_sql_sub_query = [
   106	          sql_sub_query(
   107	            only_featured: only_featured,
   108	            must_have_main_image: must_have_main_image,
   109	            limit: limit,
   110	            offset: offset,
   111	            omit_article_ids: omit_article_ids,
   112	          ),
   113	          repeated_query_variables,
   114	        ]
   115	
   116	        # Following this blog post: https://pganalyze.com/blog/active-record-subqueries-rails#the-from-subquery
   117	        #
   118	        # We're using the above `unsanitized_sql_sub_query` to create a result set so we can join to
   119	        # articles and then sort on the attributes on either the article or the result set
   120	        # (e.g. sort on the relevancy score).

   122	          "INNER JOIN (" \
   123	          "\n--- The setseed needs to be called independently; later we reference seeder \n" \
   124	          "WITH seeder AS (SELECT setseed(#{Float(@seed)})) " \
   125	          "\n--- We are using the inner logic to build relevancy score" \
   126	          "\n--- The outer part, with seeder, is to create a stable randomized number \n" \
   127	          "SELECT inner_article_relevancies.id, " \
   128	          "inner_article_relevancies.relevancy_score, " \
   129	          "RANDOM() AS randomized_value " \
   130	          "FROM seeder, " \
   131	          "(#{Article.sanitize_sql(unsanitized_sql_sub_query)}) AS inner_article_relevancies" \
   132	          ") AS article_relevancies ON articles.id = article_relevancies.id",
   133	        )

   135	        # This sub-query allows us to take the hard work of the hand-coded unsanitized sql and
   136	        # create a sub-query that we can use to help ensure that we can use all of the ActiveRecord
   137	        # goodness of scopes (e.g., limited_column_select) and eager includes.

   146	                when "top_comments"

   148	                when "more_inclusive_top_comments"

   150	                when "recent_good_comments"

   152	                when "more_inclusive_recent_good_comments"

   154	                when "most_inclusive_recent_good_comments"

   158	                end

   162	        end
   163	
   164	        scope

   181	      # The featured story should be the article that:

   183	      # - has the highest relevance score for the nil_user version

   188	      #

   191	      #
   192	      # @note Per prior work, a featured story is the article that has a main image, is marked as
   193	      #       featured (e.g., featured = true), and has the highest relevance score.  In the
   194	      #       Articles::Feeds::LargeForemExperimental object we used the hotness_score to determine

FILE app/services/edge_cache/bust_article.rb
     1	module EdgeCache
     2	  class BustArticle
     3	    TIMEFRAMES = [
     4	      [-> { 1.week.ago }, "week"],
     5	      [-> { 1.month.ago }, "month"],
     6	      [-> { 1.year.ago }, "year"],
     7	      [-> { 5.years.ago }, "infinity"],
     8	    ].freeze
     9	    def self.call(article)
    10	      return unless article
    11	
    12	      article.purge
    13	      article.user&.purge
    14	      article.organization&.purge
    15	
    16	      cache_bust = EdgeCache::Bust.new
    17	
    18	      bust_home_pages(cache_bust, article)
    19	      bust_tag_pages(cache_bust, article)
    20	      bust_user_profile_pages(article)
    21	    end
    22	
    23	    def self.bust_home_pages(cache_bust, article)
    24	      if article.published_at.to_i > Time.current.to_i
    25	        # TODO
    26	        cache_bust.call("/")
    27	      end
    28	
    29	      if article.video.present? && article.published_at.to_i > 10.days.ago.to_i
    30	        cache_bust.call("/videos")
    31	      end
    32	
    33	      TIMEFRAMES.each do |timestamp, interval|
    34	        next unless Article.published.where("published_at > ?", timestamp.call)
    35	          .order(score: :desc).limit(3).ids.include?(article.id)
    36	
    37	        cache_bust.call("/top/#{interval}")
    38	      end
    39	
    40	      if article.published && article.published_at > 1.hour.ago
    41	        cache_bust.call("/latest")
    42	      end
    43	
    44	      cache_bust.call("/") if Article.published.order(hotness_score: :desc).limit(4).ids.include?(article.id)
    45	    end
    46	
    47	    private_class_method :bust_home_pages
    48	
    49	    def self.bust_tag_pages(cache_bust, article)
    50	      return unless article.published
    51	
    52	      article.tag_list.each do |tag|
    53	        if article.published_at.to_i > 2.minutes.ago.to_i
    54	          cache_bust.call("/t/#{tag}/latest")
    55	        end
    56	
    57	        TIMEFRAMES.each do |timestamp, interval|
    58	          next unless Article.published.where("published_at > ?", timestamp.call).cached_tagged_with_any(tag)
    59	            .order(score: :desc).limit(3).ids.include?(article.id)
    60	
    61	          cache_bust.call("/top/#{interval}")
    62	          12.times do |i|
    63	            cache_bust.call("/api/articles?tag=#{tag}&top=#{i}")
    64	          end
    65	        end
    66	
    67	        next unless rand(2) == 1 &&
    68	          Article.published.cached_tagged_with(tag)
    69	            .order(hotness_score: :desc).limit(2).ids.include?(article.id)
    70	
    71	        cache_bust.call("/t/#{tag}")
    72	      end
    73	    end
    74	
    75	    private_class_method :bust_tag_pages
    76	
    77	    def self.bust_user_profile_pages(article)
    78	      return unless article.user
    79	
    80	      EdgeCache::PurgeByKey.call(
    81	        article.user.profile_cache_keys,
    82	        fallback_paths: article.user.profile_cache_bust_paths,
    83	      )
    84	    end
    85	
    86	    private_class_method :bust_user_profile_pages
    87	  end
    88	end

    22	    def self.sort(relation, term, sort_by, sort_direction)

    25	
    26	      return relation.reorder(sort_by => sort_direction) if sort_by&.to_sym == :published_at && sort_direction

FILE app/queries/articles/active_threads_query.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  # TODO: [yheuhtozr] possible future tag name i18n: see https://github.com/forem/forem/pull/16004#discussion_r780879507
     3	  class ActiveThreadsQuery
     4	    DEFAULT_OPTIONS = {
     5	      tags: ["discuss"],
     6	      time_ago: nil,
     7	      count: 10
     8	    }.with_indifferent_access.freeze
     9	
    10	    # Get the "plucked" attribute information for the article thread.
    11	    #
    12	    # @param relation [ActiveRecord::Relation] the original Article scope
    13	    # @param options [Hash]
    14	    # @option options [Array<String>] :tags which tags to select
    15	    # @option options [NilClass, String, ActiveSupport::TimeWithZone] :time_ago
    16	    # @option options [Integer] :count the number of records to pluck
    17	    #
    18	    # @return [Array<Array>] The inner array is the plucked attribute
    19	    #         values for the selected articles.  Which means be mindful
    20	    #         of the order you pass for attributes.
    21	    #
    22	    # @note The order of attributes and behavior of this method is from
    23	    #       past implementations.  A refactor to consider would be to
    24	    #       create a data structure.
    25	    #
    26	    # @see `./app/views/articles/_widget_list_item.html.erb` for the
    27	    #      importance of maintaining position of these parameters.
    28	    def self.call(relation: Article.published.from_subforem, **options)
    29	      minimum_score = Settings::UserExperience.home_feed_minimum_score.to_i
    30	      options = DEFAULT_OPTIONS.merge(options)
    31	      tags, time_ago, count = options.values_at(:tags, :time_ago, :count)
    32	
    33	      relation = relation.limit(count)
    34	      relation = relation.cached_tagged_with(tags)
    35	      relation = relation.where(score: minimum_score..)
    36	      relation = if time_ago == "latest"
    37	                   relation.order(published_at: :desc)
    38	                 elsif time_ago
    39	                   relation.where(published_at: time_ago..).order(comments_count: :desc)
    40	                 else
    41	                   relation.where(published_at: 3.days.ago..).order("last_comment_at DESC NULLS LAST")
    42	                 end
    43	      relation.pluck(:path, :title, :comments_count, :created_at, :subforem_id)
    44	    end
    45	  end
    46	end

     1	module Articles

    10	      @q = params[:q]
    11	      @top = params[:top]

    14	    end

    16	    def call
    17	      @articles = published_articles_with_users_and_organizations
    18	
    19	      if q.present?
    20	        @articles = query_articles
    21	      end
    22	
    23	      if top.present?
    24	        @articles = top_articles.order(score: :desc)

    47	      Article.published.from_subforem

    76	      @relation = @relation.not_cached_tagged_with_any(hidden_tags) if hidden_tags.any?
    77	      @relation = @relation.includes(:distinct_reaction_categories)
    78	      @relation = @relation.where("score >= 0") # Never return negative score articles
    79	      @relation = @relation.from_subforem
    80	
    81	      relation
    82	    end
    83	
    84	    def sort
    85	      return relation unless SORT_PARAMS.include?(sort_by&.to_sym)
    86	
    87	      relation.order(sort_by => sort_direction)
    88	    end
    89	
    90	    def paginate
    91	      relation.page(page).per(per_page)
    92	    end
    93	  end
    94	end

The included concerns add hidden dependencies: reactions are `dependent: :destroy`, comments are nullified by Article itself, webpage references and liquid embed references have their own commit/destroy callbacks, and Algolia indexing is injected through a concern. I’m now auditing request entry points and cross-cutting services so the final map is grouped by area instead of a raw grep dump.

   141	    def calculate_flags_for_single_article(article)
   142	      privileged_article_reactions = article.reactions.privileged_category.select do |reaction|
   143	        reaction.reactable_type == "Article"
   144	      end
   145	      vomit_article_reactions = privileged_article_reactions.select { |reaction| reaction.category == "vomit" }
   146	      vomit_count = 0
   147	
   148	      if vomit_article_reactions.present?
   149	        vomit_article_reactions.each do |vomit_reaction|
   150	          vomit_count += 1 if vomit_reaction.status != "invalid"
   151	        end
   152	      end
   153	
   154	      vomit_count
   155	    end
   156	  end

     1	class ArticleApprovalsController < ApplicationController
     2	  def create
     3	    @article = Article.find(params[:id])
     4	    unless current_user.any_admin?
     5	      # Check that the article can be moderated by the user
     6	      authorize(@article, :moderate?)
     7	      tags = @article.decorate.tags
     8	      # Raise if no tags require approval to begin with
     9	      raise Pundit::NotAuthorizedError unless tags.pluck(:requires_approval).include?(true)
    10	
    11	      # Raise if user is not authorized to approve any tag that requires approval.
    12	      tags.each do |tag|
    13	        authorize(Tag.find(tag.id), :update?) if tag.requires_approval
    14	      end
    15	    end
    16	    @article.update(approved: params[:approved])
    17	    redirect_to "#{Addressable::URI.parse(@article.path).path}/mod"
    18	  end
    19	end

    65	               Articles::Feeds::Basic.new(user: current_user, page: @page, tag: params[:tag])
    66	             elsif feed_strategy == "configured" && params[:type_of] != "following"
    67	
    68	               @feed_config = if params[:item]
    69	                                FeedConfig.find_by(id: params[:item]) || FeedConfig.order("feed_success_score DESC").limit(rand(15)).sample || FeedConfig.first_or_create
    70	                              elsif rand(20) == 0 # 5% of the time, we'll just pick a random feed config
    71	                                FeedConfig.where("feed_impressions_count < 100").order("RANDOM()").limit(1).first || FeedConfig.order("feed_success_score DESC").limit(rand(15)).sample || FeedConfig.first_or_create
    72	                              else
    73	                                FeedConfig.order("feed_success_score DESC").limit(rand(15)).sample || FeedConfig.first_or_create
    74	                              end
    75	               Articles::Feeds::Custom.new(user: current_user, page: @page, tag: params[:tag],
    76	                                           feed_config: @feed_config)
    77	             else
    78	               Articles::Feeds.feed_for(
    79	                 user: current_user,
    80	                 controller: self,
    81	                 page: @page,
    82	                 tag: params[:tag],
    83	                 number_of_articles: 35,
    84	                 type_of: params[:type_of] || "discover",
    85	               )
    86	             end
    87	      # Hey, why the to_a you say?  Because the
    88	      # LargeForemExperimental has already done this.  But the
    89	      # weighted strategy has not.  I also don't want to alter the
    90	      # weighted query implementation as it returns a lovely
    91	      # ActiveRecord::Relation.  So this is a compromise.
    92	
    93	      feed.more_comments_minimal_weight_randomized(comments_variant: @comments_variant)
    94	    end
    95	
    96	    def signed_out_base_feed
    97	      feed = if Settings::UserExperience.feed_strategy == "basic"
    98	               Articles::Feeds::Basic.new(user: nil, page: @page, tag: params[:tag])
    99	             else
   100	               Articles::Feeds.feed_for(
   101	                 user: current_user,
   102	                 controller: self,
   103	                 page: @page,
   104	                 tag: params[:tag],
   105	                 number_of_articles: 25,
   106	                 type_of: "discover",
   107	               )
   108	             end
   109	      Datadog::Tracing.trace("feed.query",
   110	                             span_type: "db",
   111	                             resource: "#{self.class}.#{__method__}",
   112	                             tags: { feed_class: feed.class.to_s.dasherize }) do
   113	        # Hey, why the to_a you say?  Because the
   114	        # LargeForemExperimental has already done this.  But the
   115	        # weighted strategy has not.  I also don't want to alter the
   116	        # weighted query implementation as it returns a lovely
   117	        # ActiveRecord::Relation.  So this is a compromise.
   118	        feed.default_home_feed(user_signed_in: false).to_a
   119	      end
   120	    end
   121	
   122	    def timeframe_feed
   123	      Articles::Feeds::Timeframe.call(params[:timeframe], tag: params[:tag], page: @page)
   124	    end
   125	
   126	    def latest_feed
   127	      articles = Articles::Feeds::Latest.call(tag: params[:tag], page: @page)
   128	      minimum_score = Settings::UserExperience.index_minimum_score
   129	      
   130	      if user_signed_in?
   131	        articles.where("score >= ? OR user_id = ?", minimum_score, current_user.id)
   132	      else
   133	        articles.where("score >= ?", minimum_score)
   134	      end
   135	    end
   136	
   137	    def latest_less_filtered_feed
   138	      Articles::Feeds::Latest.call(tag: params[:tag], page: @page)
   139	    end
   140	
   141	    def latest_following_feed
   142	      activity = current_user.user_activity
   143	      user_ids = activity&.alltime_users || current_user.cached_following_users_ids
   144	      organization_ids = activity&.alltime_organizations || current_user.cached_following_organizations_ids
   145	      left_scope  = Article.published.from_subforem.where(user_id: user_ids)
   146	      right_scope = Article.published.from_subforem.where(organization_id: organization_ids)
   147	
   148	      @articles = left_scope
   149	        .or(right_scope)
   150	        .where("score > -10")
   151	        .order("published_at DESC")
   152	        .page(@page)
   153	        .per(25)
   154	    end
   155	
   156	    def relevant_following_feed
   157	      activity = current_user.user_activity
   158	      user_ids = activity&.alltime_users || current_user.cached_following_users_ids
   159	      organization_ids = activity&.alltime_organizations || current_user.cached_following_organizations_ids
   160	      left_scope  = Article.published.from_subforem.where(user_id: user_ids)
   161	      right_scope = Article.published.from_subforem.where(organization_id: organization_ids)
   162	
   163	      @articles = left_scope
   164	        .or(right_scope)
   165	        .where("score > -10")
   166	        .order("hotness_score DESC")
   167	        .page(@page)
   168	        .per(25)
   169	    end

   171	    def preload_common_settings
   172	      # Preload frequently accessed settings to avoid N+1 queries from waterfall analysis
   173	      # This addresses the multiple site_configs queries we see in the performance trace
   174	      current_subforem_id = RequestStore.store[:subforem_id]
   175	      root_subforem_id = RequestStore.store[:root_subforem_id]
   176	
   177	      if current_subforem_id == root_subforem_id
   178	        # We're in root subforem - need to handle multiple subforems dynamically
   179	        # We'll cache settings for the most common subforems to avoid repeated queries
   180	        @cached_subforem_logos = {}
   181	        @cached_logo_png = Settings::General.logo_png(subforem_id: current_subforem_id)
   182	      else
   183	        # Single subforem - simple caching
   184	        @cached_logo_png = Settings::General.logo_png(subforem_id: current_subforem_id)
   185	        @cached_subforem_logo = @cached_logo_png
   186	      end
   187	    end
   188	
   189	    def preload_additional_subforem_settings
   190	      # Only needed when we're in root subforem and have multiple subforems in the feed
   191	      return unless @cached_subforem_logos && @stories.any?
   192	
   193	      # Get unique subforem IDs from the stories
   194	      unique_subforem_ids = @stories.map(&:subforem_id).uniq.compact
   195	      current_subforem_id = RequestStore.store[:subforem_id]
   196	
   197	      # Preload settings for all unique subforems (excluding current one which is already cached)
   198	      unique_subforem_ids.each do |subforem_id|
   199	        next if subforem_id == current_subforem_id
   200	
   201	        @cached_subforem_logos[subforem_id] ||= Settings::General.logo_png(subforem_id: subforem_id)
   202	      end
   203	    end
   204	  end
   205	end

FILE app/controllers/stories/tagged_articles_controller.rb
     1	module Stories
     2	  class TaggedArticlesController < ApplicationController
     3	    before_action :set_cache_control_headers, only: :index
     4	
     5	    SIGNED_OUT_RECORD_COUNT = 15
     6	
     7	    rescue_from ArgumentError, with: :bad_request
     8	
     9	    def index
    10	      @tag = Tag.find_by(name: params[:tag].downcase) || not_found
    11	
    12	      if @tag.alias_for.present?
    13	        redirect_permanently_to("/t/#{@tag.alias_for}")
    14	        return
    15	      end
    16	
    17	      @page = (params[:page] || 1).to_i
    18	
    19	      if user_signed_in?
    20	        @moderators = User.with_role(:tag_moderator, @tag)
    21	          .order(badge_achievements_count: :desc)
    22	          .select(:username, :profile_image, :id)
    23	      end
    24	
    25	      set_number_of_articles(tag: @tag)
    26	
    27	      set_stories(number_of_articles: @number_of_articles, tag: @tag, page: @page)
    28	
    29	      set_surrogate_key_header @stories.map(&:record_key), @tag.record_key
    30	      set_cache_control_headers(86400,
    31	                                stale_while_revalidate: 1000,
    32	                                stale_if_error: 86_400)
    33	    end
    34	
    35	    private
    36	
    37	    def set_number_of_articles(tag:)
    38	      @num_published_articles = if tag.requires_approval?
    39	                                  tag.articles.published.from_subforem.approved.count
    40	                                else
    41	                                  Rails.cache.fetch("#{tag.cache_key}/article-cached-tagged-count",
    42	                                                    expires_in: 72.hours) do
    43	                                    tagged_count(tag: tag)
    44	                                  end
    45	                                end
    46	
    47	      @number_of_articles = user_signed_in? ? 5 : SIGNED_OUT_RECORD_COUNT
    48	    end
    49	
    50	    # @raise [ActiveRecord::NotFound] if we don't have an "established" tag
    51	    def set_stories(number_of_articles:, page:, tag:)
    52	      stories = Articles::Feeds::Tag.call(tag.name, number_of_articles: number_of_articles, page: page)
    53	
    54	      stories = stories.approved if tag.requires_approval?
    55	
    56	      # NOTE: We want to check if there are any stories regardless of timeframe.
    57	      not_found unless established?(stories: stories, tag: tag)
    58	
    59	      # Now, apply the filter.
    60	      stories = stories_by_timeframe(stories: stories)
    61	      stories = stories.full_posts.from_subforem
    62	      @stories = stories.decorate
    63	    end
    64	
    65	    def tagged_count(tag:)
    66	      tag.articles.published.from_subforem.where(score: Settings::UserExperience.tag_feed_minimum_score..).count
    67	    end
    68	
    69	    # Do we have an established tag?  That means it's supported OR we have at least one published story.
    70	    #
    71	    # @param stories [ActiveRecord::Relation<Article>]
    72	    # @param tag [Tag]
    73	    # @return [TrueClass] if we have published stories for this tag
    74	    # @return [FalseClass] if we do not
    75	    def established?(stories:, tag:)
    76	      return true if tag.supported?
    77	      return true if stories.published.from_subforem.exists?
    78	
    79	      false
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    def stories_by_timeframe(stories:)
    83	      if Timeframe::FILTER_TIMEFRAMES.include?(params[:timeframe])
    84	        stories = stories.where("published_at > ?", Timeframe.datetime(params[:timeframe])) if params[:timeframe] != "infinity"
    85	        stories.where(score: -20..).order(score: :desc)
    86	      elsif params[:timeframe] == Timeframe::LATEST_TIMEFRAME
    87	        stories.where(score: -20..).order(published_at: :desc)
    88	      else
    89	        # For tags on page 1, constrain to the last 4 months so Postgres
    90	        # can narrow the scan before sorting by hotness_score. Supported tags always
    91	        # have plenty of recent content, so this is safe and avoids scanning the
    92	        # full history of a high-volume tag.
    93	        if @page == 1 && !user_signed_in?
    94	          stories = stories.where("published_at > ?", 3.months.ago)
    95	        end
    96	        stories.order(hotness_score: :desc).with_at_least_home_feed_minimum_score
    97	      end
    98	    end
    99	  end
   100	end

FILE app/controllers/concerns/api/articles_controller.rb
     1	module Api
     2	  module ArticlesController
     3	    extend ActiveSupport::Concern
     4	
     5	    INDEX_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION = %i[
     6	      id user_id organization_id collection_id
     7	      title description main_image published_at crossposted_at social_image
     8	      cached_tag_list slug path canonical_url comments_count
     9	      public_reactions_count created_at edited_at last_comment_at published
    10	      updated_at video_thumbnail_url reading_time subforem_id language
    11	    ].freeze
    12	
    13	    ADDITIONAL_SEARCH_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION = [
    14	      *INDEX_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION, :body_markdown
    15	    ].freeze
    16	    private_constant :ADDITIONAL_SEARCH_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION
    17	
    18	    SHOW_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION = [
    19	      *INDEX_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION, :body_markdown, :processed_html
    20	    ].freeze
    21	    private_constant :SHOW_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION
    22	
    23	    ME_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION = %i[
    24	      id user_id organization_id

    27	      page_views_count crossposted_at body_markdown updated_at reading_time
    28	    ].freeze
    29	    private_constant :ME_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION
    30	
    31	    def index
    32	      if invalid_tags_present?
    33	        render json: { error: "Not Found", status: 404 }, status: :not_found
    34	        return
    35	      end
    36	
    37	      @articles = ArticleApiIndexService.new(params).get
    38	      @articles = @articles.select(INDEX_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION).decorate
    39	
    40	      set_surrogate_key_header Article.table_key, @articles.map(&:record_key)

    42	
    43	    def show
    44	      @article = Article.published.from_subforem
    45	        .includes(user: :profile)
    46	        .select(SHOW_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION)
    47	        .find(params[:id])
    48	        .decorate
    49	
    50	      set_surrogate_key_header @article.record_key
    51	    end

    53	    def show_by_slug
    54	      @article = Article.published.from_subforem
    55	        .select(SHOW_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION)
    56	        .find_by!(path: "/#{params[:username]}/#{params[:slug]}")
    57	        .decorate

    59	      set_surrogate_key_header @article.record_key
    60	      render "show"

    62	
    63	    def create
    64	      authorize(Article)
    65	
    66	      @article = Articles::Creator.call(@user, article_params).decorate
    67	
    68	      if @article.persisted?
    69	        render "show", status: :created, location: @article.url
    70	      else
    71	        message = @article.errors_as_sentence
    72	        render json: { error: message, status: 422 }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    73	      end
    74	    end
    75	
    76	    def update
    77	      articles_relation = @user.super_admin? ? Article.includes(:user) : @user.articles
    78	      article = articles_relation.find(params[:id])
    79	
    80	      result = Articles::Updater.call(@user, article, article_params)
    81	
    82	      @article = result.article
    83	
    84	      if result.success
    85	        render "show", status: :ok
    86	      else
    87	        message = @article.errors_as_sentence
    88	        render json: { error: message, status: 422 }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    89	      end
    90	    end
    91	
    92	    def me
    93	      per_page = (params[:per_page] || 30).to_i
    94	      num = [per_page, per_page_max].min
    95	
    96	      @articles = case params[:status]
    97	                  when "published"
    98	                    @user.articles.published.from_subforem
    99	                  when "unpublished"
   100	                    @user.articles.unpublished.from_subforem
   101	                  when "all"
   102	                    @user.articles.from_subforem
   103	                  else
   104	                    @user.articles.published.from_subforem
   105	                  end
   106	
   107	      @articles = @articles
   108	        .includes(:organization)
   109	        .select(ME_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION)
   110	        .order(published_at: :desc, created_at: :desc)
   111	        .page(params[:page])
   112	        .per(num)
   113	        .decorate

   115	
   116	    def unpublish
   117	      @article = Article.find(params[:id])
   118	
   119	      authorize @article, :revoke_publication?
   120	
   121	      if Articles::Unpublish.call(@user, @article)
   122	        payload = { action: "api_article_unpublish", article_id: @article.id }
   123	        Audit::Logger.log(:admin_api, @user, payload)
   124	
   125	        render status: :no_content
   126	      else
   127	        render json: { message: @article.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_entity
   128	      end
   129	    end
   130	
   131	    def search
   132	      # I temporarily added a new search endpoint in the interest of getting the chatGPT plugin live without changing
   133	      # the existing index endpoint. There are some experiments which we want to conduct which I think makes sense on
   134	      # a new endpoint rather than an existing one. We may want to refactor the index one in the future.
   135	      @articles = Articles::ApiSearchQuery.call(params)
   136	
   137	      # This adds some inconsistency where we omit the body markdown when the response has more than 1 article because
   138	      # ChatGPT cannot process the long body request.
   139	      @articles = if @articles.count > 1
   140	                    @articles.select(INDEX_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION).decorate
   141	                  else
   142	                    @articles.select(ADDITIONAL_SEARCH_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_SERIALIZATION).decorate
   143	                  end
   144	    end
   145	
   146	    private
   147	
   148	    def invalid_tags_present?
   149	      tag_params = [params[:tag], params[:tags], params[:tags_exclude]].flatten.compact
   150	
   151	      return false if tag_params.empty?
   152	
   153	      all_tags = tag_params.flat_map { |t| t.to_s.split(",") }.map(&:strip).compact_blank
   154	
   155	      all_tags.any? { |t| !t.match?(/\A[[:alnum:]\-]+\z/i) }
   156	    end
   157	
   158	    def per_page_max
   159	      (ApplicationConfig["API_PER_PAGE_MAX"] || 1000).to_i
   160	    end
   161	
   162	    def article_params
   163	      convert_labels_param
   164	      allowed_params = [
   165	        :title, :body_markdown, :published, :series,
   166	        :main_image, :canonical_url, :description, { tags: [] },
   167	        :published_at, :subforem_id, :language
   168	      ]
   169	      allowed_params << :organization_id if params.dig("article", "organization_id") && allowed_to_change_org_id?
   170	      # allow if a youtube.com, mux.com, or twitch.tv URL
   171	      video_url = params.dig("article", "video_source_url")
   172	      if video_url.present?
   173	        youtube_pattern = /\Ahttps?:\/\/(www\.)?(youtube\.com\/watch\?v=|youtu\.be\/)/
   174	        mux_pattern = /\Ahttps?:\/\/player\.mux\.com\//
   175	        twitch_pattern = /\Ahttps?:\/\/(www\.)?twitch\.tv\/videos\//
   176	        allowed_params << :video_source_url if video_url.match?(youtube_pattern) || video_url.match?(mux_pattern) || video_url.match?(twitch_pattern)
   177	      end
   178	      if @user.super_admin?
   179	        allowed_params << :clickbait_score
   180	        allowed_params << :compellingness_score
   181	        allowed_params << { labels: [] }
   182	      end
   183	      params.require(:article).permit(allowed_params)
   184	    end
   185	
   186	    def allowed_to_change_org_id?
   187	      potential_user = @article&.user || @user
   188	      if @article.nil? || OrganizationMembership.exists?(user: potential_user,
   189	                                                         organization_id: params.dig("article", "organization_id"))
   190	        OrganizationMembership.exists?(user: potential_user,
   191	                                       organization_id: params.dig("article", "organization_id"))
   192	      elsif potential_user == @user
   193	        potential_user.org_admin?(params.dig("article", "organization_id")) ||
   194	          @user.any_admin?
   195	      end
   196	    end
   197	
   198	    def validate_article_param_is_hash
   199	      return if params.to_unsafe_h[:article].is_a?(Hash)
   200	
   201	      message = I18n.t("api.v0.articles_controller.must_be_json", type: params[:article].class.name)
   202	      render json: { error: message, status: 422 }, status: :unprocessable_entity
   203	    end
   204	
   205	    def convert_labels_param
   206	      labels = params.dig("article", "labels")
   207	      if labels.present?
   208	        labels = labels.is_a?(String) ? labels.gsub(" ", "").split(",") : labels
   209	        params[:article][:labels] = labels
   210	      end
   211	    end
   212	  end
   213	end

    39	  # Computes counts for comments, reactions, follows and page views per each

    41	  def grouped_by_day
    42	    return {} unless start_date && end_date
    43	
    44	    # The activities fast-path is itself the cache (single indexed lookup per
    45	    # article_activities row), and we deliberately leave it uncached so worker
    46	    # writes show up immediately. The raw-row fallback, however, runs whenever
    47	    # any in-scope article is missing an ArticleActivity row (i.e. mid-backfill
    48	    # for an owner with many articles), so we wrap *only* that branch in a
    49	    # short-TTL cache to avoid hammering the DB on every reload while backfill
    50	    # catches up. The TTL is short enough that newly-backfilled freshness still
    51	    # arrives within ~one minute.
    52	    fast = grouped_by_day_from_activities
    53	    return fast if fast
    54	
    55	    cache_key = [
    56	      "analytics-grouped-by-day-raw-v1",
    57	      user_or_org.class.name, user_or_org.id,
    58	      start_date, end_date, article_id
    59	    ].join("-")
    60	    Rails.cache.fetch(cache_key, expires_in: 1.minute) { grouped_by_day_from_raw }
    61	  end

    81	  # Returns top contributors who engaged with the user/org's articles
    82	  # via reactions (excl. bookmarks) and comments, excluding self-interactions.
    83	  # Comments are weighted 6x, reactions 1x.
    84	  def top_contributors(limit: 20)
    85	    return [] unless article_data.exists?
    86	
    87	    # Use subqueries to avoid loading all IDs into memory for large datasets,
    88	    # but use the direct array for single articles to prevent Postgres sequential scans
    89	    article_ids_subquery = @article_id ? [@article_id] : article_data.select(:id)
    90	
    91	    # Determine author IDs to exclude self-interactions
    92	    author_ids = @article_id ? article_data.pluck(:user_id) : article_data.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
    93	
    94	    # Reactions (excl readinglist/self) → weight 1
    95	    reactions_sql = Reaction.for_analytics
    96	      .where(reactable_id: article_ids_subquery, reactable_type: "Article")
    97	      .where.not(category: "readinglist")
    98	      .where.not(user_id: author_ids)
    99	    reactions_sql = reactions_sql.where(created_at: start_date..end_date) if start_date && end_date
   100	    reactions_query = reactions_sql
   101	      .select("user_id, COUNT(*) AS reactions_count, 0 AS comments_count, COUNT(*) AS score")
   102	      .group(:user_id)
   103	
   104	    # Comments (excl self, score > 0) → weight 6
   105	    # Comments carry more weight than reactions because they require
   106	    # significantly more effort and drive meaningful discussion.
   107	    comments_sql = Comment
   108	      .where(commentable_id: article_ids_subquery, commentable_type: "Article")
   109	      .where("score > 0")
   110	      .where.not(user_id: author_ids)
   111	    comments_sql = comments_sql.where(created_at: start_date..end_date) if start_date && end_date
   112	    comments_query = comments_sql
   113	      .select("user_id, 0 AS reactions_count, COUNT(*) AS comments_count, COUNT(*) * 6 AS score")
   114	      .group(:user_id)
   115	
   116	    # UNION and aggregate
   117	    union_sql = "(" \
   118	      "#{reactions_query.to_sql}" \
   119	      " UNION ALL " \
   120	      "#{comments_query.to_sql}" \
   121	    ")"
   122	
   123	    rows = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.select_all(
   124	      "SELECT user_id, SUM(reactions_count)::int AS reactions_count, " \
   125	      "SUM(comments_count)::int AS comments_count, SUM(score)::int AS score " \
   126	      "FROM #{union_sql} AS combined " \
   127	      "GROUP BY user_id ORDER BY score DESC, user_id ASC LIMIT #{limit.to_i}",
   128	    )

   130	    user_ids = rows.map { |r| r["user_id"] }

   151	  def follower_engagement
   152	    follower_scope = Follow

   154	      .where(blocked: false)
   155	
   156	    total_followers = follower_scope.count

   158	    return { total_followers: total_followers, engaged_followers: 0, ratio: 0.0 } unless article_data.exists?

   160	    follower_ids_subquery = follower_scope.select(:follower_id)
   161	    article_ids_subquery = @article_id ? [@article_id] : article_data.select(:id)
   162	
   163	    # Followers who reacted (excl readinglist)
   164	    reacting_scope = Reaction.for_analytics
   165	      .where(reactable_id: article_ids_subquery, reactable_type: "Article")
   166	      .where.not(category: "readinglist")

   168	    reacting_scope = reacting_scope.where(created_at: start_date..end_date) if start_date && end_date
   169	
   170	    # Followers who commented (score > 0)
   171	    commenting_scope = Comment
   172	      .where(commentable_id: article_ids_subquery, commentable_type: "Article")
   173	      .where("score > 0")
   174	      .where(user_id: follower_ids_subquery)
   175	    commenting_scope = commenting_scope.where(created_at: start_date..end_date) if start_date && end_date
   176	
   177	    engaged_count = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.select_value(
   178	      "SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FROM (" \
   179	      "#{reacting_scope.select(:user_id).to_sql} UNION ALL #{commenting_scope.select(:user_id).to_sql}" \
   180	      ") AS engaged",

   183	    {

    18	  def self.create(params, current_user:)

    20	  end
    21	
    22	  def self.toggle(params, current_user:)
    23	    new(params, current_user: current_user).toggle

    25	
    26	  def initialize(params, current_user:)
    27	    @current_user = current_user
    28	
    29	    @params = params
    30	    @reactable_id = params[:reactable_id]
    31	    @reactable_type = params[:reactable_type]
    32	    @category = params[:category] || "like"
    33	  end
    34	
    35	  attr_reader :category, :params, :current_user, :reactable_type, :reactable_id
    36	
    37	  delegate :rate_limiter, to: :current_user
    38	
    39	  def create
    40	    destroy_contradictory_mod_reactions if %w[Article Comment].include?(reactable_type)
    41	    return noop_result if existing_reaction
    42	
    43	    create_new_reaction
    44	  end
    45	
    46	  def toggle
    47	    destroy_contradictory_mod_reactions if %w[Article Comment].include?(reactable_type)
    48	    return handle_existing_reaction if existing_reaction
    49	
    50	    create_new_reaction
    51	  end
    52	
    53	  private
    54	
    55	  def destroy_contradictory_mod_reactions
    56	    return unless ReactionCategory[category].privileged?
    57	
    58	    reactions = Reaction.contradictory_mod_reactions(
    59	      category: category,
    60	      reactable_id: reactable_id,
    61	      reactable_type: reactable_type,
    62	      user: current_user,
    63	    )
    64	    return if reactions.blank?
    65	
    66	    reactions.find_each { |reaction| destroy_reaction(reaction) }
    67	  end
    68	
    69	  def destroy_reaction(reaction)
    70	    destroyed_reaction = Reaction.transaction do
    71	      locked = Reaction.lock.find_by(id: reaction.id)
    72	      next unless locked
    73	
    74	      locked.destroy
    75	      locked
    76	    end
    77	
    78	    return unless destroyed_reaction
    79	
    80	    sink_articles(destroyed_reaction)
    81	    send_notifications_without_delay(destroyed_reaction)
    82	    destroyed_reaction
    83	  end
    84	
    85	  def existing_reaction
    86	  @existing_reaction ||= Reaction.where(
    87	    user_id: current_user.id,
    88	    reactable_id: reactable_id,
    89	    reactable_type: reactable_type,
    90	    category: category,
    91	  ).first
    92	  end
    93	
    94	  def create_new_reaction
    95	    reaction = build_reaction(category)
    96	    result = result(reaction, nil)
    97	
    98	    begin
    99	      if reaction.save
   100	        rate_limit_reaction_creation
   101	        sink_articles(reaction)
   102	        send_notifications(reaction)
   103	        record_feed_event(reaction)
   104	        update_last_reacted_at(reaction)
   105	      end
   106	    rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
   107	      Rails.logger.error "Reaction already exists: #{reaction.inspect}"
   108	    end
   109	
   110	    result.action = "create"
   111	
   112	    if category == "readinglist" && current_user.setting.experience_level
   113	      rate_article(reaction)
   114	    end
   115	
   116	    if current_user.auditable?
   117	      Audit::Logger.log(:moderator, current_user, params.dup)
   118	    end
   119	
   120	    result
   121	  end
   122	
   123	  def build_reaction(category)
   124	    create_params = {
   125	      user_id: current_user.id,
   126	      reactable_id: reactable_id,
   127	      reactable_type: reactable_type,
   128	      category: category
   129	    }
   130	    if (current_user&.any_admin? || current_user&.super_moderator?) &&
   131	        ReactionCategory[category].negative?
   132	      create_params[:status] = "confirmed"
   133	    end
   134	    Reaction.new(create_params)
   135	  end
   136	
   137	  def handle_existing_reaction
   138	    locked_reaction = destroy_reaction(existing_reaction)
   139	    log_audit(locked_reaction) if locked_reaction
   140	    result(existing_reaction, "destroy")
   141	  end
   142	
   143	  def log_audit(reaction)
   144	    return unless reaction.negative? && current_user.auditable?
   145	
   146	    updated_params = params.dup
   147	    updated_params[:action] = "destroy"
   148	    Audit::Logger.log(:moderator, current_user, updated_params)
   149	  end
   150	
   151	  def result(reaction, action)
   152	    Result.new category: category, reaction: reaction, action: action
   153	  end
   154	
   155	  def rate_limit_reaction_creation
   156	    rate_limiter.track_limit_by_action(:reaction_creation)
   157	  end
   158	
   159	  def sink_articles(reaction)
   160	    Moderator::SinkArticles.call(reaction.reactable_id) if vomit_on_user?
   161	  end
   162	
   163	  def send_notifications(reaction)
   164	    Notification.send_reaction_notification(reaction, reaction.target_user)
   165	    return unless reaction.reaction_on_organization_article?
   166	
   167	    Notification.send_reaction_notification(reaction, reaction.reactable.organization)
   168	  end
   169	
   170	  def send_notifications_without_delay(reaction)
   171	    Notification.send_reaction_notification_without_delay(reaction, reaction.target_user)
   172	    return unless reaction.reaction_on_organization_article?
   173	
   174	    Notification.send_reaction_notification_without_delay(reaction, reaction.reactable.organization)
   175	  end
   176	
   177	  def record_feed_event(reaction)
   178	    return unless (reaction.visible_to_public? || reaction.category == "readinglist") &&
   179	      reaction.reactable_type == "Article"
   180	
   181	    FeedEvent.record_journey_for(reaction.user, article: reaction.reactable, category: :reaction)
   182	  end
   183	
   184	  def rate_article(reaction)
   185	    user_experience_level = current_user.setting.experience_level
   186	    return unless user_experience_level
   187	
   188	    RatingVote.create(article_id: reaction.reactable_id,
   189	                      group: "experience_level",
   190	                      user_id: current_user.id,
   191	                      context: "readinglist_reaction",
   192	                      rating: user_experience_level)
   193	  end
   194	
   195	  def noop_result
   196	    Result.new category: category, action: "none", reaction: existing_reaction

   198

   200	    reactable_type == "User" && category == "vomit"

   202

FILE app/services/comments/calculate_score.rb
     1	module Comments
     2	  class CalculateScore
     3	    def self.call(...)
     4	      new(...).call
     5	    end
     6	
     7	    def initialize(comment)
     8	      @comment = comment
     9	    end
    10	
    11	    def call
    12	      score = BlackBox.comment_quality_score(comment)
    13	      score -= 500 if comment.user&.spam?
    14	      score += Settings::UserExperience.index_minimum_score if comment.user&.base_subscriber?
    15	      previous_score = comment.score.to_i
    16	      comment.update_columns(score: score, updated_at: Time.current)
    17	
    18	      if score >= 3 && comment.respond_to?(:semantic_embedding) && comment.semantic_embedding.nil?
    19	        Comments::GenerateEmbeddingWorker.perform_async(comment.id)
    20	      end
    21	
    22	      enqueue_article_activity_update(previous_score, score) if comment.commentable_type == "Article"
    23	
    24	      comment.user&.touch(:last_comment_at)
    25	
    26	      # update commentable
    27	      commentable = comment.commentable
    28	
    29	      commentable.touch(:last_comment_at) if commentable.respond_to?(:last_comment_at)
    30	      Comments::Count.call(commentable, recalculate: true) if commentable.is_a?(Article)
    31	
    32	      # busting comment cache includes busting commentable cache
    33	      Comments::BustCacheWorker.new.perform(comment.id)
    34	    end
    35	
    36	    private
    37	
    38	    attr_reader :comment
    39	
    40	    # Emit an article-activity enqueue when the comment crosses the
    41	    # "counted" threshold (score > 0). update_columns above bypasses
    42	    # callbacks, so the Comment after_commit can't observe this transition.
    43	    def enqueue_article_activity_update(previous_score, current_score)
    44	      was_counted = previous_score.positive?
    45	      is_counted = current_score.to_i.positive?
    46	      return if was_counted == is_counted
    47	
    48	      action = is_counted ? "create" : "destroy"
    49	      payload = { "iso" => comment.created_at.to_date.iso8601 }
    50	      Articles::UpdateArticleActivityWorker.perform_async(
    51	        comment.commentable_id, "comment", action, payload
    52	      )
    53	    end
    54	  end
    55	end

     1	module Mentions
     2	  # This class creates mentions + associated notifications for Articles and Comments.
     3	  # This class will check to see if there are any @-mentions in the post, and will
     4	  # create the associated mentions inline if necessary.
     5	  class CreateAll
     6	    def self.call(...)
     7	      new(...).call
     8	    end
     9	
    10	    def initialize(notifiable)
    11	      @notifiable = notifiable
    12	    end
    13	
    14	    def call
    15	      mentioned_users = users_mentioned_in_text_excluding_author
    16	
    17	      delete_mentions_removed_from_notifiable_text(mentioned_users)
    18	      create_mentions_for(mentioned_users)
    19	    end
    20	
    21	    private
    22	
    23	    def users_mentioned_in_text_excluding_author
    24	      mentioned_usernames = extract_usernames_from_mentions_in_text
    25	
    26	      collect_existing_users(mentioned_usernames)
    27	        .then do |existing_mentioned_users|
    28	          reject_notifiable_author(existing_mentioned_users)
    29	        end
    30	    end
    31	
    32	    def collect_existing_users(usernames)
    33	      User.registered.where(username: usernames)
    34	    end
    35	
    36	    def create_mentions_for(users)
    37	      users.each { |user| create_mention_for(user) }
    38	    end
    39	
    40	    def extract_usernames_from_mentions_in_text
    41	      # The "mentioned-user" css is added by Html::Parser#user_link_if_exists
    42	      doc = Nokogiri::HTML(notifiable.processed_html)
    43	
    44	      # Remove any mentions that are embedded within any liquid tags
    45	      non_liquid_tag_mentions = doc.css(".mentioned-user").reject do |tag|
    46	        tag.ancestors(".liquid-comment").any? ||
    47	          tag.ancestors("[class^=ltag]").any? ||
    48	          tag.ancestors("[class*=' ltag']").any?
    49	      end
    50	
    51	      non_liquid_tag_mentions.map { |link| link.text.delete("@").downcase }
    52	    end
    53	
    54	    def reject_notifiable_author(users)
    55	      users.reject { |user| authored_by?(user, notifiable) }
    56	    end
    57	
    58	    def authored_by?(user, notifiable)
    59	      user.id == notifiable.user_id
    60	    end
    61	
    62	    def delete_mentions_removed_from_notifiable_text(users)
    63	      mentions = notifiable.mentions.where.not(user_id: users).destroy_all
    64	      Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: mentions.map(&:id), notifiable_type: "Mention") if mentions.present?
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    def user_has_comment_notifications?(user)
    68	      user.notifications.exists?(notifiable_id: notifiable.id, notifiable_type: "Comment")
    69	    end
    70	
    71	    def create_mention_for(user)
    72	      # Do not create additional notifications for being mentioned in a comment.
    73	      if notifiable.is_a?(Comment) && user_has_comment_notifications?(user)
    74	        return
    75	      end
    76	
    77	      # The mentionable_type is the model that created the mention, the user is the user to be mentioned.
    78	      mention = Mention.create(user_id: user.id, mentionable_id: notifiable.id,
    79	                               mentionable_type: notifiable.class.name)
    80	
    81	      # If notifiable is an Article, we need to create the notification for the mention immediately so
    82	      # that the notification exists in the database before we attempt to create other Article-related notifications.
    83	      # However, if notifiable is a Comment, we can create the notification for the mention in the background.
    84	      case notifiable
    85	      when Article
    86	        Notification.send_mention_notification_without_delay(mention)
    87	      when Comment
    88	        Notification.send_mention_notification(mention)
    89	      end
    90	
    91	      mention
    92	    end
    93	
    94	    attr_reader :notifiable
    95	  end
    96	end

    14	    def call

    16

    18	        notifiable_id: notifiable.id,
    19	        notifiable_type: notifiable.class.name,
    20	        action: action,
    21	      )
    22	      # as we only select the first notification right after, there is no need
    23	      # to load all of them in memory with `.blank?`, thus we choose `.none?`
    24	      return if notifications.none?

    28	      new_json_data[:user] = user_data(notifiable.user)
    29	      add_organization_data = notifiable.is_a?(Article) && notifiable.organization
    30	      new_json_data[:organization] = organization_data(notifiable.organization) if add_organization_data
    31	
    32	      notifications.update_all(json_data: new_json_data)
