FILE app/services/notifications/milestone/send.rb
     1	module Notifications
     2	  module Milestone
     3	    ARTICLE_FINAL_PUBLICATION_TIME_FOR_MILESTONE = Time.zone.local(2019, 2, 25)
     4	
     5	    class Send
     6	      def self.call(...)
     7	        new(...).call
     8	      end
     9	
    10	      # @param type [String] - "View" or "Reaction"
    11	      # @param article [Object] - ActiveRecord Article object
    12	      def initialize(type, article)
    13	        @type = type
    14	        @article = article
    15	        @next_milestone = next_milestone
    16	      end
    17	
    18	      def call
    19	        return unless should_send_milestone?
    20	
    21	        create_notification(user_id: article.user_id)
    22	        return unless article.organization_id
    23	
    24	        create_notification(organization_id: article.organization_id)
    25	      end
    26	
    27	      private
    28	
    29	      attr_reader :type, :article
    30	
    31	      def create_notification(hash_id)
    32	        Notification.create!({
    33	          notifiable_id: article.id,
    34	          notifiable_type: "Article",
    35	          json_data: json_data,
    36	          action: "Milestone::#{type}::#{@next_milestone}"
    37	        }.merge(hash_id))
    38	      end
    39	
    40	      def json_data
    41	        gif_id = Constants::RandomGifs::IDS.sample
    42	        { article: Notifications.article_data(article), gif_id: gif_id }
    43	      end
    44	
    45	      def article_published_behind_time?
    46	        article.published_at < ARTICLE_FINAL_PUBLICATION_TIME_FOR_MILESTONE
    47	      end
    48	
    49	      def should_send_milestone?
    50	        return if article_published_behind_time?
    51	
    52	        last_milestone_notification = Notification.find_by(
    53	          user_id: article.user_id,
    54	          notifiable_type: "Article",
    55	          notifiable_id: article.id,
    56	          action: "Milestone::#{type}::#{@next_milestone}",
    57	        )
    58	
    59	        case type
    60	        when "View"
    61	          last_milestone_notification.blank? && article.page_views_count > @next_milestone
    62	        when "Reaction"
    63	          last_milestone_notification.blank? && article.public_reactions_count > @next_milestone
    64	        end
    65	      end
    66	
    67	      def next_milestone
    68	        case type
    69	        when "View"
    70	          milestones = [1024, 2048, 4096, 8192, 16_384, 32_768, 65_536, 131_072, 262_144, 524_288, 1_048_576]
    71	          milestone_count = article.page_views_count
    72	        when "Reaction"
    73	          milestones = [64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096, 8192]
    74	          milestone_count = article.public_reactions_count
    75	        end
    76	
    77	        closest_number = milestones.min_by { |num| (milestone_count - num).abs }
    78	        if milestone_count > closest_number
    79	          closest_number
    80	        else
    81	          milestones[milestones.index(closest_number) - 1]
    82	        end
    83	      end
    84	    end
    85	  end
    86	end

FILE app/services/notifications/moderation/send.rb
     1	# Send notifications from moderation
     2	module Notifications
     3	  module Moderation
     4	    MODERATORS_AVAILABILITY_DELAY = 22.hours
     5	    class Send
     6	      SUPPORTED = [Comment, Article].freeze
     7	
     8	      def self.call(...)
     9	        new(...).call
    10	      end
    11	
    12	      def initialize(moderator, notifiable)
    13	        @moderator = moderator
    14	        @notifiable = notifiable
    15	      end
    16	
    17	      delegate :user_data, :comment_data, :article_data, to: Notifications
    18	
    19	      def call
    20	        return unless notifiable_supported?(notifiable)
    21	
    22	        # do not create the notification if the comment/article was created by
    23	        # the moderator of the user has the `limited` role
    24	        return if notifiable.user.limited? || moderator == notifiable.user
    25	
    26	        json_data = { user: user_data(User.staff_account) }
    27	        notifiable_name = notifiable.class.name.downcase
    28	        json_data[notifiable_name] = public_send "#{notifiable_name}_data", notifiable
    29	        json_data["#{notifiable_name}_user"] = user_data(notifiable.user)
    30	        new_notification = Notification.create!(
    31	          user_id: moderator.id,
    32	          notifiable_id: notifiable.id,
    33	          notifiable_type: notifiable.class.name,
    34	          action: "Moderation",
    35	          json_data: json_data,
    36	        )
    37	        moderator.update_column(:last_moderation_notification, Time.current)
    38	        new_notification
    39	      end
    40	
    41	      private
    42	
    43	      attr_reader :notifiable, :moderator
    44	
    45	      def notifiable_supported?(notifiable)
    46	        SUPPORTED.include?(notifiable.class)
    47	      end
    48	    end
    49	  end
    50	end

FILE app/services/notifications/new_comment/send.rb
     1	# send notifications about the new comment
     2	
     3	module Notifications
     4	  module NewComment
     5	    class Send
     6	      include ActionView::Helpers::TextHelper
     7	
     8	      def self.call(...)
     9	        new(...).call
    10	      end
    11	
    12	      def initialize(comment)
    13	        @comment = comment
    14	      end
    15	
    16	      delegate :user_data, :comment_data, to: Notifications
    17	
    18	      def call
    19	        return if comment.score.negative?
    20	
    21	        user_ids = Set.new(comment_user_ids + subscribed_user_ids + top_level_user_ids + author_subscriber_user_ids)
    22	        user_ids.delete(comment.user_id)
    23	
    24	        json_data = {
    25	          user: user_data(comment.user),
    26	          comment: comment_data(comment)
    27	        }
    28	
    29	        user_ids.each do |user_id|
    30	          Notification.create(
    31	            user_id: user_id,
    32	            notifiable_id: comment.id,
    33	            notifiable_type: comment.class.name,
    34	            subforem_id: comment.commentable.subforem_id,
    35	            action: nil,
    36	            json_data: json_data,
    37	          )
    38	        end
    39	
    40	        # Send PNs using Rpush - respecting users' notification delivery settings
    41	        targets = User.joins(:notification_setting)
    42	          .where(id: user_ids, notification_setting: { mobile_comment_notifications: true }).ids
    43	
    44	        PushNotifications::Send.call(
    45	          user_ids: targets,
    46	          title: I18n.t("services.notifications.new_comment.new"),
    47	          body: "#{I18n.t('views.notifications.comment.commented_html', user: comment.user.username,
    48	                                                                        title: comment.commentable.title.strip)}:\n" \
    49	                "#{strip_tags(comment.processed_html).strip}",
    50	          payload: {
    51	            url: URL.url(comment.path),
    52	            type: "new comment"
    53	          },
    54	        )
    55	
    56	        return unless comment.commentable.organization_id
    57	
    58	        Notification.create(
    59	          organization_id: comment.commentable.organization_id,
    60	          notifiable_id: comment.id,
    61	          notifiable_type: comment.class.name,
    62	          action: nil,
    63	          json_data: json_data,
    64	        )
    65	        # no push notifications for organizations yet
    66	      end
    67	
    68	      private
    69	
    70	      attr_reader :comment
    71	
    72	      def user_ids_for(config_name)
    73	        NotificationSubscription
    74	          .where(notifiable_id: comment.commentable_id, notifiable_type: "Article", config: config_name)
    75	          .pluck(:user_id)
    76	      end
    77	
    78	      def comment_user_ids
    79	        comment.ancestors.where(receive_notifications: true).pluck(:user_id)
    80	      end
    81	
    82	      def subscribed_user_ids
    83	        user_ids_for("all_comments")
    84	      end
    85	
    86	      def top_level_user_ids
    87	        return [] if comment.ancestry.present?
    88	
    89	        user_ids_for("top_level_comments")
    90	      end
    91	
    92	      def author_subscriber_user_ids
    93	        return [] if comment.user_id != comment.commentable.user_id
    94	
    95	        user_ids_for("only_author_comments")
    96	      end
    97	    end
    98	  end
    99	end

FILE app/services/notifications/new_follower/follow_data.rb
     1	module Notifications
     2	  module NewFollower
     3	    # A light-weight(ish) data structure for passing between systems (e.g. from application to
     4	    # background jobs).
     5	    class FollowData
     6	      class DataError < RuntimeError; end
     7	
     8	      include ActiveModel::Model
     9	      include ActiveModel::Validations
    10	
    11	      attr_accessor :followable_id, :followable_type, :follower_id
    12	
    13	      validates :followable_id, numericality: { only_integer: true }
    14	      validates :followable_type, inclusion: { in: %w[User Organization] }
    15	      validates :follower_id, numericality: { only_integer: true }
    16	
    17	      # Coerce the given :object into a Notifications::NewFollower::FollowData
    18	      #
    19	      # @param object [Notifications::NewFollower::FollowData, Follow, #symbolize_keys] what we will
    20	      #        attempt to coerce.
    21	      #
    22	      # @return Notifications::NewFollower::FollowData
    23	      # @raise Notifications::NewFollower::FollowData::DataError when given invalid data.
    24	      # @raise NoMethodError if we attempt to symbolize_keys an object.
    25	      def self.coerce(object)
    26	        case object
    27	        when Notifications::NewFollower::FollowData
    28	          object
    29	        when Follow
    30	          new(
    31	            followable_id: object.followable_id,
    32	            followable_type: object.followable_type,
    33	            follower_id: object.follower_id,
    34	          )
    35	        else
    36	          new(object.symbolize_keys)
    37	        end
    38	      end
    39	
    40	      def initialize(attributes)
    41	        super
    42	        raise DataError unless valid?
    43	      end
    44	
    45	      def to_h
    46	        {
    47	          "followable_id" => followable_id,
    48	          "followable_type" => followable_type,
    49	          "follower_id" => follower_id
    50	        }
    51	      end
    52	    end
    53	  end
    54	end

FILE app/services/notifications/new_follower/send.rb
     1	# send notifications about the new followers
     2	module Notifications
     3	  module NewFollower
     4	    class Send
     5	      def self.call(...)
     6	        new(...).call
     7	      end
     8	
     9	      # @param follow_data [Hash]
    10	      #   * :followable_id [Integer]
    11	      #   * :followable_type [String] - "User" or "Organization"
    12	      #   * :follower_id [Integer] - user id
    13	      def initialize(follow_data, is_read: false)
    14	        follow_data = FollowData.coerce(follow_data)
    15	        @followable_id = follow_data.followable_id # fetch(:followable_id)
    16	        @followable_type = follow_data.followable_type # fetch(:followable_type)
    17	        @follower_id = follow_data.follower_id # fetch(:follower_id)
    18	        @is_read = is_read
    19	      end
    20	
    21	      delegate :user_data, to: Notifications
    22	
    23	      def call
    24	        recent_follows = Follow.non_suspended(followable_type, followable_id)
    25	          .where("follows.created_at > ?", 24.hours.ago).order(created_at: :desc)
    26	
    27	        notification_params = { action: "Follow" }
    28	        case followable_type
    29	        when "User"
    30	          notification_params[:user_id] = followable_id
    31	        when "Organization"
    32	          notification_params[:organization_id] = followable_id
    33	        end
    34	        followers = User.where(id: recent_follows.map(&:follower_id))
    35	        aggregated_siblings = followers.map { |follower| user_data(follower) }
    36	        if aggregated_siblings.empty?
    37	          notification = Notification.find_by(notification_params)&.destroy
    38	        else
    39	          json_data = { user: user_data(follower), aggregated_siblings: aggregated_siblings }
    40	          notification = Notification.find_or_initialize_by(notification_params)
    41	
    42	          # we explicitly load the correct follow, to avoid incurring in the possibility of
    43	          # two different follows created at the same time
    44	          notifiable_follow = recent_follows.detect { |f| f.follower_id == @follower_id }
    45	
    46	          # if this method is called after a ".stop_following" the follower_id won't be
    47	          # present, hence, we load the most recent
    48	          notifiable_follow ||= recent_follows.first
    49	
    50	          notification.notifiable_id = notifiable_follow.id
    51	          notification.notifiable_type = "Follow"
    52	
    53	          notification.json_data = json_data
    54	          notification.notified_at = Time.current
    55	          notification.read = is_read
    56	          notification.save!
    57	        end
    58	        notification
    59	      end
    60	
    61	      private
    62	
    63	      attr_reader :followable_id, :followable_type, :follower_id, :is_read
    64	
    65	      def follower
    66	        User.find(follower_id)
    67	      end
    68	    end
    69	  end
    70	end

     4	    FOLLOWER_SEND_LIMIT = 10_000
     5	    class Send

    10	      # @param notifiable [Article]
    11	      # @param action [String] for now only "Published"
    12	      def initialize(notifiable, action = nil)
    13	        @notifiable = notifiable
    14	        @action = action
    15	      end
    16	
    17	      delegate :user_data, :article_data, :organization_data, to: Notifications
    18	
    19	      def call
    20	        return unless notifiable.is_a?(Article)
    21	
    22	        json_data = {
    23	          user: user_data(notifiable.user),
    24	          article: article_data(notifiable)
    25	        }
    26	        json_data[:organization] = organization_data(notifiable.organization) if notifiable.organization_id
    27	
    28	        notifications_attributes = []
    29	
    30	        # If a user was mentioned in the article, they will have already received a mention.
    31	        # We explicitly need to exclude them from the article_followers array if they already
    32	        # have a mention in order to avoid sending a user multiple notifications for one article.
    33	        user_ids_with_article_mentions = notifiable.mentions&.pluck(:user_id)
    34	
    35	        article_followers = User.joins("INNER JOIN follows ON follows.follower_id = users.id")
    36	          .where("(follows.followable_id = ? AND follows.followable_type = ?)
    37	                 OR (follows.followable_id = ? AND follows.followable_type = ?)",
    38	                 notifiable&.user&.id, "User", notifiable&.organization&.id, "Organization")
    39	          .where(follows: { subscription_status: "all_articles" })
    40	          .where.not(id: (user_ids_with_article_mentions + [notifiable.user]))
    41	          .recently_active(FOLLOWER_SEND_LIMIT).distinct
    42	
    43	        article_followers.find_each do |follower|
    44	          now = Time.current
    45	          notifications_attributes.push(
    46	            user_id: follower.id,
    47	            notifiable_id: notifiable.id,
    48	            notifiable_type: notifiable.class.name,
    49	            subforem_id: notifiable.subforem_id,
    50	            action: action,
    51	            json_data: json_data,
    52	            created_at: now,
    53	            notified_at: now,
    54	            updated_at: now,
    55	          )
    56	        end
    57	
    58	        return if notifications_attributes.blank?
    59	
    60	        upsert_index = choose_upsert_index(action)
    61	
    62	        context_notification_attributes = {
    63	          context_id: notifiable.id,
    64	          context_type: notifiable.class.name,
    65	          action: action
    66	        }
    67	
    68	        ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
    69	          Notification.upsert_all(
    70	            notifications_attributes,
    71	            unique_by: upsert_index,
    72	            returning: %i[id],
    73	          )
    74	
    75	          ContextNotification.upsert(context_notification_attributes,
    76	                                     unique_by: :index_context_notification_on_context_and_action)
    77	        end
    78	      end
    79	
    80	      private
    81	
    82	      attr_reader :notifiable, :action
    83	
    84	      def choose_upsert_index(action)
    85	        return :index_notifications_on_user_notifiable_and_action_not_null if action.present?
    86	
    87	        :index_notifications_on_user_notifiable_action_is_null
    88	      end
    89	    end
    90	  end
    91	end

FILE app/services/notifications/reactions/reaction_data.rb
     1	module Notifications
     2	  module Reactions
     3	    # A light-weight(ish) data structure for passing between systems (e.g. from application to
     4	    # background jobs).
     5	    class ReactionData
     6	      class DataError < RuntimeError; end
     7	
     8	      include ActiveModel::Model
     9	      include ActiveModel::Validations
    10	
    11	      attr_accessor :reactable_id, :reactable_type, :reactable_user_id, :reactable_subforem_id
    12	
    13	      validates :reactable_id, numericality: { only_integer: true }
    14	      validates :reactable_type, inclusion: { in: %w[Article Comment] }
    15	      validates :reactable_user_id, numericality: { only_integer: true }
    16	
    17	      # Coerce the given :object into a Notifications::Reactions::ReactionData
    18	      #
    19	      # @param object [Object] what we will attempt to coerce.
    20	      #
    21	      # @return Notifications::Reactions::ReactionData
    22	      # @raise Notifications::Reactions::ReactionData::DataError when given invalid data.
    23	      # @raise NoMethodError if we attempt to symbolize_keys an object.
    24	      def self.coerce(object)
    25	        case object
    26	        when Notifications::Reactions::ReactionData
    27	          object
    28	        when Reaction
    29	          new(
    30	            reactable_id: object.reactable_id,
    31	            reactable_type: object.reactable_type,
    32	            reactable_user_id: object.reactable.user_id,
    33	            reactable_subforem_id: object.reactable.subforem_id
    34	          )
    35	        else
    36	          new(object.symbolize_keys)
    37	        end
    38	      end
    39	
    40	      def initialize(attributes)
    41	        super
    42	        raise DataError unless valid?
    43	      end
    44	
    45	      def to_h
    46	        {
    47	          "reactable_id" => reactable_id,
    48	          "reactable_type" => reactable_type,
    49	          "reactable_user_id" => reactable_user_id,
    50	          "reactable_subforem_id" => reactable_subforem_id
    51	        }
    52	      end
    53	    end
    54	  end
    55	end

FILE app/services/notifications/reactions/send.rb
     1	# send notifications about the new reaction
     2	module Notifications
     3	  module Reactions
     4	    class Send
     5	      Response = Struct.new(:action, :notification_id)
     6	
     7	      def self.call(...)
     8	        new(...).call
     9	      end
    10	
    11	      # @param reaction_data [Hash]
    12	      #   * :reactable_id [Integer] - article or comment id
    13	      #   * :reactable_type [String] - "Article" or "Comment"
    14	      #   * :reactable_user_id [Integer] - user id
    15	      #   * :reactable_subforem_id [Integer] - subforem id
    16	      # @param receiver [User] or [Organization]
    17	      def initialize(reaction_data, receiver)
    18	        @reaction = ReactionData.coerce(reaction_data)
    19	        @receiver = receiver
    20	      end
    21	
    22	      delegate :user_data, to: Notifications
    23	
    24	      # @return [OpenStruct, #action, #notification_id]
    25	      def call
    26	        return unless receiver.is_a?(User) || receiver.is_a?(Organization)
    27	
    28	        reaction_siblings = Reaction.public_category.where(reactable_id: reaction.reactable_id,
    29	                                                           reactable_type: reaction.reactable_type)
    30	          .where.not(reactions: { user_id: reaction.reactable_user_id })
    31	          .preload(:reactable).includes(:user).where.not(users: { id: nil })
    32	          .order("reactions.created_at" => :desc)
    33	
    34	        aggregated_reaction_siblings = reaction_siblings.map do |reaction|
    35	          { category: reaction.category, created_at: reaction.created_at, user: user_data(reaction.user) }
    36	        end
    37	
    38	        notification_params = {
    39	          notifiable_type: reaction.reactable_type,
    40	          notifiable_id: reaction.reactable_id,
    41	          subforem_id: reaction.reactable_subforem_id,
    42	          action: "Reaction"
    43	        }
    44	        case receiver
    45	        when User
    46	          notification_params[:user_id] = receiver.id
    47	        when Organization
    48	          notification_params[:organization_id] = receiver.id
    49	        end
    50	
    51	        if aggregated_reaction_siblings.empty?
    52	          Notification.where(notification_params).delete_all
    53	          Response.new(:deleted)
    54	        else
    55	          recent_reaction = reaction_siblings.first
    56	
    57	          json_data = reaction_json_data(recent_reaction, aggregated_reaction_siblings)
    58	
    59	          notification = Notification.find_or_initialize_by(notification_params)
    60	
    61	          previous_siblings_size = notification.json_data&.dig("reaction", "aggregated_siblings")&.size || 0
    62	
    63	          notification.json_data = json_data
    64	          notification.notified_at = Time.current
    65	          notification.read = false if json_data[:reaction][:aggregated_siblings].size > previous_siblings_size
    66	
    67	          notification_id = save_notification(notification_params, notification)
    68	
    69	          Response.new(:saved, notification_id)
    70	        end
    71	      end
    72	
    73	      private
    74	
    75	      attr_reader :reaction, :receiver
    76	
    77	      # when a notification exists in the DB already it's safe to just save it,
    78	      # when it doesn't, there could be a race condition when 2 jobs try to create
    79	      # duplicate notifications concurrently, in this case upsert is used to
    80	      # rely on PostgreSQL constraints, thus we use `.upsert`
    81	      def save_notification(params, notification)
    82	        if notification.persisted?
    83	          notification.save!
    84	          notification.id
    85	        else
    86	          # in the upsert, we are only interested in updating the following columns:
    87	          # json_data, notified_at, read and the notification params used by `.find_or_initialize_by`
    88	          upsert_columns = %w[json_data notified_at read]
    89	          upsert_attributes = notification.attributes.select { |k| upsert_columns.include?(k) }
    90	          upsert_attributes.merge!(params)
    91	
    92	          # unfortunately Rails requires the timestamps to be present even if unused in case of upsert
    93	          # see <https://github.com/rails/rails/issues/35493>
    94	          now = Time.current
    95	          upsert_attributes["created_at"] = upsert_attributes["updated_at"] = now
    96	
    97	          # we also need to select the correct index to let PostgreSQL know
    98	          # how to determine conflict on rows
    99	          upsert_index = choose_upsert_index(notification)
   100	
   101	          upsert_result = Notification.upsert(
   102	            upsert_attributes,
   103	            unique_by: upsert_index,
   104	            returning: %i[id],
   105	          )
   106	
   107	          upsert_result.to_a.first["id"]
   108	        end
   109	      end
   110	
   111	      def choose_upsert_index(notification)
   112	        # if none of these is present, we use the two columns for the upsert
   113	        return %i[notifiable_id notifiable_type] unless
   114	          notification.action? || notification.user_id? || notification.organization_id?
   115	
   116	        if notification.action?
   117	          return :index_notifications_on_user_notifiable_and_action_not_null if notification.user_id?
   118	
   119	          :index_notifications_on_org_notifiable_and_action_not_null
   120	        else
   121	          return :index_notifications_on_user_notifiable_action_is_null if notification.user_id?
   122	
   123	          :index_notifications_on_org_notifiable_action_is_null
   124	        end
   125	      end
   126	
   127	      def reaction_json_data(recent_reaction, siblings)
   128	        {
   129	          user: user_data(recent_reaction.user),
   130	          reaction: {
   131	            category: recent_reaction.category,
   132	            reactable_type: recent_reaction.reactable_type,
   133	            reactable_id: recent_reaction.reactable_id,
   134	            reactable: {
   135	              path: recent_reaction.reactable.path,
   136	              title: recent_reaction.reactable.title,
   137	              class: {
   138	                name: recent_reaction.reactable.class.name
   139	              }
   140	            },
   141	            aggregated_siblings: siblings,
   142	            updated_at: recent_reaction.updated_at
   143	          }
   144	        }
   145	      end
   146	    end
   147	  end
   148	end

    18	      user.remove_from_mailchimp_newsletters if user.email?
    19	      remove_profile_info
    20	      handle_user_status("Suspended", I18n.t("services.moderator.banish_user.spam_account"))

    33	      new_name = "spam_#{rand(1_000_000)}"
    34	      new_username = "spam_#{rand(1_000_000)}"
    35	      if User.find_by(name: new_name) || User.find_by(username: new_username)
    36	        new_name = "spam_#{rand(1_000_000)}"
    37	        new_username = "spam_#{rand(1_000_000)}"
    38	      end
    39	      user.update_columns(name: new_name, username: new_username, old_username: user.username,
    40	                          profile_updated_at: Time.current)

    60	        content: content || "#{@admin.username} updated #{@user.username}",
    61	      )
    62	    end
    63	
    64	    # rubocop:disable Metrics/CyclomaticComplexity
    65	    def handle_user_status(role, note)
    66	      case role
    67	      when "Admin"
    68	        assign_elevated_role_to_user(user, :admin)
    69	        TagModerators::AddTrustedRole.call(user)
    70	      when "Comment Suspended"
    71	        comment_suspended
    72	      when "Limited"
    73	        limited
    74	      when "Suspended" || "Spammer"
    75	        user.add_role(:suspended)
    76	        remove_privileges
    77	      when "Spam"
    78	        user.add_role(:spam)
    79	        remove_privileges
    80	        remove_notifications
    81	        resolve_spam_reports
    82	        confirm_flag_reactions
    83	        user.profile.touch
    84	      when "Super Moderator"
    85	        assign_elevated_role_to_user(user, :super_moderator)
    86	        TagModerators::AddTrustedRole.call(user)
    87	      when "Good standing"
    88	        regular_member
    89	      when /^(Resource Admin: )/
    90	        check_super_admin
    91	        remove_negative_roles
    92	        user.add_role(:single_resource_admin, role.split("Resource Admin: ").last.safe_constantize)
    93	      when "Super Admin"
    94	        assign_elevated_role_to_user(user, :super_admin)
    95	        TagModerators::AddTrustedRole.call(user)
    96	      when "Tech Admin"
    97	        assign_elevated_role_to_user(user, :tech_admin)
    98	        # DataUpdateScripts falls under the admin namespace
    99	        # and hence requires a single_resource_admin role to view
   100	        # this technical admin resource
   101	        user.add_role(:single_resource_admin, DataUpdateScript)
   102	      when "Trusted"
   103	        remove_negative_roles
   104	        TagModerators::AddTrustedRole.call(user)
   105	      when "Warned"
   106	        warned
   107	      when "Base Subscriber"
   108	        base_subscriber
   109	      end
   110	      create_note(role, note)
   111	
   112	      user.articles.published.find_each(&:async_score_calc)
   113	      user.comments.find_each(&:calculate_score)
   114	    end
   115	    # rubocop:enable Metrics/CyclomaticComplexity
   116	
   117	    def assign_elevated_role_to_user(user, role)
   118	      check_super_admin
   119	      remove_negative_roles
   120	      user.add_role(role)
   121	
   122	      # Clear cache key if the elevated role matches Rack::Attack bypass roles
   123	      return unless Rack::Attack::ADMIN_ROLES.include?(role.to_s)
   124	
   125	      Rails.cache.delete(Rack::Attack::ADMIN_API_CACHE_KEY)
   126	    end
   127	
   128	    def check_super_admin
   129	      raise I18n.t("services.moderator.manage_activity_and_roles.need_super") unless @admin.super_admin?
   130	    end
   131	
   132	    def comment_suspended
   133	      user.add_role(:comment_suspended)
   134	      user.remove_role(:suspended)
   135	      remove_privileges
   136	    end
   137	
   138	    def limited
   139	      user.add_role(:limited)
   140	      remove_privileges
   141	    end
   142	
   143	    def regular_member
   144	      remove_negative_roles
   145	      remove_mod_roles
   146	    end
   147	
   148	    def warned
   149	      user.add_role(:warned)
   150	      user.remove_role(:suspended) if user.suspended?
   151	      user.remove_role(:spam) if user.spam?
   152	      remove_privileges
   153	    end
   154	
   155	    def base_subscriber
   156	      user.add_role(:base_subscriber)
   157	      user.touch
   158	      user.profile&.touch
   159	      NotifyMailer.with(user: user).base_subscriber_role_email.deliver_now
   160	    end
   161	
   162	    def remove_negative_roles
   163	      user.remove_role(:limited) if user.limited?
   164	      user.remove_role(:suspended) if user.suspended?
   165	      user.remove_role(:spam) if user.spam?
   166	      user.remove_role(:warned) if user.warned?
   167	      user.remove_role(:comment_suspended) if user.comment_suspended?
   168	    end
   169	
   170	    def update_roles
   171	      handle_user_status(user_params[:user_status], user_params[:note_for_current_role])
   172	    end
   173	
   174	    private
   175	
   176	    def resolve_spam_reports
   177	      Users::ResolveSpamReportsWorker.perform_async(user.id)
   178	    end
   179	
   180	    def confirm_flag_reactions
   181	      Users::ConfirmFlagReactionsWorker.perform_async(user.id)
   182	    end
   183	  end
   184	end

     1	module Moderator
     2	  class MergeUser < ManageActivityAndRoles
     3	    def self.call(admin:, keep_user:, delete_user_id:)
     4	      new(keep_user: keep_user, admin: admin, delete_user_id: delete_user_id).merge
     5	    end
     6	
     7	    attr_reader :keep_user, :admin, :delete_user_id
     8	
     9	    def initialize(admin:, keep_user:, delete_user_id:)
    10	      @keep_user = keep_user
    11	      @admin = admin
    12	      @delete_user = User.find(delete_user_id.to_i)
    13	    end
    14	
    15	    def merge
    16	      raise StandardError, I18n.t("services.moderator.merge_user.same_user") if @delete_user.id == @keep_user.id
    17	
    18	      handle_identities
    19	      merge_content
    20	      merge_follows
    21	      merge_mentions
    22	      merge_profile
    23	      update_social
    24	      Users::DeleteWorker.new.perform(@delete_user.id, true)
    25	      @keep_user.touch(:profile_updated_at)
    26	      Users::MergeSyncWorker.perform_async(@keep_user.id)
    27	
    28	      EdgeCache::Bust.call("/#{@keep_user.username}")
    29	    end
    30	
    31	    private
    32	
    33	    def handle_identities
    34	      raise StandardError, I18n.t("services.moderator.merge_user.multiple") if @delete_user.identities.count >= 2
    35	      raise StandardError, I18n.t("services.moderator.merge_user.duplicate") if
    36	        (@delete_user.identities.pluck(:provider) & @keep_user.identities.pluck(:provider)).any?
    37	
    38	      return true if @delete_user.identities.none?
    39	
    40	      @delete_user.identities.update_all(user_id: @keep_user.id)
    41	    end
    42	
    43	    def update_social
    44	      @old_tu = @delete_user.twitter_username
    45	      @old_gu = @delete_user.github_username
    46	      ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
    47	        @delete_user.update_columns(twitter_username: nil, github_username: nil)
    48	        @keep_user.update_columns(twitter_username: @old_tu) if @keep_user.twitter_username.nil?
    49	        @keep_user.update_columns(github_username: @old_gu) if @keep_user.github_username.nil?
    50	        @keep_user.touch(:profile_updated_at, :last_followed_at) # clears cache on sidebar
    51	      end
    52	    end
    53	
    54	    def merge_profile
    55	      if @delete_user.github_repos.any?
    56	        @delete_user.github_repos.update_all(user_id: @keep_user.id)
    57	        @keep_user.touch(:github_repos_updated_at)
    58	      end
    59	      if @delete_user.badge_achievements.any?
    60	        @delete_user.badge_achievements.update_all(user_id: @keep_user.id)
    61	        BadgeAchievement.counter_culture_fix_counts(where: { users: { id: @keep_user.id } })
    62	      end
    63	
    64	      @keep_user.update_columns(created_at: @delete_user.created_at) if @delete_user.created_at < @keep_user.created_at
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    def merge_follows
    68	      @delete_user.follows&.update_all(follower_id: @keep_user.id) if @delete_user.follows.any?
    69	      @delete_user_followers = Follow.followable_user(@delete_user.id)
    70	      @delete_user_followers.update_all(followable_id: @keep_user.id) if @delete_user_followers.any?
    71	    end
    72	
    73	    def merge_mentions
    74	      @delete_user.mentions.update_all(user_id: @keep_user.id) if @delete_user.mentions.any?
    75	    end
    76	
    77	    def merge_content
    78	      merge_reactions if @delete_user.reactions.any?
    79	      merge_comments if @delete_user.comments.any?
    80	      merge_articles if @delete_user.articles.any?
    81	    end
    82	
    83	    def merge_reactions
    84	      @delete_user.reactions.update_all(user_id: @keep_user.id)
    85	      @keep_user.reactions_count = @keep_user.reactions.size
    86	    end
    87	
    88	    def merge_comments
    89	      @delete_user.comments.update_all(user_id: @keep_user.id)
    90	      @keep_user.comments_count = @keep_user.comments.size
    91	    end
    92	
    93	    def merge_articles
    94	      @delete_user.articles.update_all(user_id: @keep_user.id)
    95	      @keep_user.articles_count = @keep_user.articles.size
    96	    end
    97	  end
    98	end

FILE app/services/moderator/unpublish_all_articles.rb
     1	# Unpublish posts and delete comments w/ boolean attr (setting deleted: true) to allow revert
     2	# Create a corresponding audit_log record
     3	module Moderator
     4	  class UnpublishAllArticles
     5	    def self.call(...)
     6	      new(...).call
     7	    end
     8	
     9	    # @param target_user_id [Integer] the id of the user whose posts are being unpublished
    10	    # @param action_user_id [Integer] the id of the user who unpublishes
    11	    # @param listener [String] listener for the audit logger
    12	    def initialize(target_user_id:, action_user_id:, listener: :admin_api)
    13	      @target_user_id = target_user_id
    14	      @action_user_id = action_user_id
    15	      @listener = listener
    16	    end
    17	
    18	    delegate :user_data, to: Notifications
    19	
    20	    def call
    21	      user = User.find_by(id: target_user_id)
    22	      return unless user
    23	
    24	      target_comments = user.comments.where(deleted: false)
    25	      target_articles = user.articles.published
    26	
    27	      # cache for logging
    28	      target_comments_ids = target_comments.ids
    29	      target_articles_ids = target_articles.ids
    30	
    31	      target_comments.update(deleted: true)
    32	
    33	      target_articles.find_each do |article|
    34	        if article.has_frontmatter?
    35	          article.body_markdown.sub!(/^published:\s*true\s*$/, "published: false")
    36	        end
    37	        article.published = false
    38	        article.save(validate: false)
    39	        clean_up_notifications(article)
    40	      end
    41	
    42	      payload = {
    43	        target_user_id: target_user_id,
    44	        target_article_ids: target_articles_ids,
    45	        target_comment_ids: target_comments_ids
    46	      }
    47	
    48	      audit_log(payload)
    49	    end
    50	
    51	    private
    52	
    53	    attr_reader :target_user_id, :action_user_id, :listener
    54	
    55	    def clean_up_notifications(article)
    56	      Notification.remove_all_by_action_without_delay(notifiable_ids: article.id,
    57	                                                      notifiable_type: "Article",
    58	                                                      action: "Published")
    59	
    60	      ContextNotification.delete_by(context_id: article.id, context_type: "Article", action: "Published")
    61	
    62	      return unless article.comments.exists?
    63	
    64	      Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.comments.ids, notifiable_type: "Comment")
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    def audit_log(payload = {})
    68	      payload["action"] = listener == :moderator ? "unpublish_all_articles" : "api_user_unpublish"
    69	      action_user = User.find_by(id: action_user_id)
    70	      Audit::Logger.log(listener, action_user, payload)
    71	    end
    72	  end
    73	end

    31	    def self.more_rigorous_user_profile_spam_checking?
    32	      FeatureFlag.enabled?(:more_rigorous_user_profile_spam_checking)

    34

    40	
    41	    # Test the article for spamminess.  If it's not spammy, don't do anything.
    42	    #
    43	    # If it is spammy, escalate the situation!
    44	    #
    45	    # @param article [Article] the article to check for spamminess
    46	    # @param attributes [Array<Symbol>] test these attributes of the article.
    47	    def self.handle_article!(article:, attributes: %i[title body_markdown])
    48	      if article_linked_domain_spam?(article)
    49	        article.update_column(:automod_label, "clear_and_obvious_spam")
    50	        article.automod_label = "clear_and_obvious_spam"
    51	      else
    52	        # First, run content moderation labeling
    53	        label_article_content!(article)
    54	      end
    55	
    56	      # Handle clear and obvious violations immediately
    57	      if %w[clear_and_obvious_spam clear_and_obvious_harmful clear_and_obvious_inciting].include?(article.automod_label)
    58	        issue_spam_reaction_for!(reactable: article)
    59	
    60	        if Reaction.user_has_been_given_too_many_spammy_article_reactions?(
    61	          user: article.user,
    62	          include_user_profile: more_rigorous_user_profile_spam_checking?,
    63	        )
    64	          suspend!(user: article.user)
    65	        end
    66	
    67	        return :spam
    68	      end
    69	
    70	      # High quality content bypasses spam checks entirely
    71	      if %w[very_good_and_on_topic great_and_on_topic very_good_but_offtopic_for_subforem
    72	            great_but_off_topic_for_subforem].include?(article.automod_label)
    73	        return :not_spam
    74	      end
    75	
    76	      # For likely violations, bypass badge count and other restrictions but still run checks
    77	      bypass_restrictions = %w[likely_spam likely_harmful likely_inciting].include?(article.automod_label)
    78	
    79	      # Continue with existing spam detection logic
    80	      text = attributes.map { |attr| article.public_send(attr) }.join("\n")
    81	
    82	      # Check if we should trigger spam detection
    83	      should_check = Settings::RateLimit.trigger_spam_for?(text: text) ||
    84	        (article.processed_html.include?("<a") && Ai::Base::DEFAULT_KEY.present? &&
    85	         (bypass_restrictions || article.user.badge_achievements_count < 4) &&
    86	         Ai::ArticleCheck.new(article).spam?)
    87	
    88	      return :not_spam unless should_check
    89	
    90	      issue_spam_reaction_for!(reactable: article)
    91	
    92	      return unless Reaction.user_has_been_given_too_many_spammy_article_reactions?(
    93	        user: article.user,
    94	        include_user_profile: more_rigorous_user_profile_spam_checking?,
    95	      )
    96	
    97	      suspend!(user: article.user)
    98	    end
    99	
   100	    # Test the comment for spamminess.  If it's not spammy, don't do anything.
   101	    #
   102	    # If it is spammy, escalate the situation!
   103	    #
   104	    # @param comment [Comment] the comment to check for spamminess
   105	    def self.handle_comment!(comment:)
   106	      # Existing checks for trusted users.
   107	      return :not_spam if comment.user.badge_achievements_count > 6
   108	      return :not_spam if comment.user.base_subscriber?
   109	
   110	      if (domain = extract_first_domain_from(comment.processed_html)) && extensive_domain_spam?(domain: domain,
   111	                                                                                                current_comment: comment)
   112	        issue_spam_reaction_for!(reactable: comment)
   113	        suspend_if_user_is_repeat_offender(user: comment.user)
   114	        return :spam # Return early as it's confirmed spam.
   115	      end
   116	
   117	      rate_limit_spam = Settings::RateLimit.trigger_spam_for?(text: comment.body_markdown)
   118	
   119	      # Return if neither of the spam conditions are met.
   120	      return :not_spam unless rate_limit_spam ||
   121	        (comment.processed_html.include?("<a") && Ai::Base::DEFAULT_KEY.present? && Ai::CommentCheck.new(comment).spam?)
   122	
   123	      issue_spam_reaction_for!(reactable: comment)
   124	      suspend_if_user_is_repeat_offender(user: comment.user)
   125	    end
   126	
   127	    # Test the user for spamminess.  If it's not spammy, don't do anything.
   128	    #
   129	    # If it is spammy, escalate the situation!
   130	    #
   131	    # @param user [User] the user to check for spamminess
   132	    def self.handle_user!(user:)
   133	      text = [user.name]
   134	
   135	      if more_rigorous_user_profile_spam_checking?
   136	        text += [
   137	          user.email,
   138	          user.github_username,
   139	          user.profile&.website_url,
   140	          user.profile&.location,
   141	          user.profile&.summary,
   142	          user.twitter_username,
   143	          user.username,
   144	        ].compact
   145	      end
   146	
   147	      text = text.join("\n")
   148	
   149	      return :not_spam unless Settings::RateLimit.trigger_spam_for?(text: text)
   150	
   151	      issue_spam_reaction_for!(reactable: user)
   152	    end
   153	
   154	    # Test a user profile update for clear and obvious spam or abuse.
   155	    #
   156	    # @param user [User] the user to check for spamminess
   157	    def self.handle_profile_update!(user:)
   158	      return :skipped if user.spam_or_suspended?
   159	      return :skipped unless eligible_for_profile_spam_check?(user: user)
   160	      return :skipped unless Ai::Base::DEFAULT_KEY.present?
   161	
   162	      label = Ai::ProfileModerationLabeler.new(user).label
   163	      return :not_spam unless clear_profile_violation_label?(label)
   164	
   165	      issue_spam_reaction_for!(reactable: user)
   166	
   167	      if label == "clear_and_obvious_spam"
   168	        user.add_role(:spam)
   169	      else
   170	        suspend!(user: user)
   171	      end
   172	
   173	      :spam
   174	    end
   175	
   176	    # Suspend the given user because of too many spammy actions.
   177	    #
   178	    # @param user [User]
   179	    #
   180	    def self.suspend!(user:)
   181	      user.add_role(:suspended)
   182	
   183	      Note.create(
   184	        author_id: Settings::General.mascot_user_id,
   185	        noteable: user,
   186	        reason: "automatic_suspend",
   187	        content: I18n.t("models.comment.suspended_too_many"),
   188	      )
   189	
   190	      return unless unpublish_all_posts_when_user_auto_suspended?
   191	
   192	      user.articles.update_all(published: false)
   193	    end
   194	
   195	    # Have the mascot of this Forem react negatively to this reactable.
   196	    #
   197	    # @param reactable [ActiveRecord::Base]
   198	    def self.issue_spam_reaction_for!(reactable:)
   199	      Reaction.create(
   200	        user_id: Settings::General.mascot_user_id,
   201	        reactable: reactable,
   202	        category: "vomit",
   203	      )
   204	    end
   205	
   206	    # NEW/private: Helper method to check for extensive domain-based spam.
   207	    def self.extensive_domain_spam?(domain:, current_comment:)
   208	      # Find other comments in the last 48 hours that contain the same domain.
   209	      other_comments = Comment.where("created_at > ?", 48.hours.ago)
   210	        .where.not(id: current_comment.id)
   211	        .where("processed_html LIKE ?", "%#{ActionController::Base.helpers.sanitize(domain)}%")
   212	
   213	      # If there are more than 10 other comments, check their scores.
   214	      other_comments_count = other_comments.count
   215	      return false unless other_comments_count > 10
   216	
   217	      # Find the number of those comments with a score less than -100.
   218	      low_scoring_comments_count = other_comments.where("score < ?", -100).count
   219	
   220	      # If more than 80% are low-scoring, it's considered spam.

FILE app/services/subforem_reassignment_service.rb
     1	##
     2	# Service to handle reassigning articles to more appropriate subforems
     3	# when they are marked as offtopic for their current subforem.
     4	class SubforemReassignmentService
     5	  OFFTOPIC_LABELS = %w[
     6	    ok_but_offtopic_for_subforem
     7	    very_good_but_offtopic_for_subforem
     8	    great_but_off_topic_for_subforem
     9	  ].freeze
    10	
    11	  # Mapping from offtopic labels to their on-topic equivalents
    12	  OFFTopic_TO_ON_TOPIC_MAPPING = {
    13	    "ok_but_offtopic_for_subforem" => "okay_and_on_topic",
    14	    "very_good_but_offtopic_for_subforem" => "very_good_and_on_topic",
    15	    "great_but_off_topic_for_subforem" => "great_and_on_topic"

    19	    @article = article

    21	
    22	  ##
    23	  # Checks if the article should be reassigned to a different subforem
    24	  # and performs the reassignment if appropriate.
    25	  #
    26	  # @return [Boolean] true if reassignment occurred, false otherwise
    27	  def check_and_reassign
    28	    return false unless should_reassign?
    29	
    30	    new_subforem_id = find_appropriate_subforem
    31	    return false unless new_subforem_id
    32	
    33	    perform_reassignment(new_subforem_id)
    34	    true
    35	  end
    36	
    37	  private
    38	
    39	  attr_reader :article
    40	
    41	  ##
    42	  # Determines if the article should be reassigned based on its automod label.
    43	  #
    44	  # @return [Boolean] true if the article has an offtopic label, is not spam, and user allows reassignment
    45	  def should_reassign?
    46	    OFFTOPIC_LABELS.include?(article.automod_label) && 
    47	      !spam_label? && 
    48	      user_allows_reassignment?
    49	  end
    50	
    51	  ##
    52	  # Determines if the article has a spam-related automod label.
    53	  #
    54	  # @return [Boolean] true if the article is marked as spam
    55	  def spam_label?
    56	    %w[
    57	      clear_and_obvious_spam
    58	      likely_spam
    59	      clear_and_obvious_harmful
    60	      likely_harmful
    61	      clear_and_obvious_inciting
    62	      likely_inciting
    63	      clear_and_obvious_low_quality
    64	      likely_low_quality
    65	    ].include?(article.automod_label)
    66	  end
    67	
    68	  ##
    69	  # Checks if the user allows automatic subforem reassignment.
    70	  #
    71	  # @return [Boolean] true if the user allows reassignment, false otherwise
    72	  def user_allows_reassignment?
    73	    article.user&.setting&.disallow_subforem_reassignment != true
    74	  end
    75	
    76	  ##
    77	  # Gets the on-topic equivalent of the current offtopic automod label.
    78	  #
    79	  # @return [String, nil] the on-topic label equivalent, or nil if no mapping exists
    80	  def on_topic_equivalent_label
    81	    OFFTopic_TO_ON_TOPIC_MAPPING[article.automod_label]
    82	  end
    83	
    84	  ##
    85	  # Uses AI to find the most appropriate subforem for the article.
    86	  #
    87	  # @return [Integer, nil] the ID of the most appropriate subforem, or nil if none found
    88	  def find_appropriate_subforem
    89	    begin
    90	      finder = Ai::SubforemFinder.new(article)
    91	      finder.find_appropriate_subforem
    92	    rescue StandardError => e
    93	      Rails.logger.error("Failed to find appropriate subforem for article #{article.id}: #{e}")
    94	      nil
    95	    end
    96	  end
    97	
    98	  ##
    99	  # Performs the actual reassignment and sends notification.
   100	  #
   101	  # @param new_subforem_id [Integer] the ID of the new subforem
   102	  def perform_reassignment(new_subforem_id)
   103	    old_subforem_id = article.subforem_id
   104	    new_subforem = Subforem.find(new_subforem_id)
   105	
   106	    # Update both subforem and automod label
   107	    update_attributes = { subforem_id: new_subforem_id }
   108	    
   109	    # Update automod label to on-topic equivalent if available
   110	    if on_topic_equivalent_label
   111	      update_attributes[:automod_label] = on_topic_equivalent_label
   112	    end
   113	
   114	    article.update!(update_attributes)
   115	
   116	    # Send notification about the subforem change
   117	    send_subforem_change_notification(old_subforem_id, new_subforem_id)
   118	
   119	    Rails.logger.info("Article #{article.id} reassigned from subforem #{old_subforem_id} to #{new_subforem_id}" \
   120	                     "#{on_topic_equivalent_label ? " and automod label updated to #{on_topic_equivalent_label}" : ""}")
   121	  end
   122	
   123	  ##
   124	  # Sends a notification to the article author about the subforem change.
   125	  #
   126	  # @param old_subforem_id [Integer, nil] the ID of the previous subforem
   127	  # @param new_subforem_id [Integer] the ID of the new subforem
   128	  def send_subforem_change_notification(old_subforem_id, new_subforem_id)
   129	    Notifications::SubforemChangeNotificationWorker.perform_async(
   130	      article.id,
   131	      old_subforem_id,
   132	      new_subforem_id,
   133	    )
   134	  end
   135	end

FILE app/services/users/delete_articles.rb
     1	module Users
     2	  module DeleteArticles
     3	    module_function
     4	
     5	    def call(user)
     6	      return if user.articles.blank?
     7	
     8	      virtual_articles = user.articles.map { |article| Article.new(article.attributes) }
     9	      user.articles.find_each do |article|
    10	        article.reactions.delete_all
    11	        article.comments.includes(:user).find_each do |comment|
    12	          comment.reactions.delete_all
    13	          EdgeCache::BustComment.call(comment)
    14	          EdgeCache::BustUser.call(comment.user)
    15	          comment.delete
    16	        end
    17	        article.discussion_lock&.delete
    18	        article.context_notes.delete_all
    19	        article.article_activity&.delete
    20	        article.trend_memberships.delete_all
    21	        article.profile_pins.delete_all
    22	        article.delete
    23	        article.purge
    24	      end
    25	      virtual_articles.each do |article|
    26	        EdgeCache::BustArticle.call(article)
    27	      end
    28	    end
    29	  end
    30	end

FILE app/services/scheduled_automations/article_content_badge_awarder.rb
     1	module ScheduledAutomations
     2	  ##
     3	  # Service that awards badges to users who have posted quality articles
     4	  # based on specific criteria and keywords.
     5	  #
     6	  # This automation:
     7	  # - Searches for articles matching keywords (case-insensitive)
     8	  # - Filters by minimum indexable threshold
     9	  # - Uses AI to assess article quality based on custom criteria
    10	  # - Awards badges to qualifying users
    11	  # - Respects weekly limits for badges that allow multiple awards
    12	  #
    13	  # @example Award badges for quality articles
    14	  #   automation = ScheduledAutomation.find(1)
    15	  #   result = ScheduledAutomations::ArticleContentBadgeAwarder.call(automation)
    16	  #   puts "Awarded #{result.users_awarded} badges"
    17	  class ArticleContentBadgeAwarder
    18	    Result = Struct.new(:success?, :users_awarded, :error_message, keyword_init: true)
    19	
    20	    LOOKBACK_HOURS = 2
    21	    LOOKBACK_BUFFER = 15.minutes
    22	    MIN_DAYS_BETWEEN_AWARDS = 7
    23	
    24	    class << self
    25	      def call(automation)
    26	        new(automation).call
    27	      end
    28	    end
    29	
    30	    def initialize(automation)
    31	      @automation = automation
    32	      @badge_slug = automation.action_config["badge_slug"]
    33	      @keywords = automation.action_config["keywords"] || []
    34	      @criteria = automation.action_config["criteria"]
    35	      @lookback_hours = (automation.action_config["lookback_hours"] || LOOKBACK_HOURS).to_i
    36	
    37	      # Look back 2 hours + 15 minutes (or configured hours + buffer) from last run
    38	      # If no last run, use the configured hours + buffer from now
    39	      last_run = automation.last_run_at
    40	      @since_time = if last_run
    41	                      last_run - (@lookback_hours.hours + LOOKBACK_BUFFER)
    42	                    else
    43	                      (@lookback_hours.hours + LOOKBACK_BUFFER).ago
    44	                    end
    45	    end
    46	
    47	    def call
    48	      validate_config!
    49	
    50	      badge = Badge.find_by(slug: @badge_slug)
    51	      unless badge
    52	        return Result.new(
    53	          success?: false,
    54	          users_awarded: 0,
    55	          error_message: "Badge with slug '#{@badge_slug}' not found",
    56	        )
    57	      end
    58	
    59	      badge_id = badge.id
    60	
    61	      users_awarded = award_badges_to_qualifying_users(badge_id, badge)
    62	
    63	      Result.new(
    64	        success?: true,
    65	        users_awarded: users_awarded,
    66	        error_message: nil,
    67	      )
    68	    rescue StandardError => e
    69	      Result.new(
    70	        success?: false,
    71	        users_awarded: 0,
    72	        error_message: "#{e.class}: #{e.message}",
    73	      )
    74	    end
    75	
    76	    private
    77	
    78	    def validate_config!
    79	      raise ArgumentError, "badge_slug is required in action_config" if @badge_slug.blank?
    80	      raise ArgumentError, "criteria is required in action_config" if @criteria.blank?
    81	    end
    82	
    83	    def award_badges_to_qualifying_users(badge_id, badge)
    84	      # Find articles matching keywords and time window
    85	      candidate_articles = find_candidate_articles
    86	
    87	      return 0 if candidate_articles.empty?
    88	
    89	      users_awarded = 0
    90	      assessed_user_ids = []
    91	
    92	      candidate_articles.each do |article|
    93	        next if article.user.nil? || article.user.banished?
    94	        next if assessed_user_ids.include?(article.user_id)
    95	
    96	        # Check if user already received this badge recently (within the last week)
    97	        # Only check if badge allows multiple awards
    98	        if badge.allow_multiple_awards && recently_awarded?(article.user_id, badge_id)
    99	          assessed_user_ids << article.user_id
   100	          next
   101	        end
   102	
   103	        # Skip if badge doesn't allow multiple awards and user already has it
   104	        if !badge.allow_multiple_awards && BadgeAchievement.exists?(user_id: article.user_id, badge_id: badge_id)
   105	          assessed_user_ids << article.user_id
   106	          next
   107	        end
   108	
   109	        # Assess if the article qualifies using AI
   110	        next unless qualifies_for_badge?(article)
   111	
   112	        achievement = BadgeAchievement.create(
   113	          user_id: article.user_id,
   114	          badge_id: badge_id,
   115	          rewarding_context_message_markdown: generate_message(article),
   116	        )
   117	
   118	        next unless achievement.persisted?
   119	
   120	        article.user.touch
   121	        users_awarded += 1
   122	        assessed_user_ids << article.user_id
   123	      end
   124	
   125	      users_awarded
   126	    end
   127	
   128	    def find_candidate_articles
   129	      # Start with published articles in the time window
   130	      # We use Time.zone.now instead of Time.current to ensure consistency with Timecop in tests
   131	      articles = Article.published
   132	        .where("articles.published_at > ?", @since_time)
   133	        .where("articles.published_at <= ?", Time.zone.now)
   134	        .includes(:user)
   135	
   136	      # If keywords are provided, search for articles matching them
   137	      if @keywords.present?
   138	        # Use search_articles for efficient keyword matching (case-insensitive)
   139	        search_term = @keywords.join(" ")
   140	
   141	        # In some test environments, pg_search might not be fully functional due to trigger issues
   142	        # We provide a simple fallback if no results are found with search_articles
   143	        search_results = articles.search_articles(search_term)
   144	
   145	        if search_results.exists?
   146	          articles = search_results
   147	        else
   148	          # Fallback to simple ILIKE search for title and body
   149	          keyword_conditions = @keywords.map do
   150	            "(articles.title ILIKE ? OR articles.body_markdown ILIKE ?)"
   151	          end.join(" OR ")
   152	          bind_values = @keywords.flat_map { |keyword| ["%#{keyword}%", "%#{keyword}%"] }
   153	          articles = articles.where(keyword_conditions, *bind_values)
   154	        end
   155	      end
   156	
   157	      # Filter by minimum indexable threshold using SQL conditions
   158	      min_score = Settings::UserExperience.index_minimum_score
   159	      min_date = Time.at(Settings::UserExperience.index_minimum_date)
   160	
   161	      articles
   162	        .where("articles.score >= ?", -1)
   163	        .where("articles.published_at >= ?", min_date)
   164	        .where("(articles.score >= ? OR articles.featured = ?)", min_score, true)
   165	    end
   166	
   167	    def recently_awarded?(user_id, badge_id)
   168	      # Check if user received this badge within the last week
   169	      cutoff = MIN_DAYS_BETWEEN_AWARDS.days.ago
   170	      BadgeAchievement
   171	        .where(user_id: user_id, badge_id: badge_id)
   172	        .where("created_at > ?", cutoff)
   173	        .exists?
   174	    end
   175	
   176	    def qualifies_for_badge?(article)
   177	      # Use AI to assess if article meets quality criteria
   178	      assessor = Ai::BadgeCriteriaAssessor.new(article, criteria: @criteria)
   179	      assessor.qualifies?
   180	    end
   181	
   182	    def generate_message(article)
   183	      article_url = URL.article(article)
   184	
   185	      "Congratulations on posting a quality article! " \
   186	        "Your post [#{article.title}](#{article_url}) met our quality criteria and earned you this badge."
   187	    end
   188	  end
   189	end

FILE app/services/email_digest_article_collector.rb
     1	class EmailDigestArticleCollector
     2	  include FieldTest::Helpers
     3	  include Instrumentation
     4	
     5	  ARTICLES_TO_SEND = "EmailDigestArticleCollector#articles_to_send".freeze
     6	  RESULTS_COUNT = 7 # Winner of digest_count_03_18 field test
     7	  CLICK_LOOKBACK = 30
     8	  DIGEST_ARTICLE_COLUMNS = %i[id title description path cached_user cached_tag_list
     9	                              subforem_id comment_score comments_count ai_summary ai_summary_generated_at].freeze
    10	
    11	  # Personalized email digests require a higher minimum score than the home feed
    12	  # to ensure that the content sent in emails is of higher quality.
    13	  MINIMUM_SCORE_OFFSET = 15
    14	
    15	  attr_reader :feed_config_id
    16	
    17	  def initialize(user, force_send: false)
    18	    @user = user
    19	    @force_send = force_send
    20	  end
    21	
    22	  def articles_to_send
    23	    instrument ARTICLES_TO_SEND, tags: { user_id: @user.id } do
    24	      return [] unless @force_send || should_receive_email?
    25	
    26	      if FeatureFlag.enabled?(:personalized_email_digests) &&
    27	          Settings::UserExperience.feed_strategy == "configured"
    28	
    29	        variant = field_test(:personalized_email_digests_ab_test, participant: @user)
    30	
    31	        if variant == "personalized"
    32	          articles = personalized_articles
    33	          return articles if articles
    34	        end
    35	      end
    36	
    37	      legacy_articles
    38	    end
    39	  end
    40	
    41	  def should_receive_email?
    42	    return true unless last_email_sent
    43	    return false if last_email_sent > 18.hours.ago
    44	    return true if last_email_clicked?
    45	
    46	    email_sent_within_lookback_period = last_email_sent >= Settings::General.periodic_email_digest.days.ago
    47	    return false if email_sent_within_lookback_period && !recent_tracked_click?
    48	
    49	    true
    50	  end
    51	
    52	  private
    53	
    54	  # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
    55	  def legacy_articles
    56	    order = Arel.sql("(((score + comment_score) * ((feed_success_score * 12) + 0.1)) - (clickbait_score * 2)) DESC")
    57	    articles = if @user.cached_followed_tag_names.any?
    58	                 # Set subforem context for followed subforems or default
    59	                 set_subforem_context
    60	
    61	                 articles_query = @user.followed_articles
    62	                   .select(*DIGEST_ARTICLE_COLUMNS)
    63	                   .published
    64	                   .full_posts
    65	                   .where("published_at > ?", cutoff_date)
    66	                   .where(email_digest_eligible: true)
    67	                   .not_authored_by(@user.id)
    68	                   .where("score > ?", 8)
    69	
    70	                 # Only filter by subforem if we're not skipping subforem filtering
    71	                 articles_query = articles_query.where(subforem_id: @subforem_ids) unless @skip_subforem_filtering
    72	
    73	                 articles_query.order(order).limit(RESULTS_COUNT)
    74	               else
    75	                 tags = @user.cached_followed_tag_names_or_recent_tags
    76	                 # Set subforem context for followed subforems or default
    77	                 set_subforem_context
    78	
    79	                 articles_query = if @skip_subforem_filtering
    80	                                    # If skipping subforem filtering, get articles from anywhere
    81	                                    Article.select(*DIGEST_ARTICLE_COLUMNS)
    82	                                      .published
    83	                                      .full_posts
    84	                                      .where("published_at > ?", cutoff_date)
    85	                                      .where(email_digest_eligible: true)
    86	                                      .not_authored_by(@user.id)
    87	                                      .where("score > ?", 11)
    88	                                      .merge(Article.featured.or(Article.cached_tagged_with_any(tags)))
    89	                                      .order(order)
    90	                                      .limit(RESULTS_COUNT)
    91	                                  else
    92	                                    # Normal logic with subforem filtering and tags
    93	                                    Article.select(*DIGEST_ARTICLE_COLUMNS)
    94	                                      .published
    95	                                      .full_posts
    96	                                      .where("published_at > ?", cutoff_date)
    97	                                      .where(email_digest_eligible: true)
    98	                                      .not_authored_by(@user.id)
    99	                                      .where("score > ?", 11)
   100	                                      .where(subforem_id: @subforem_ids)
   101	                                      .order(order)
   102	                                      .limit(RESULTS_COUNT)
   103	                                      .merge(Article.featured.or(Article.cached_tagged_with_any(tags)))
   104	                                  end
   105	               end
   106	
   107	    # Fallback if there are not enough articles
   108	    if articles.length < 3
   109	      if @skip_subforem_filtering
   110	        # If we're skipping subforem filtering, get articles from anywhere
   111	        articles_query = Article.select(*DIGEST_ARTICLE_COLUMNS)
   112	          .published
   113	          .full_posts
   114	          .where("published_at > ?", cutoff_date)
   115	          .where(email_digest_eligible: true)
   116	          .where("score > ?", 11)
   117	      else
   118	        # For fallback, include both followed subforems and default subforem
   119	        fallback_subforem_ids = @subforem_ids.dup
   120	        default_subforem_id = Subforem.cached_default_id
   121	        if default_subforem_id && fallback_subforem_ids.exclude?(default_subforem_id)
   122	          fallback_subforem_ids << default_subforem_id
   123	        end
   124	
   125	        articles_query = Article.select(*DIGEST_ARTICLE_COLUMNS)
   126	          .published
   127	          .full_posts
   128	          .where("published_at > ?", cutoff_date)
   129	          .where(email_digest_eligible: true)
   130	          .where("score > ?", 11)
   131	          .where(subforem_id: fallback_subforem_ids)
   132	      end
   133	
   134	      articles = articles_query.not_authored_by(@user.id)
   135	        .order(order)
   136	        .limit(RESULTS_COUNT)
   137	
   138	      if @user.cached_antifollowed_tag_names.any?
   139	        articles = articles.not_cached_tagged_with_any(@user.cached_antifollowed_tag_names)
   140	      end
   141	    end
   142	
   143	    # Ensure we operate on an array to avoid relation-slicing surprises
   144	    articles = articles.to_a
   145	
   146	    # Pop second article to front if the first article is the same as the last email
   147	    if articles.any? && last_email_includes_title_in_subject?(articles.first.title)
   148	      articles = articles.rotate(1)
   149	    end
   150	
   151	    articles.length < 3 ? [] : articles
   152	  end
   153	  # rubocop:enable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
   154	
   155	  def personalized_articles
   156	    feed_config = FeedConfig.order(feed_success_score: :desc).limit(15).to_a.sample || FeedConfig.first_or_create
   157	    return unless feed_config
   158	
   159	    set_subforem_context
   160	
   161	    score_sql = feed_config.score_sql(@user)
   162	
   163	    articles_query = Article
   164	      .select(*DIGEST_ARTICLE_COLUMNS)
   165	      .published
   166	      .full_posts
   167	      .where("score >= ?", Settings::UserExperience.home_feed_minimum_score + MINIMUM_SCORE_OFFSET)
   168	      .where("published_at > ?", cutoff_date)
   169	      .where(email_digest_eligible: true)
   170	      .not_authored_by(@user.id)
   171	      .order(Arel.sql("#{score_sql} DESC"))
   172	      .limit(RESULTS_COUNT)
   173	
   174	    articles_query = articles_query.where(subforem_id: @subforem_ids) unless @skip_subforem_filtering
   175	
   176	    blocked_ids = UserBlock.cached_blocked_ids_for_blocker(@user.id)
   177	    articles_query = articles_query.where.not(user_id: blocked_ids) if blocked_ids.any?
   178	
   179	    hidden_tags = @user.cached_antifollowed_tag_names
   180	    articles_query = articles_query.not_cached_tagged_with_any(hidden_tags) if hidden_tags.any?
   181	
   182	    articles = articles_query.to_a
   183	    articles = articles.rotate(1) if articles.any? && last_email_includes_title_in_subject?(articles.first.title)
   184	    
   185	    if articles.length >= 3
   186	      @feed_config_id = feed_config.id
   187	      articles
   188	    else
   189	      nil
   190	    end
   191	  rescue StandardError
   192	    nil
   193	  end
   194	
   195	  def set_subforem_context
   196	    # Get user's followed subforems from UserActivity
   197	    user_activity = @user.user_activity
   198	    followed_subforem_ids = user_activity&.alltime_subforems || []
   199	    default_subforem_id = Subforem.cached_default_id
   200	
   201	    # Check if user has a custom onboarding subforem (not nil and not default)
   202	    has_custom_onboarding = @user.onboarding_subforem_id.present? &&
   203	      @user.onboarding_subforem_id != default_subforem_id
   204	
   205	    if followed_subforem_ids.any?
   206	      # User follows subforems - use those
   207	      @subforem_ids = followed_subforem_ids
   208	      @skip_subforem_filtering = false
   209	    elsif has_custom_onboarding
   210	      # User has custom onboarding subforem - don't filter by subforem at all
   211	      @subforem_ids = []
   212	      @skip_subforem_filtering = true
   213	    else
   214	      # User doesn't follow any subforems and has no custom onboarding - use default subforem
   215	      @subforem_ids = default_subforem_id ? [default_subforem_id] : []
   216	      @skip_subforem_filtering = false
   217	    end
   218	  end
   219	
   220	  def recent_tracked_click?

   263	  def discussion_unlock_confirm
   264	    # This allows admins to also use this action vs searching only in the current_user.articles scope
   265	    @article = Article.find_by(slug: params[:slug])
   266	    not_found unless @article
   267	    authorize @article
   268	
   269	    @discussion_lock = @article.discussion_lock
   270	  end
   271	
   272	  private
   273	
   274	  def base_editor_assignments
   275	    @user = current_user
   276	    @version = @user.setting.editor_version if @user
   277	    @organizations = @user&.organizations
   278	    @tag = Tag.find_by(name: params[:template])
   279	    @prefill = params[:prefill].to_s.gsub("\\n ", "\n").gsub("\\n", "\n")
   280	    @user_approved_liquid_tags = Users::ApprovedLiquidTags.call(@user)
   281	  end
   282	
   283	  def handle_user_or_organization_feed
   284	    if (@user = User.find_by(username: params[:username]))
   285	      @articles = @articles.where(user_id: @user.id)
   286	    elsif (@user = Organization.find_by(slug: params[:username]))
   287	      @articles = @articles.where(organization_id: @user.id).includes(:user)
   288	    end
   289	  end
   290	
   291	  def handle_tag_feed
   292	    tag_name = Tag.aliased_name(params[:tag])
   293	    return unless tag_name
   294	
   295	    @tag = Tag.find_by(name: tag_name)
   296	    @articles = @articles.cached_tagged_with(tag_name)
   297	  end
   298	
   299	  def set_article
   300	    owner = User.find_by(username: params[:username]) || Organization.find_by(slug: params[:username])
   301	    found_article = if params[:slug] && owner
   302	                      owner.articles.find_by(slug: params[:slug])
   303	                    else
   304	                      Article.includes(:user).find(params[:id])
   305	                    end
   306	    @article = found_article || not_found
   307	  end
   308	
   309	  # TODO: refactor all of this update logic into the Articles::Updater possibly,
   310	  # ideally there should only be one place to handle the update logic
   311	  def article_params_json
   312	    return @article_params_json if @article_params_json
   313	
   314	    params.require(:article) # to trigger the correct exception in case `:article` is missing
   315	
   316	    params["article"].transform_keys!(&:underscore)
   317	
   318	    allowed_params = if params["article"]["version"] == "v1"
   319	                       %i[body_markdown]
   320	                     else
   321	                       %i[
   322	                         title body_markdown main_image published description video_thumbnail_url
   323	                         tag_list canonical_url series collection_id archived published_at timezone
   324	                         published_at_date published_at_time type_of body_url subforem_id
   325	                       ]
   326	                     end
   327	
   328	    # Allow video_source_url if it's a valid YouTube, Mux, or Twitch URL
   329	    video_url = params.dig("article", "video_source_url")
   330	    if video_url.present?
   331	      youtube_pattern = /\Ahttps?:\/\/(www\.)?(youtube\.com\/watch\?v=|youtu\.be\/)/
   332	      mux_pattern = /\Ahttps?:\/\/player\.mux\.com\//
   333	      twitch_pattern = /\Ahttps?:\/\/(www\.)?twitch\.tv\/videos\//
   334	      allowed_params << :video_source_url if video_url.match?(youtube_pattern) || video_url.match?(mux_pattern) || video_url.match?(twitch_pattern)
   335	    end
   336	
   337	    # NOTE: the organization logic is still a little counter intuitive but this should
   338	    # fix the bug <https://github.com/forem/forem/issues/2871>
   339	    if org_admin_user_change_privilege
   340	      allowed_params << :user_id
   341	    end
   342	
   343	    if params["article"].key?("organization_id") && allowed_to_change_org_id?
   344	      # change the organization of the article only if explicitly asked to do so
   345	      allowed_params << :organization_id
   346	    end
   347	
   348	    allowed_params << :co_author_ids_list if allowed_to_manage_org_co_authors?
   349	
   350	    manage_published_at_params
   351	
   352	    @article_params_json = params.require(:article).permit(allowed_params)
   353	  end
   354	
   355	  def manage_published_at_params
   356	    time_zone_str = params["article"].delete("timezone")
   357	
   358	    time = params["article"].delete("published_at_time")
   359	    date = params["article"].delete("published_at_date")
   360	
   361	    if date.present?
   362	      time_zone = Time.find_zone(time_zone_str)
   363	      time_zone ||= Time.find_zone("UTC")
   364	      params["article"]["published_at"] = time_zone.parse("#{date} #{time}")
   365	    elsif params["article"]["version"] != "v1" && !params["article"]["from_dashboard"]
   366	      params["article"]["published_at"] = nil
   367	    end
   368	  end
   369	
   370	  def allowed_to_change_org_id?
   371	    potential_user = @article&.user || current_user
   372	    potential_org_id = requested_organization_id || @article&.organization_id
   373	    OrganizationMembership.exists?(user: potential_user, organization_id: potential_org_id) ||
   374	      current_user.any_admin?
   375	  end
   376	
   377	  def org_admin_user_change_privilege
   378	    params[:article][:user_id] &&
   379	      # if current_user is an org admin of the article's org
   380	      current_user.org_admin?(@article.organization_id) &&
   381	      # and if the author being changed to belongs to the article's org
   382	      OrganizationMembership.exists?(user_id: params[:article][:user_id], organization_id: @article.organization_id)
   383	  end
   384	
   385	  def allowed_to_manage_org_co_authors?
   386	    organization_id = effective_organization_id
   387	
   388	    organization_id.present? && current_user.org_admin?(organization_id)
   389	  end
   390	
   391	  def effective_organization_id
   392	    return requested_organization_id if params["article"].key?("organization_id") && allowed_to_change_org_id?
   393	
   394	    @article&.organization_id
   395	  end
   396	
   397	  def requested_organization_id
   398	    params["article"]["organization_id"].presence
   399	  end
   400	end
