FILE app/services/articles/updater.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  class Updater
     3	    Result = Struct.new(:success, :article, keyword_init: true)
     4	
     5	    def self.call(...)
     6	      new(...).call
     7	    end
     8	
     9	    def initialize(user, article, article_params)
    10	      @user = user
    11	      @article = article
    12	      @article_params = normalize_params(article_params)
    13	    end
    14	
    15	    def call
    16	      user.rate_limiter.check_limit!(:article_update)
    17	      success = article.update(article_params)
    18	
    19	      if success
    20	        user.rate_limiter.track_limit_by_action(:article_update)
    21	
    22	        if became_unpublished?
    23	          remove_all_notifications
    24	          bust_author_profile_cache
    25	        end
    26	
    27	        send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers if remains_published?
    28	        refresh_auto_audience_segments if became_published?
    29	        complete_onboarding_first_post if became_published?
    30	      end
    31	
    32	      Result.new(success: success, article: article.decorate)
    33	    end
    34	
    35	    private
    36	
    37	    attr_reader :user, :article, :article_params
    38	
    39	    def normalize_params(original_params)
    40	      article_params = original_params.dup
    41	
    42	      # updated edited time only if already published and not edited by an admin
    43	      update_edited_at = article.user == user && article.published
    44	
    45	      # remove published_at values received from a user if an articles was published before (has past published_at)
    46	      # published_at will remain as it was in this case
    47	      article_params.delete :published_at if article.published_at && !article.scheduled?
    48	
    49	      # NOTE: It's surprising that this is article.user and not @user
    50	      Articles::Attributes.new(article_params, article.user, organization: article.organization)
    51	        .for_update(update_edited_at: update_edited_at)
    52	    end
    53	
    54	    def refresh_auto_audience_segments
    55	      user.refresh_auto_audience_segments
    56	    end
    57	
    58	    def complete_onboarding_first_post
    59	      return if user.registered_at&.before?(28.days.ago)
    60	
    61	      user.onboarding_checklist&.complete_item!("made_first_post")
    62	    end
    63	
    64	    def became_published?
    65	      article.published? && !article.published_previously_was
    66	    end
    67	
    68	    def remains_published?
    69	      article.published && article.saved_change_to_published.blank?
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    def became_unpublished?
    73	      article.saved_changes["published"] == [true, false]
    74	    end
    75	
    76	    # If the article has already been published and is only being updated, then we need to create
    77	    # mentions and send notifications to mentioned users inline via the Mentions::CreateAll service.
    78	    def send_to_mentioned_users_and_followers
    79	      Mentions::CreateAll.call(article)
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    # Remove any associated notifications if Article is unpublished
    83	    def remove_all_notifications
    84	      Notification.remove_all_by_action_without_delay(notifiable_ids: article.id, notifiable_type: "Article",
    85	                                                      action: "Published")
    86	      ContextNotification.delete_by(context_id: article.id, context_type: "Article",
    87	                                    action: "Published")
    88	
    89	      if article.comments.exists?
    90	        Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.comments.ids,
    91	                                notifiable_type: "Comment")
    92	      end
    93	      return unless article.mentions.exists?
    94	
    95	      Notification.remove_all(notifiable_ids: article.mentions.ids,
    96	                              notifiable_type: "Mention")
    97	    end
    98	
    99	    def bust_author_profile_cache
   100	      EdgeCache::BustUser.call(article.user)
   101	    end
   102	  end
   103	end

FILE app/services/articles/unpublish.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  module Unpublish
     3	    module_function
     4	
     5	    def call(user, article)
     6	      attributes = {}
     7	      if article.has_frontmatter?
     8	        body_markdown = article.body_markdown.sub(/\npublished:\s*true\s*\n/, "\npublished: false\n")
     9	        attributes[:body_markdown] = body_markdown
    10	      else
    11	        attributes[:published] = false
    12	      end
    13	
    14	      Articles::Updater.call(user, article, attributes)
    15	    end
    16	  end
    17	end

FILE app/services/articles/page_view_updater.rb
     1	module Articles
     2	  # This module is responsible for updating a specific page view for a given article and user.
     3	  #
     4	  # @see Articles::UpdatePageViewsWorker for the sibling that's responsible for recording page
     5	  #      views.
     6	  module PageViewUpdater
     7	    EXTENDED_PAGEVIEW_NUMBER = 60
     8	    # @param article_id [Integer]
     9	    # @param user_id [Integer]
    10	    #
    11	    # @return [TrueClass] if we updated or created a PageView
    12	    # @return [FalseClass] if we did not update a PageView
    13	    #
    14	    # @note Regardless of return status, consider the business logic successful unless we raise an
    15	    #       exception.  The return value is present for easing testing.  The `find_or_create_by`
    16	    #       adds a complication in the testing logic
    17	    #       (e.g., `expect { Articles::PageViewUpdater.call }.not_to change(PageView, :count) `
    18	    def self.call(article_id:, user_id:)
    19	      # Don't record views to non-existent or unpublished articles.
    20	      return false unless Article.published.exists?(id: article_id)
    21	      # Don't record author's own views.
    22	      return false if Article.published.from_subforem.exists?(id: article_id, user_id: user_id)
    23	
    24	      page_view = PageView.order(created_at: :desc)
    25	        .find_or_create_by(article_id: article_id, user_id: user_id)
    26	      return true if page_view.new_record?
    27	
    28	      new_time_mark = page_view.time_tracked_in_seconds + 15
    29	      page_view.update_column(:time_tracked_in_seconds, new_time_mark)
    30	      if new_time_mark == EXTENDED_PAGEVIEW_NUMBER
    31	        FeedEvent.record_journey_for(page_view.user, article: page_view.article, category: :extended_pageview)
    32	        UpdateUserInterestEmbeddingWorker.perform_async(user_id, article_id, 0.05) if user_id
    33	      end
    34	
    35	      true
    36	    end
    37	  end
    38	end

   220	  def replace_legacy_code_html(html)
   221	    return html if html.blank?
   222	    return html unless html.include?("runkit-element")
   223	
   224	    fragment = Nokogiri::HTML.fragment(html)
   225	    fragment.css('.runkit-element').each do |element|
   226	      preamble = element.at_css('code:nth-of-type(1)')&.text.to_s
   227	      content = element.at_css('code:nth-of-type(2)')&.text.to_s
   228	
   229	      replacement_html = LegacyCodeTag.fallback_html(
   230	        preamble: preamble,
   231	        parsed_content: content,
   232	      )
   233	
   234	      element.replace(Nokogiri::HTML.fragment(replacement_html))
   235	    end
   236	
   237	    fragment.to_html
   238	  rescue StandardError
   239	    html
   240	  end

     1	class FeedEvent < ApplicationRecord
     2	  # These are "optional" mostly so that we can perform validated bulk inserts
     3	  # without triggering article/user validation.
     4	  # Since there are database-level constraints, it's fine to skip the automatic
     5	  # Rails-side association validation (which causes an N+1 query).
     6	  belongs_to :article, optional: true
     7	  belongs_to :user, optional: true
     8	  belongs_to :feed_config, optional: true
     9	
    10	  after_save :update_article_counters_and_scores
    11	  after_save :create_feed_config_offshoot
    12	  after_create_commit :record_field_test_event
    13	
    14	  enum :category, {
    15	    impression: 0,
    16	    click: 1,
    17	    reaction: 2,
    18	    comment: 3,
    19	    extended_pageview: 4
    20	  }
    21	
    22	  CONTEXT_TYPE_HOME = "home".freeze
    23	  CONTEXT_TYPE_SEARCH = "search".freeze
    24	  CONTEXT_TYPE_TAG = "tag".freeze
    25	  CONTEXT_TYPE_EMAIL = "email".freeze
    26	  CONTEXT_TYPE_SIDEBAR = "sidebar".freeze
    27	  VALID_CONTEXT_TYPES = [
    28	    CONTEXT_TYPE_HOME,
    29	    CONTEXT_TYPE_SEARCH,
    30	    CONTEXT_TYPE_TAG,
    31	    CONTEXT_TYPE_EMAIL,
    32	    CONTEXT_TYPE_SIDEBAR
    33	  ].freeze
    34	  DEFAULT_TIMEBOX = 5.minutes.freeze
    35	
    36	  REACTION_SCORE_MULTIPLIER = 6
    37	  COMMENT_SCORE_MULTIPLIER = 12
    38	
    39	  validates :article_position, numericality: { only_integer: true, greater_than: 0 }
    40	  validates :context_type, inclusion: { in: VALID_CONTEXT_TYPES }, presence: true
    41	  # Since we have disabled association validation, this is handy to filter basic bad data
    42	  validates :article_id, presence: true, numericality: { only_integer: true }
    43	  validates :user_id, numericality: { only_integer: true }, allow_nil: true
    44	
    45	  def self.record_journey_for(user, article:, category:)
    46	    return unless %i[reaction comment extended_pageview].include?(category)
    47	
    48	    last_click = where(user: user, category: :click).last
    49	    return unless last_click&.article_id == article.id
    50	
    51	    create_with(last_click.slice(:article_position, :context_type, :feed_config_id))
    52	      .find_or_create_by(
    53	        category: category,
    54	        user: user,
    55	        article: article,
    56	      )
    57	  end
    58	
    59	  def self.bulk_update_counters_by_article_id(article_ids)
    60	    unique_article_ids = article_ids.uniq
    61	    update_counters_for_articles(unique_article_ids)
    62	  end
    63	
    64	  def self.update_counters_for_articles(article_ids)
    65	    article_ids.each do |article_id|
    66	      update_single_article_counters(article_id)
    67	    end
    68	  end
    69	
    70	  def self.update_single_article_counters(article_id)
    71	    ThrottledCall.perform("article_feed_success_score_#{article_id}", throttle_for: 5.minutes) do
    72	      impressions = FeedEvent.where(article_id: article_id, category: "impression")
    73	      return if impressions.empty?
    74	
    75	      clicks = FeedEvent.where(article_id: article_id, category: "click")
    76	      reactions = FeedEvent.where(article_id: article_id, category: "reaction")
    77	      comments = FeedEvent.where(article_id: article_id, category: "comment")
    78	      pageviews = FeedEvent.where(article_id: article_id, category: "extended_pageview")
    79	
    80	      # Count the distinct users for impressions and each event type
    81	      distinct_impressions_users = impressions.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
    82	      distinct_clicks_users = clicks.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
    83	      distinct_reactions_users = reactions.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
    84	      distinct_comments_users = comments.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
    85	      distinct_pageviews_users = pageviews.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
    86	
    87	      # Calculate score based on distinct users
    88	      reactions_score = distinct_reactions_users.size * REACTION_SCORE_MULTIPLIER
    89	      clicks_score = distinct_clicks_users.size # 1x multiplier for clicks
    90	      comments_score = distinct_comments_users.size * COMMENT_SCORE_MULTIPLIER
    91	      pageviews_score = distinct_pageviews_users.size # 1x multiplier for extended pageviews
    92	
    93	      score = (clicks_score + pageviews_score + reactions_score + comments_score).to_f / distinct_impressions_users.size
    94	
    95	      # Update the article counters
    96	      Article.where(id: article_id).update_all(
    97	        feed_success_score: score,
    98	        feed_clicks_count: clicks.size,
    99	        feed_impressions_count: impressions.size,
   100	      )
   101	    end
   102	  end
   103	
   104	  def self.update_single_feed_config_counters(feed_config_id)
   105	    ThrottledCall.perform("feed_config_feed_success_score_#{feed_config_id}", throttle_for: 5.minutes) do
   106	      impressions = FeedEvent.where(feed_config_id: feed_config_id, category: "impression")
   107	      return if impressions.empty?
   108	
   109	      clicks = FeedEvent.where(feed_config_id: feed_config_id, category: "click")
   110	      reactions = FeedEvent.where(feed_config_id: feed_config_id, category: "reaction")
   111	      comments = FeedEvent.where(feed_config_id: feed_config_id, category: "comment")
   112	      pageviews = FeedEvent.where(feed_config_id: feed_config_id, category: "extended_pageview")
   113	
   114	      # Count the distinct users for impressions and each event type
   115	      distinct_impressions_users = impressions.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
   116	      distinct_clicks_users = clicks.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
   117	      distinct_reactions_users = reactions.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
   118	      distinct_comments_users = comments.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
   119	      distinct_pageviews_users = pageviews.distinct.pluck(:user_id)
   120	
   121	      # Calculate score based on distinct users
   122	      reactions_score = distinct_reactions_users.size * REACTION_SCORE_MULTIPLIER * 2
   123	      clicks_score = clicks.sum("POWER(2.0/3, article_position - 1)")
   124	      comments_score = distinct_comments_users.size * COMMENT_SCORE_MULTIPLIER * 2
   125	      pageviews_score = distinct_pageviews_users.size # 1x multiplier for extended pageviews
   126	
   127	      score = (clicks_score + pageviews_score + reactions_score + comments_score).to_f / distinct_impressions_users.size
   128	      
   129	      # Update the feed config counters
   130	      FeedConfig.find_by(id: feed_config_id)&.update_columns(feed_success_score: score, feed_impressions_count: impressions.size)
   131	    end
   132	  end
   133	
   134	  private
   135	
   136	  def update_article_counters_and_scores
   137	    self.class.update_single_article_counters(article_id) if article_id
   138	    self.class.update_single_feed_config_counters(feed_config_id) if feed_config_id
   139	  end
   140	
   141	  def create_feed_config_offshoot
   142	    # Any time we get a reaction or comment, we branch off a slightly modified feed based on the success of the current one
   143	    return unless feed_config
   144	    return unless %w[reaction comment].include?(category)
   145	
   146	    feed_config.create_slightly_modified_clone!
   147	  end
   148	
   149	  # @see AbExperiment::GoalConversionHandler
   150	  def record_field_test_event
   151	    return if FieldTest.config["experiments"].nil?
   152	    return if category.to_s == "impression"
   153	    return unless user_id
   154	    return unless context_type == CONTEXT_TYPE_EMAIL # We are only doing this for email at the moment
   155	
   156	    Users::RecordFieldTestEventWorker
   157	      .perform_async(user_id, AbExperiment::GoalConversionHandler::USER_CREATES_EMAIL_FEED_EVENT_GOAL)
   158	  end
   159	end

FILE app/services/feed_events/bulk_upsert.rb
     1	module FeedEvents
     2	  # Inserts a collection of feed events into the database.
     3	  #
     4	  # If there are duplicate events in the collection (i.e. having the same user,
     5	  # article, and category) only one will be inserted.
     6	  #
     7	  # If a timebox is provided and there is a duplicate event (using the criteria
     8	  # for uniqueness above) that was created within it, any matching new event will
     9	  # not be created.
    10	  #
    11	  # This avoids inflating metrics (whether by accident or deliberately).
    12	  class BulkUpsert
    13	    ATTRIBUTES_FOR_INSERT = %i[article_id user_id article_position category context_type feed_config_id].freeze
    14	
    15	    def self.call(...)
    16	      new(...).call
    17	    end
    18	
    19	    # @param feed_events_data [Array<Hash|FeedEvent>] A list of feed events attributes to upsert
    20	    # @param timebox [ActiveSupport::Duration] A time window (in minutes) within which feed events must be unique
    21	    def initialize(feed_events_data, timebox: FeedEvent::DEFAULT_TIMEBOX)
    22	      @feed_events_data = feed_events_data
    23	      @timebox = timebox
    24	    end
    25	
    26	    def call
    27	      return if valid_events.blank?
    28	
    29	      if valid_events.size == 1
    30	        create_single_event!
    31	        return
    32	      end
    33	
    34	      # It's *possible* to construct a single SQL query that does what we want
    35	      # here, (i.e. find existing records, filter out duplicates, and insert the
    36	      # resulting set) but it is more complicated than it sounds.
    37	      # For instance, using Postgres' upserting feature (`ON CONFLICT DO...`) is
    38	      # not an option because we *don't* actually want the records to be unique
    39	      # in general, just within the provided timebox, so there is no constraint
    40	      # to trigger a conflict.
    41	      # Instead we take a Rails-ish `find_or_create_by` approach: first try to
    42	      # find matching record(s), then figure out which new record(s) to insert
    43	      # application-side. Making two queries does potentially allow duplicates
    44	      # (through race conditions), but that's an acceptable margin of error and
    45	      # would be the case anyway with a single query (without locking the table)
    46	      if timebox.present?
    47	        find_existing_events_within_timebox do |event|
    48	          track_recent_event(event)
    49	        end
    50	      end
    51	
    52	      records_to_insert = valid_events.filter_map do |event|
    53	        unless recent_event?(event)
    54	          track_recent_event(event)
    55	          ATTRIBUTES_FOR_INSERT.index_with { |attr| event[attr] }
    56	        end
    57	      end
    58	
    59	      return if records_to_insert.blank?
    60	
    61	      article_ids = records_to_insert.pluck(:article_id).compact.uniq
    62	      valid_article_ids = Set.new(Article.where(id: article_ids).pluck(:id))
    63	      user_ids = records_to_insert.pluck(:user_id).compact.uniq
    64	      valid_user_ids = Set.new(User.where(id: user_ids).pluck(:id))
    65	
    66	      records_to_insert.select! do |r|
    67	        valid_article_ids.include?(r[:article_id]) &&
    68	          (r[:user_id].nil? || valid_user_ids.include?(r[:user_id]))
    69	      end
    70	
    71	      return if records_to_insert.blank?
    72	
    73	      begin
    74	        FeedEvent.insert_all(records_to_insert)
    75	        FeedEvent.bulk_update_counters_by_article_id(records_to_insert.pluck(:article_id).sample(5))
    76	      rescue ActiveRecord::InvalidForeignKey
    77	        # Race condition: article or user was deleted after our check but before insert_all.
    78	        # We can gracefully ignore this failure rather than throwing a 500 for an analytics payload.
    79	      end
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    private
    83	
    84	    attr_reader :feed_events_data, :timebox
    85	
    86	    def valid_events
    87	      @valid_events ||= feed_events_data.filter_map do |event_data|
    88	        event = FeedEvent.new(event_data)
    89	        event if event.valid?
    90	      rescue ArgumentError
    91	        # Enums raise ArgumentError if assigned with invalid value
    92	      end
    93	    end
    94	
    95	    def create_single_event!
    96	      event = valid_events.first
    97	      FeedEvent
    98	        .where.not("created_at > ?", timebox.ago.utc) # Only proceed if
    99	        .create_with(event.slice(:article_position, :context_type))
   100	        .find_or_create_by(event.slice(:article_id, :user_id, :category))
   101	    rescue ActiveRecord::InvalidForeignKey
   102	      # Ignore if the article or user was deleted concurrently
   103	    end
   104	
   105	    def recent_events
   106	      @recent_events ||= Hash.new do |users, user_id|
   107	        users[user_id] = Hash.new do |categories, category|
   108	          categories[category] = {} # articles
   109	        end
   110	      end
   111	    end
   112	
   113	    def recent_event?(event)
   114	      recent_events[event.user_id][event.category][event.article_id]
   115	    end
   116	
   117	    def track_recent_event(event)
   118	      recent_events[event.user_id][event.category][event.article_id] = true
   119	    end
   120	
   121	    def find_existing_events_within_timebox(&block)
   122	      values = valid_events.reduce([]) do |acc, event|
   123	        acc << event.article_id
   124	        # Postgres is...iffy about comparing NULL (NULL !== NULL), and chokes if it is present in a tuple comparison.
   125	        # Coalescing to 0 is fine because primary keys auto-increment from 1.
   126	        acc << (event.user_id || 0)
   127	        acc << FeedEvent.categories[event[:category]]
   128	      end
   129	
   130	      values_clause = Array.new(valid_events.length) { "(?, ?, ?)" }.join(", ")
   131	
   132	      FeedEvent
   133	        .where("created_at > ?", timebox.ago.utc)
   134	        .where(
   135	          "(article_id, COALESCE(user_id, 0), category) IN (#{values_clause})",
   136	          *values,
   137	        )
   138	        .each(&block)
   139	    end
   140	  end
   141	end

FILE app/workers/reactions/bust_reactable_cache_worker.rb
     1	module Reactions
     2	  class BustReactableCacheWorker
     3	    include Sidekiq::Job
     4	
     5	    sidekiq_options queue: :high_priority, retry: 10
     6	
     7	    def perform(reaction_id)
     8	      reaction = Reaction.find_by(id: reaction_id)
     9	      return unless reaction&.reactable
    10	
    11	      EdgeCache::PurgeByKey.call(
    12	        reaction.user&.profile_identity_record_key,
    13	        fallback_paths: reaction.user ? [reaction.user.path] : nil,
    14	      )
    15	
    16	      case reaction.reactable_type
    17	      when "Article"
    18	        article = reaction.reactable
    19	        EdgeCache::PurgeByKey.call(
    20	          Reaction.surrogate_key_for_article(article.id),
    21	          fallback_paths: "/reactions?article_id=#{article.id}",
    22	        )
    23	
    24	        # We only want to bust on the creation or deletion of the "first" reaction.
    25	        # This is logically called *after* creation, but *before* deletion. So "1" is correct in each case.
    26	        if Reaction.for_articles([reaction.reactable_id]).public_category.size == 1
    27	          EdgeCache::BustArticle.call(article)
    28	        end
    29	        Articles::UpdateDependentEmbedsWorker.perform_async(article.id)
    30	      when "Comment"
    31	        commentable = reaction.reactable.commentable
    32	        if commentable
    33	          EdgeCache::PurgeByKey.call(
    34	            Reaction.surrogate_key_for_commentable(commentable),
    35	            fallback_paths: "/reactions?commentable_id=#{commentable.id}&commentable_type=#{commentable.class.name}",
    36	          )
    37	        end
    38	
    39	      end
    40	    end
    41	  end
    42	end

FILE app/workers/reactions/bust_homepage_cache_worker.rb
     1	module Reactions
     2	  class BustHomepageCacheWorker
     3	    include Sidekiq::Job
     4	
     5	    sidekiq_options queue: :high_priority, retry: 10
     6	
     7	    def perform(reaction_id)
     8	      reaction = Reaction.find_by(id: reaction_id, reactable_type: "Article")
     9	      return unless reaction&.reactable
    10	
    11	      featured_articles_ids = Article.featured.order(hotness_score: :desc).limit(3).ids
    12	      return unless featured_articles_ids.include?(reaction.reactable_id)
    13	
    14	      reaction.reactable.touch
    15	      cache_bust = EdgeCache::Bust.new
    16	      cache_bust.call("/")
    17	      cache_bust.call("/")
    18	      cache_bust.call("/?i=i")
    19	      cache_bust.call("?i=i")
    20	    end
    21	  end
    22	end

FILE app/workers/notifications/create_round_robin_moderation_notifications_worker.rb
     1	module Notifications
     2	  class CreateRoundRobinModerationNotificationsWorker
     3	    include Sidekiq::Job
     4	
     5	    sidekiq_options queue: :medium_priority, retry: 10
     6	
     7	    MODERATOR_SAMPLE_SIZE = 4
     8	
     9	    def perform(notifiable_id, notifiable_type)
    10	      random_moderators = Users::SelectModeratorsQuery.call.order(Arel.sql("RANDOM()"))
    11	        .first(MODERATOR_SAMPLE_SIZE)
    12	      return unless random_moderators.any?
    13	
    14	      if notifiable_type == "Comment"
    15	        notifiable = Comment.find_by(id: notifiable_id)
    16	        # return if it's a comment whose commentable has been deleted
    17	        return unless notifiable&.commentable
    18	
    19	      elsif notifiable_type == "Article"
    20	        notifiable = Article.find_by(id: notifiable_id)
    21	      end
    22	
    23	      return unless notifiable && !notifiable.user.limited?
    24	
    25	      random_moderators.each do |mod|
    26	        next if mod == notifiable.user
    27	
    28	        Notifications::Moderation::Send.call(mod, notifiable)
    29	      end
    30	    end
    31	  end
    32	end

FILE app/workers/notifications/new_reaction_worker.rb
     1	module Notifications
     2	  class NewReactionWorker
     3	    include Sidekiq::Job
     4	
     5	    sidekiq_options queue: :medium_priority, retry: 10
     6	
     7	    def perform(reaction_data, receiver_data)
     8	      # Sidekiq Parameters are hash with stringified keys, so we need to symbolize keys
     9	      receiver_data = receiver_data.symbolize_keys
    10	      reaction_data = reaction_data.symbolize_keys
    11	
    12	      receiver_klass = receiver_data.fetch(:klass)
    13	      return unless %w[User Organization].include?(receiver_klass)
    14	
    15	      receiver = receiver_klass.constantize.find_by(id: receiver_data.fetch(:id))
    16	      Notifications::Reactions::Send.call(reaction_data, receiver) if should_send?(receiver, receiver_klass)
    17	    end
    18	
    19	    def should_send?(receiver, receiver_klass)
    20	      return false unless receiver
    21	      return true if receiver_klass == "Organization"
    22	
    23	      receiver.notification_setting.reaction_notifications
    24	    end
    25	  end
    26	end

FILE app/services/notifications/milestone/send.rb
     1	module Notifications
     2	  module Milestone
     3	    ARTICLE_FINAL_PUBLICATION_TIME_FOR_MILESTONE = Time.zone.local(2019, 2, 25)
     4	
     5	    class Send
     6	      def self.call(...)
     7	        new(...).call
     8	      end
     9	
    10	      # @param type [String] - "View" or "Reaction"
    11	      # @param article [Object] - ActiveRecord Article object
    12	      def initialize(type, article)
    13	        @type = type
    14	        @article = article
    15	        @next_milestone = next_milestone
    16	      end
    17	
    18	      def call
    19	        return unless should_send_milestone?
    20	
    21	        create_notification(user_id: article.user_id)
    22	        return unless article.organization_id
    23	
    24	        create_notification(organization_id: article.organization_id)
    25	      end
    26	
    27	      private
    28	
    29	      attr_reader :type, :article
    30	
    31	      def create_notification(hash_id)
    32	        Notification.create!({
    33	          notifiable_id: article.id,
    34	          notifiable_type: "Article",
    35	          json_data: json_data,
    36	          action: "Milestone::#{type}::#{@next_milestone}"
    37	        }.merge(hash_id))
    38	      end
    39	
    40	      def json_data
    41	        gif_id = Constants::RandomGifs::IDS.sample
    42	        { article: Notifications.article_data(article), gif_id: gif_id }
    43	      end
    44	
    45	      def article_published_behind_time?
    46	        article.published_at < ARTICLE_FINAL_PUBLICATION_TIME_FOR_MILESTONE
    47	      end
    48	
    49	      def should_send_milestone?
    50	        return if article_published_behind_time?
    51	
    52	        last_milestone_notification = Notification.find_by(
    53	          user_id: article.user_id,
    54	          notifiable_type: "Article",
    55	          notifiable_id: article.id,
    56	          action: "Milestone::#{type}::#{@next_milestone}",
    57	        )
    58	
    59	        case type
    60	        when "View"
    61	          last_milestone_notification.blank? && article.page_views_count > @next_milestone
    62	        when "Reaction"
    63	          last_milestone_notification.blank? && article.public_reactions_count > @next_milestone
    64	        end
    65	      end
    66	
    67	      def next_milestone
    68	        case type
    69	        when "View"
    70	          milestones = [1024, 2048, 4096, 8192, 16_384, 32_768, 65_536, 131_072, 262_144, 524_288, 1_048_576]
    71	          milestone_count = article.page_views_count
    72	        when "Reaction"
    73	          milestones = [64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096, 8192]
    74	          milestone_count = article.public_reactions_count
    75	        end
    76	
    77	        closest_number = milestones.min_by { |num| (milestone_count - num).abs }
    78	        if milestone_count > closest_number
    79	          closest_number
    80	        else
    81	          milestones[milestones.index(closest_number) - 1]
    82	        end
    83	      end
    84	    end
    85	  end
    86	end

FILE app/services/notifications/subforem_change_notification/send.rb
     1	# send notifications about a subforem change
     2	module Notifications
     3	  module SubforemChangeNotification
     4	    class Send
     5	      def self.call(...)
     6	        new(...).call
     7	      end
     8	
     9	      def initialize(article:, old_subforem_id:, new_subforem_id:)
    10	        @article = article
    11	        @old_subforem_id = old_subforem_id
    12	        @new_subforem_id = new_subforem_id
    13	      end
    14	
    15	      delegate :user_data, to: Notifications
    16	
    17	      def call
    18	        json_data = {
    19	          user: user_data(User.mascot_account),
    20	          article: { title: article.title, path: article.path },
    21	          old_subforem: old_subforem_data,
    22	          new_subforem: new_subforem_data,
    23	          reason: "Your article was automatically moved to a more appropriate community based on its content."
    24	        }
    25	        Notification.create(
    26	          user_id: article.user_id,
    27	          notifiable_id: article.id,
    28	          notifiable_type: "Article",
    29	          action: "subforem_change",
    30	          json_data: json_data,
    31	        )
    32	      end
    33	
    34	      private
    35	
    36	      attr_reader :article, :old_subforem_id, :new_subforem_id
    37	
    38	      def old_subforem_data
    39	        return unless old_subforem_id
    40	
    41	        old_subforem = Subforem.find_by(id: old_subforem_id)
    42	        return unless old_subforem
    43	
    44	        {
    45	          id: old_subforem.id,
    46	          domain: old_subforem.domain,
    47	          name: Settings::Community.community_name(subforem_id: old_subforem.id),
    48	          misc: old_subforem.misc
    49	        }
    50	      end
    51	
    52	      def new_subforem_data
    53	        new_subforem = Subforem.find(new_subforem_id)
    54	        {
    55	          id: new_subforem.id,
    56	          domain: new_subforem.domain,
    57	          name: Settings::Community.community_name(subforem_id: new_subforem.id),
    58	          misc: new_subforem.misc
    59	        }
    60	      end
    61	    end

FILE app/workers/sync_webpage_references_worker.rb
     1	class SyncWebpageReferencesWorker
     2	  include Sidekiq::Job
     3	  sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority, lock: :until_executing, on_conflict: :replace
     4	
     5	  def perform(record_class_name, record_id)
     6	    record_class = record_class_name.safe_constantize
     7	    return unless record_class
     8	
     9	    record = record_class.find_by(id: record_id)
    10	    return unless record
    11	
    12	    urls = WebpageExtractor.extract(record)
    13	    
    14	    previous_domain_ids = record.webpage_references.pluck(:linked_domain_id)
    15	    current_domain_ids = []
    16	
    17	    WebpageReference.transaction do
    18	      # 1. Clear out existing references for this record
    19	      record.webpage_references.delete_all
    20	
    21	      # 2. Process and insert new references
    22	      if urls.any?
    23	        references_to_insert = []
    24	        urls.each do |url|
    25	          domain = LinkedDomain.find_or_create_by_url(url)
    26	          next unless domain
    27	
    28	          current_domain_ids << domain.id
    29	
    30	          references_to_insert << {
    31	            record_type: record_class_name,
    32	            record_id: record.id,
    33	            linked_domain_id: domain.id,
    34	            url: url,
    35	            created_at: Time.current,
    36	            updated_at: Time.current
    37	          }
    38	        end
    39	
    40	        WebpageReference.insert_all(references_to_insert) if references_to_insert.any?
    41	      end
    42	    end
    43	
    44	    # Trigger score update for the domains involved
    45	    domain_ids_to_update = (previous_domain_ids + current_domain_ids).uniq
    46	    
    47	    domain_ids_to_update.each do |domain_id|
    48	      LinkedDomains::UpdateScoreWorker.perform_async(domain_id)
    49	    end
    50	  end
    51	end

FILE app/workers/update_user_interest_embedding_worker.rb
     1	class UpdateUserInterestEmbeddingWorker
     2	  include Sidekiq::Job
     3	  sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority, lock: :until_executing, on_conflict: :replace
     4	
     5	  def perform(user_id, article_id, blend_factor = 0.2)
     6	    blend_factor = blend_factor.to_f.clamp(0.0, 1.0)
     7	    article = Article.find_by(id: article_id)
     8	    return unless article && article.respond_to?(:semantic_embedding) && article.semantic_embedding.present?
     9	
    10	    article_vector = article.semantic_embedding.to_a
    11	    return unless article_vector.length == 768
    12	
    13	    UserActivity.transaction do
    14	      user_activity = UserActivity.lock.find_or_create_by!(user_id: user_id)
    15	
    16	      if user_activity.respond_to?(:interest_embedding)
    17	        current_vector = user_activity.interest_embedding&.to_a
    18	
    19	        new_vector = if current_vector.blank? || current_vector.length != 768
    20	                       article_vector
    21	                     else
    22	                       # Blend vectors using Exponential Moving Average
    23	                       current_vector.zip(article_vector).map do |c, a|
    24	                         (c * (1 - blend_factor)) + (a * blend_factor)
    25	                       end
    26	                     end
    27	
    28	        user_activity.update_column(:interest_embedding, new_vector)
    29	      end
    30	    end
    31	  end
    32	end

FILE app/workers/follows/update_points_worker.rb
     1	module Follows
     2	  class UpdatePointsWorker
     3	    include Sidekiq::Job
     4	    sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority, retry: 10, lock: :until_and_while_executing
     5	
     6	    def perform(article_id, user_id)
     7	      article = Article.find_by(id: article_id)
     8	      user = User.find_by(id: user_id)
     9	      return unless article && user
    10	
    11	      adjust_other_tag_follows_of_user(user.id)
    12	      followed_tag_names =
    13	        user.cached_followed_tag_names + user.cached_antifollowed_tag_names
    14	      article.tag_list.each do |tag_name|
    15	        if followed_tag_names.include?(tag_name)
    16	          recalculate_tag_follow_points(tag_name, user)
    17	        end
    18	      end
    19	    end
    20	
    21	    def recalculate_tag_follow_points(tag_name, user)
    22	      tag = Tag.find_by(name: tag_name)
    23	      follow = Follow.follower_tag(user.id).where(followable_id: tag.id).last
    24	
    25	      follow.implicit_points = calculate_implicit_points(tag, user)
    26	      follow.save
    27	    end
    28	
    29	    def calculate_implicit_points(tag, user)
    30	      last_100_reactable_ids = user.reactions.where(reactable_type: "Article", points: 0..)
    31	        .pluck(:reactable_id).last(100)
    32	      last_100_long_page_view_article_ids = user.page_views.where(time_tracked_in_seconds: 45..)
    33	        .pluck(:article_id).last(100)
    34	      articles = Article.where(id: last_100_reactable_ids + last_100_long_page_view_article_ids)
    35	      tags = articles.map(&:tag_list).flatten
    36	      occurrences = tags.count(tag.name)
    37	      bonus = inverse_popularity_bonus(tag)
    38	      finalized_points(occurrences, bonus)
    39	    end
    40	
    41	    def adjust_other_tag_follows_of_user(user_id)
    42	      # As we bump one follow up, we should also give a slight penalty
    43	      # to other follows to ensure re-balancing of overall points
    44	      # This will help stale tags fade after a temporary interest bump
    45	
    46	      # 0.98 is used to ensure this is "a very small amount"
    47	      # And percentage seems to makes sense, as it will be relative to
    48	      # size of current points.
    49	
    50	      # That number could be adjusted at any point if we have reason to
    51	      # believe it is too much or too little.
    52	      Follow.follower_tag(user_id).order(Arel.sql("RANDOM()")).limit(5).each do |follow|
    53	        follow.update_column(:points, (follow.points * 0.98))
    54	      end
    55	    end
    56	
    57	    def inverse_popularity_bonus(tag)
    58	      # Let's give a bonus to "less popular" tags on the platform
    59	      # To help balance the weight of popular topic.
    60	      # On DEV, javascript has way more taggings than rust, for example, so we can
    61	      # help rust outweigh JS in this calculation slightly.
    62	      # The bonus will be applied to the logarithmic scale, as to blunt any outsized impact.
    63	      top_100_tag_names = cached_app_wide_top_tag_names
    64	      top_100_tag_names.index(tag.name) || (top_100_tag_names.size * 1.5)
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    def finalized_points(occurrences, bonus)
    68	      Math.log(occurrences + bonus + 1) # + 1 in all cases is to avoid log(0) => -infinity
    69	    end
    70	
    71	    def cached_app_wide_top_tag_names
    72	      Rails.cache.fetch("top-100-tags") do
    73	        Tag.order(hotness_score: :desc).limit(100).pluck(:name)
    74	      end
    75	    end
    76	  end
    77	end

FILE app/workers/concepts/backfill_classifier_worker.rb
     1	module Concepts
     2	  class BackfillClassifierWorker
     3	    include Sidekiq::Job
     4	    sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority, lock: :until_executing, on_conflict: :replace
     5	
     6	    def perform(concept_id)
     7	      concept = Concept.find_by(id: concept_id)
     8	      return unless concept
     9	
    10	      vector_literal = "[#{concept.anchor_embedding.to_a.join(',')}]"
    11	      quoted_vector = Concept.connection.quote(vector_literal)
    12	
    13	      # 1. Clean up existing memberships for this concept
    14	      concept.concept_memberships.delete_all
    15	
    16	      threshold = concept.similarity_threshold || Concepts::Classifier::DEFAULT_THRESHOLD
    17	
    18	      # 2. Query all matching published articles
    19	      matching_articles = Article.published
    20	        .select("articles.id, (semantic_embedding <=> #{quoted_vector}) AS computed_distance")
    21	        .where.not(semantic_embedding: nil)
    22	        .where("semantic_embedding <=> #{quoted_vector} <= ?", threshold)
    23	        .to_a
    24	
    25	      memberships = matching_articles.map do |art|
    26	        {
    27	          concept_id: concept.id,
    28	          record_type: "Article",
    29	          record_id: art.id,
    30	          distance: art.computed_distance.to_f,
    31	          created_at: Time.current,
    32	          updated_at: Time.current
    33	        }
    34	      end
    35	
    36	      # 3. Query all matching comments
    37	      matching_comments = Comment
    38	        .select("comments.id, (semantic_embedding <=> #{quoted_vector}) AS computed_distance")
    39	        .where.not(semantic_embedding: nil)
    40	        .where("semantic_embedding <=> #{quoted_vector} <= ?", threshold)
    41	        .to_a
    42	
    43	      matching_comments.each do |com|
    44	        memberships << {
    45	          concept_id: concept.id,
    46	          record_type: "Comment",
    47	          record_id: com.id,
    48	          distance: com.computed_distance.to_f,
    49	          created_at: Time.current,
    50	          updated_at: Time.current
    51	        }
    52	      end
    53	
    54	      # 4. Bulk insert memberships
    55	      return unless memberships.any?
    56	
    57	      ConceptMembership.insert_all!(memberships)
    58	    end
    59	  end
    60	end

FILE app/workers/concepts/lookback_worker.rb
     1	module Concepts
     2	  class LookbackWorker
     3	    include Sidekiq::Job
     4	    sidekiq_options queue: :low_priority, lock: :until_executing, on_conflict: :replace
     5	
     6	    def perform(concept_id, days)
     7	      concept = Concept.find_by(id: concept_id)
     8	      return unless concept
     9	
    10	      days = days.to_i
    11	      return if days <= 0
    12	
    13	      prev_days = concept.max_lookback_days
    14	      return if days <= prev_days
    15	
    16	      start_time = days.days.ago
    17	
    18	      overlap_days = 2
    19	      if prev_days > 0 && prev_days > overlap_days
    20	        end_time = (prev_days - overlap_days).days.ago
    21	      else
    22	        end_time = nil
    23	      end
    24	
    25	      vector_literal = "[#{concept.anchor_embedding.to_a.join(',')}]"
    26	      quoted_vector = Concept.connection.quote(vector_literal)
    27	
    28	      threshold = concept.similarity_threshold || Concepts::Classifier::DEFAULT_THRESHOLD
    29	
    30	      # 1. Query matching published articles
    31	      articles_query = Article.published
    32	        .select("articles.id, (semantic_embedding <=> #{quoted_vector}) AS computed_distance")
    33	        .where.not(semantic_embedding: nil)
    34	        .where("semantic_embedding <=> #{quoted_vector} <= ?", threshold)
    35	        .where("published_at >= ?", start_time)
    36	
    37	      if end_time
    38	        articles_query = articles_query.where("published_at <= ?", end_time)
    39	      end
    40	
    41	      matching_articles = articles_query.to_a
    42	
    43	      memberships = matching_articles.map do |art|
    44	        {
    45	          concept_id: concept.id,
    46	          record_type: "Article",
    47	          record_id: art.id,
    48	          distance: art.computed_distance.to_f,
    49	          created_at: Time.current,
    50	          updated_at: Time.current
    51	        }
    52	      end
    53	
    54	      # 2. Query matching comments
    55	      comments_query = Comment
    56	        .select("comments.id, (semantic_embedding <=> #{quoted_vector}) AS computed_distance")
    57	        .where.not(semantic_embedding: nil)
    58	        .where("semantic_embedding <=> #{quoted_vector} <= ?", threshold)
    59	        .where("created_at >= ?", start_time)
    60	
    61	      if end_time
    62	        comments_query = comments_query.where("created_at <= ?", end_time)
    63	      end
    64	
    65	      matching_comments = comments_query.to_a
    66	
    67	      matching_comments.each do |com|
    68	        memberships << {
    69	          concept_id: concept.id,
    70	          record_type: "Comment",
    71	          record_id: com.id,
    72	          distance: com.computed_distance.to_f,
    73	          created_at: Time.current,
    74	          updated_at: Time.current
    75	        }
    76	      end
    77	
    78	      # 3. Bulk upsert memberships
    79	      if memberships.any?
    80	        ConceptMembership.upsert_all(
    81	          memberships,
    82	          unique_by: %i[concept_id record_type record_id],
    83	          update_only: %i[distance updated_at],
    84	          record_timestamps: false
    85	        )
    86	      end
    87	
    88	      # 4. Update max_lookback_days
    89	      concept.reload.update!(max_lookback_days: [concept.max_lookback_days, days].max)
    90	    end
    91	  end
    92	end
